title: The Shape Of Things Undone
summary: It's four in the morning. Christine — a carer, forty-something — is heating a sandwich in a microwave she has an understanding with. Upstairs, they're opening the new wing. Speeches. Free biscuits. Down here, she's doing the checklist. An audio performance about what gets lost when medicine looks ahead — and what stubbornly persists. On the eve of a genomics research launch at a care facility being quietly transformed: a mug with no…
_description: It's four in the morning. Christine — a carer, forty-something — is heating a sandwich in a microwave she has an understanding with. Upstairs, they're opening the new wing. Speeches. Free biscuits. Down here, she's doing the checklist. An audio performance about what gets lost when medicine looks ahead — and what stubbornly persists. On the eve of a genomics research launch at a care facility being quietly transformed: a mug with no name, a father who looked at Christine like the girl who delivers the prescriptions, and what it means to hold a story when science is looking at people who haven't arrived yet. Voices overlap, pause, return. Time loosens. Sit inside a story, not behind it. Tender, dark, unexpectedly funny. Written by Lita Doolan (multi award-winning playwright). ★★★★ WhatsOnStage Performed by Julie Broadbent (OffWest End nom). 'Whatever I forget or remember — I'll still know where this mug is.'

title: Home Away From Home Bridges In Between By Amrita Chandradas
summary: Amrita Chandradas is a Singaporean documentary photographer and her work explores the less discussed issues of displacement and struggles through on-the-ground personal insights. She graduated with an MA in Photojournalism and Documentary photography from London College of Communications and was recently bestowed as the UK Alumni winner for the "Culture and Creativity Award" by The British Council and The British High Commission. She…
_description: Amrita Chandradas is a Singaporean documentary photographer and her work explores the less discussed issues of displacement and struggles through on-the-ground personal insights. She graduated with an MA in Photojournalism and Documentary photography from London College of Communications and was recently bestowed as the UK Alumni winner for the "Culture and Creativity Award" by The British Council and The British High Commission. She was also a finalist for the Asian Women Photographers Showcase, Excellence in Digital News by SOPA News. Her work is featured at The National Geographic, The New York Times, The Washington Post, The LA Times, BBC World, British Journal of Photography, SONY, National Heritage Board, Singapore Tourism Board, Time Out Singapore, and Bloomberg Business amongst many others. THIS EVENT IS SPONSORED BY THE IRENE MENSAH BURSARY & FLO CREATIVE IN COLLABORATION WITH PLUS X INNOVATION

title: Art Pals At Bottoms Rest
summary: An art exhibition of prints and original art by artists that are connected through friendship, curiosity and common interests. Did you know that you most probably live within 100 metres of one of the artists in this exhibition? This show is an introduction to the creative power that bubbles around us. Interpret each art piece as a window into the work of each of the artists.

title: Zoe Thorne Gold Top
venue_name: Laughing Horse @ a&o Hostel bar (Front Room)
summary: Thought you wouldn't be able to see any grownup shows at the Brighton Fringe this year because you have a baby? Think again! It's hard to get out to see live comedy when you're a parent, and it can be expensive and nerve-wracking organising a babysitter. Gold Top is a daytime comedy gig where you can bring the baby along too, so there's no need to miss out! Comedian and mum of two Zoe Thorne will ensure plenty of laughs as she talks…
_description: Thought you wouldn't be able to see any grownup shows at the Brighton Fringe this year because you have a baby? Think again! It's hard to get out to see live comedy when you're a parent, and it can be expensive and nerve-wracking organising a babysitter. Gold Top is a daytime comedy gig where you can bring the baby along too, so there's no need to miss out! Comedian and mum of two Zoe Thorne will ensure plenty of laughs as she talks about the trials and tribulations of parenting and childbirth, and you can feel like a grownup out on the town again. But you're also free to feed, change and rock your baby without worrying about disturbing the rest of the audience, plus you'll be home by witching hour. And best of all, there will be no winding of any bobbins in sight. Please note: all babies should be under the age of 1 as there may be some unsuitable language - this isn't Rhyme Time. All proceeds will go to Royal Papworth Hospital Charity.

summary: Hot on the heels of Camden Fringe and off-West End success, 1816 returns to the stage. It’s Byron and Shelley, as you've never seen them before. A Friends meets Frankenstein comedy-drama musical, from the same company that launched SIX: 1816 is the ultimate origin story of the first modern vampire novel and Frankenstein, where a group of five Romantics get trapped for a summer they'd rather forget in their storm-swept villa by Lake…
_description: Hot on the heels of Camden Fringe and off-West End success, 1816 returns to the stage. It’s Byron and Shelley, as you've never seen them before. A Friends meets Frankenstein comedy-drama musical, from the same company that launched SIX: 1816 is the ultimate origin story of the first modern vampire novel and Frankenstein, where a group of five Romantics get trapped for a summer they'd rather forget in their storm-swept villa by Lake Geneva... Trapped indoors by torrential rain, legendary writers Byron, Percy and Mary Shelley and their friends, Polidori and Claire search for inspiration by the gloomy Lake Geneva. Driven by a desire for escapism, they challenge one another to create the best ghost story. But things fall apart the longer they are stuck together. Polidori’s diary is brought to life in the 1816: The Year Without A Summer musical.

title: Iterum Felix Happy Again
summary: Iterum Felix – Happy Again: Curiosities for the Future is an exhibition by artist Ryan Callanan, presented at Whistleblower Gallery. The show brings together works made from salvaged, reclaimed, and donated objects, reworked through painting and physical modification. Alongside materials sourced more broadly, the gallery has invited famous acquaintances and members of the wider community to contribute personal items, each carrying its…
_description: Iterum Felix – Happy Again: Curiosities for the Future is an exhibition by artist Ryan Callanan, presented at Whistleblower Gallery. The show brings together works made from salvaged, reclaimed, and donated objects, reworked through painting and physical modification. Alongside materials sourced more broadly, the gallery has invited famous acquaintances and members of the wider community to contribute personal items, each carrying its own history before being transformed. Drawing on Callanan’s ongoing use of the smiley motif and references to rave culture, these pieces give overlooked objects a new form and presence. Visitors are also invited to contribute an object for possible reinterpretation as part of the exhibition. Through reuse, alteration, and recontextualisation, Iterum Felix – Happy Again reflects on renewal, nostalgia, and how value and meaning can shift when familiar objects are seen differently.

title: Monologue
summary: The story is a façade. The energy is real. Fresh from the vibrant contemporary scene of Tehran, "MONOLOGUE!" makes its UK debut after a highly successful run at the iconic Molavi Hall. On the surface, it begins as a crime narrative told across six episodic fragments. But don’t be fooled. This is not traditional storytelling; it is a post-modern rebellion against structure. Starring Arshia Etemadi as "The Actor," the performance blurs…
_description: The story is a façade. The energy is real. Fresh from the vibrant contemporary scene of Tehran, "MONOLOGUE!" makes its UK debut after a highly successful run at the iconic Molavi Hall. On the surface, it begins as a crime narrative told across six episodic fragments. But don’t be fooled. This is not traditional storytelling; it is a post-modern rebellion against structure. Starring Arshia Etemadi as "The Actor," the performance blurs the terrifyingly thin line between a scripted character and a living human being on stage. Clidook Group invites you to witness a raw experiment where no two nights are ever the same. Performed in Persian with English Surtitles—until the improvisation breaks the rules, shifting into English and universal physical language.

title: The Love We Think We Deserve
summary: Around 1 in a 100 people live with Borderline Personality Disorder, yet it is one of the most misunderstood and stigmatized mental health conditions out there. Online searches reinforce harmful stereotypes and the majority of articles on mental health advise you how to “spot borderlines” – so you can avoid them. Rosie Cappuccino writes in her book, ‘talking about BPD’: “A couple of clicks reveal widespread derogatory attitudes towards…
_description: Around 1 in a 100 people live with Borderline Personality Disorder, yet it is one of the most misunderstood and stigmatized mental health conditions out there. Online searches reinforce harmful stereotypes and the majority of articles on mental health advise you how to “spot borderlines” – so you can avoid them. Rosie Cappuccino writes in her book, ‘talking about BPD’: “A couple of clicks reveal widespread derogatory attitudes towards the condition [...] YouTube videos [...] warn that people with BPD are more trouble than they are worth, impossible to connect with and incapable of love. [...] for many people the risks of being deemed untrustworthy, incapable or even dangerous can be too high. [...] Many people [...] feel safer disclosing anything but BPD.” With this play, I aim to give an insight into what living with BPD feels like and contribute to breaking down the stigma, bit by bit. “I am not the other. I am one of you. Speak about me as if I were in the room- because I am.”

title: The Winner Rolls It All
summary: The Winner Rolls It All is a fast-paced, high-stakes board game musical. _description: The Winner Rolls It All is a fast-paced, high-stakes board game musical. Featuring a score parodying one of Eurovision's most iconic winners, and a book crammed with pop-culture references, think The Rocky Horror Show meets Starlight Express...but for huns. A riff on Mamma Mia! and featuring a score made up of parodies of ABBA songs, with a story about a board game that everybody is familiar with. Featuring bonds, stocks, investments, betrayal, capitalism & Gemma Collins quotes, there's something for everyone in this hilarious parody musical.

title: The Illustrious Reign Of King Louis Xix In Its Entirety
summary: Paris, 1830. King Charles X unwillingly signs the final document of his reign—his abdication. This clears the path for his son and heir, Louis XIX, to ascend the throne and usher in a new era of prosperity, wisdom, and dignified leadership for the Kingdom of France. Twenty minutes later, it's over. House of The Onion presents the untold and (mostly) true story of history's shortest-reigning monarch. A young king inherits a nation in…
_description: Paris, 1830. King Charles X unwillingly signs the final document of his reign—his abdication. This clears the path for his son and heir, Louis XIX, to ascend the throne and usher in a new era of prosperity, wisdom, and dignified leadership for the Kingdom of France. Twenty minutes later, it's over. House of The Onion presents the untold and (mostly) true story of history's shortest-reigning monarch. A young king inherits a nation in crisis. Does he have what it takes? Absolutely not. Does anyone tell him this? Also no. What follows is a glorious disaster. Advisors nod along. Courtiers applaud. Someone suggests a war. Someone else suggests two wars. Things escalate with remarkable incompetence - the kind reserved for real monarchs. It is a tale of spectacular confidence and enthusiastic inadequacy. Sometimes history is written by the victors. Sometimes it’s written frantically, while looking for an exit unoccupied by citizens with pitchforks.

title: Slay
summary: Following a PTSD diagnosis, small-town, small-screen-obsessed Kit escapes the North East for London-until a text from her ex detonates her recovery, sending her spiralling through therapy, chaos and increasingly unhelpful coping fantasies. In a generation that processes trauma via TikTok, Kit is determined to stay the main character (because if she doesn’t laugh, she’ll cry and probably post it). As symptoms, triggers and disordered…
_description: Following a PTSD diagnosis, small-town, small-screen-obsessed Kit escapes the North East for London-until a text from her ex detonates her recovery, sending her spiralling through therapy, chaos and increasingly unhelpful coping fantasies. In a generation that processes trauma via TikTok, Kit is determined to stay the main character (because if she doesn’t laugh, she’ll cry and probably post it). As symptoms, triggers and disordered eating collide, she faces a choice: confront her past or continue doing what she does best - be delusional and avoid it. From Offie nominated actor-writer, Lois-Amber Toole's SLAY is a darkly funny, unapologetically North East reckoning with class, internet culture and the pressure on young women’s bodies.

title: Piran Armes Is A Bit Weird Work In Progress
summary: Piran is the 2025 Chichester Fringe New Act of the Year Winner and 2025 Komedia New Act Semi Finalist. A born and bred Brightonian, he's never quite seemed to fit in. He has an odd name, a job nobody understands and now he's reflecting on how his past has led him to the quirky character he seems to have landed himself as. He shares silly thoughts, unique impressions, bizarre pastimes and origins of his unusual name. Piran delivers…
_description: Piran is the 2025 Chichester Fringe New Act of the Year Winner and 2025 Komedia New Act Semi Finalist. A born and bred Brightonian, he's never quite seemed to fit in. He has an odd name, a job nobody understands and now he's reflecting on how his past has led him to the quirky character he seems to have landed himself as. He shares silly thoughts, unique impressions, bizarre pastimes and origins of his unusual name. Piran delivers laughs with charm, wit and just the right amount of madness. He's a bit weird.

title: Rpj Role Playing James
summary: Step into a world of wild imagination, chaos and comedy. Under the guidance of ex-comedian and self-proclaimed “James-Master” James Bennison, audience members are plucked at random and thrust into the spotlight as the heroes of a totally improvised fantasy adventure. Armed only with enthusiasm (and maybe a grenade), they must battle bizarre creatures, solve ridiculous riddles, and roll gigantic dice, all while James desperately tries…
_description: Step into a world of wild imagination, chaos and comedy. Under the guidance of ex-comedian and self-proclaimed “James-Master” James Bennison, audience members are plucked at random and thrust into the spotlight as the heroes of a totally improvised fantasy adventure. Armed only with enthusiasm (and maybe a grenade), they must battle bizarre creatures, solve ridiculous riddles, and roll gigantic dice, all while James desperately tries to keep the story on the rails. Whether you’re a seasoned role-player or have never touched a character sheet in your life, this show invites you to join a roaring quest where everything is made up and no two nights are ever the same. Expect epic triumphs, catastrophic failures, and the kind of heroic nonsense that can only come from comedy colliding with high fantasy. If you love laughter, adventure and glorious unpredictability join the party. Your destiny awaits, whether you want it or not.

title: Spirited Away Ghibli Taste Tales
summary: Join us for a magical evening filled with delicious Ghibli-inspired treats and enchanting tales from Spirited Away! Welcome to Ghibli Taste & Tales presents Spirited Away! Join us for an unforgettable evening of mystery, magic, and mouth-watering dishes inspired by Studio Ghibli’s iconic masterpiece. Hosted at Blossoms Cocktail Bar and Restaurant, this in-person event invites you to step into the spirit world and follow Chihiro’s…
_description: Join us for a magical evening filled with delicious Ghibli-inspired treats and enchanting tales from Spirited Away! Welcome to Ghibli Taste & Tales presents Spirited Away! Join us for an unforgettable evening of mystery, magic, and mouth-watering dishes inspired by Studio Ghibli’s iconic masterpiece. Hosted at Blossoms Cocktail Bar and Restaurant, this in-person event invites you to step into the spirit world and follow Chihiro’s journey through a land of strange creatures, ancient bathhouses, and unforgettable encounters. Lose yourself in the atmospheric setting, savour themed cocktails and gourmet bites, and relive the wonder of this beloved film with fellow fans. Don’t miss your chance to experience the magic of Spirited Away brought to life through food, drink, and storytelling. Enjoy three enchanting courses, paired with two cocktails to guide you through this otherworldly adventure. (Non-alcoholic options available). Vegan and vegetarian options available on request.

title: The Coffee Machine Is Broken
summary: The Vitruvian Theatre Company’s Brighton Fringe debut! A hospital waiting room - everyone’s favourite place to spend an evening. A woman faces an impossible choice between life and death. A father and son reach for each other but miss every time. A daughter split open by grief, longing for it to end. A young nurse stretched past breaking point. A man, drunk, loud, and impossible to ignore. All different. All waiting. Over the course of…
_description: The Vitruvian Theatre Company’s Brighton Fringe debut! A hospital waiting room - everyone’s favourite place to spend an evening. A woman faces an impossible choice between life and death. A father and son reach for each other but miss every time. A daughter split open by grief, longing for it to end. A young nurse stretched past breaking point. A man, drunk, loud, and impossible to ignore. All different. All waiting. Over the course of a single night in, ‘The Coffee Machine is Broken’ explores the fragile connections people cling to when everything feels uncertain. Lives hang in the balance, strangers collide, and love is tested in all its messiest forms. Conversations overlap, inner thoughts spill out, and moments of connection appear where you least expect them. Tender and darkly funny, this play captures the raw, human moments that exist between life and death, when all you can do is wait.

title: Carousels Pop Up Poets
summary: Joyful, funny and touching, you've never heard the spoken word quite like this! Returning to The Fringe after ‘popping up’ to unsuspecting audiences in 2024, Carousel’s learning disabled comedians and poets have once again come to laugh in the face of societal preconceptions, and in turn make us all laugh at life in general. Performing are poets Charlie Hatton, Susannah Hall-Hirst and Electric Mutant X plus special guests. Expect…
_description: Joyful, funny and touching, you've never heard the spoken word quite like this! Returning to The Fringe after ‘popping up’ to unsuspecting audiences in 2024, Carousel’s learning disabled comedians and poets have once again come to laugh in the face of societal preconceptions, and in turn make us all laugh at life in general. Performing are poets Charlie Hatton, Susannah Hall-Hirst and Electric Mutant X plus special guests. Expect surreal whimsey, psychedelic sci-fi word wizardry and poems of love, life and loss. Ending the night on a literal high-note is local Pop-Rock legends Beat Express. "I was awe-inspired by the levels of creativity, imagination, talent and charisma exhibited by all of your poets." (Audience member) The Pop-Up Poets are supported by local learning disability-led arts charity Carousel, a multi award-winning organisation championing learning disabled people's inclusion in the arts.

title: The Hunger Artist By Franz Kafka
summary: Have you ever given so much of YOURSELF to something you LOVE, there’s nothing left of YOU? How far would you go to find MEANING, to fulfil your PASSION? What is Art? And what does it mean to be an Artist? Franz Kafka’s ‘Hunger Artist’ nourishes his soul the only way he knows how, by starving himself, in his small, barred cage, before crowds of spectators, some admiring, some sneering and contemptuous. Seeking acclaim for his craft and…
_description: Have you ever given so much of YOURSELF to something you LOVE, there’s nothing left of YOU? How far would you go to find MEANING, to fulfil your PASSION? What is Art? And what does it mean to be an Artist? Franz Kafka’s ‘Hunger Artist’ nourishes his soul the only way he knows how, by starving himself, in his small, barred cage, before crowds of spectators, some admiring, some sneering and contemptuous. Seeking acclaim for his craft and acceptance, validation, spiritual meaning and perhaps love from his audience, this is his ‘art’ and, though often torturous for him, he will stop at nothing in his pursuit of perfection. In this, Franz Kafka’s final work, he died on 3rd June 1924, we celebrate his life and work with this Solo retelling of this dramatic short story. Performed by Jonathan Sidgwick (Eastenders, Coronation Street, Hollyoaks, The Mousetrap West End Tour. Jon has worked several times for Steven Berkoff (Messiah at The Old Vic; Oedipus - Edinburgh Festival, Spoleto Festival).

title: The Queen Shakespeare And Me
summary: Affectionate musical comedy tribute to a thoroughly modern, fully empowered female role model – a woman who headed up a country & united a commonwealth for over 70 years, earning respect, admiration & affection from around the world. This powerful, colourful lady head of state reflects on her long life and reveals her "all" to her people – her undying love for her corgis, the men in her life, her singlehanded success at forging peace…
_description: Affectionate musical comedy tribute to a thoroughly modern, fully empowered female role model – a woman who headed up a country & united a commonwealth for over 70 years, earning respect, admiration & affection from around the world. This powerful, colourful lady head of state reflects on her long life and reveals her "all" to her people – her undying love for her corgis, the men in her life, her singlehanded success at forging peace processes, her unexpected, delightful designs for leading the country, with a surprise "coming out" at 96. "Age cannot wither her, nor custom stale her infinite variety" You are regally commanded to join Her Majesty for tea, free cake & biscuits, and a glimpse into unseen aspects of her life. With audience participation, SingalongaLiz, Shakespeare & quiz. We may mourn her passing and the end of an era, but HM would not have wished us to be miserable. Don't miss the UK’s answer to Mrs Maisel! Handbag – the regal older lady’s Fleabag!

title: Borderline
summary: Struggling musician Izzy and serial dater Marina both get diagnosed with one of the most stigmatised and misunderstood mental health disorders. Izzy strives to find peace in her relationship with her girlfriend, while Marina tries to find meaning beyond partying and one-night-stands. Will these two very different women find a way to navigate life in this neurotypical world? Our recommended ticket price is £15.00, although we have a…
_description: Struggling musician Izzy and serial dater Marina both get diagnosed with one of the most stigmatised and misunderstood mental health disorders. Izzy strives to find peace in her relationship with her girlfriend, while Marina tries to find meaning beyond partying and one-night-stands. Will these two very different women find a way to navigate life in this neurotypical world? Our recommended ticket price is £15.00, although we have a sliding scale from £10. If you would like to see the show but the cost is prohibitive, please contact us to arrange a ticket. We also welcome any payment above the recommended price, which will go towards supporting production costs (we are just two theatremakers doing it all ourselves!)

title: Darling
summary: Society is abuzz: the ball of the season is about to begin. Step into a Regency world of romance, rivalry and delightful mischief. A colourful cast of characters gathers beneath the watchful eye of the Queen. Some seek love, others favour and fortune, but beneath the polished etiquette, order begins to unravel. As tensions rise, dazzling acrobatics, aerial feats and playful physical comedy reveal the ambitions and secrets simmering…
_description: Society is abuzz: the ball of the season is about to begin. Step into a Regency world of romance, rivalry and delightful mischief. A colourful cast of characters gathers beneath the watchful eye of the Queen. Some seek love, others favour and fortune, but beneath the polished etiquette, order begins to unravel. As tensions rise, dazzling acrobatics, aerial feats and playful physical comedy reveal the ambitions and secrets simmering beneath high society. Meet royals, ladies-in-waiting and a few unexpected guests. Laugh, gasp, and cheer as spectacular circus unfolds all around you. Secure your tickets posthaste! Darling, you simply must attend.

title: Miss Havishams Clothes Swap
summary: Miss Havisham requests your presence at an evening devoted to the exchange of garments and the offering of sartorial wisdom. Confined to her gently rotting bridal attire for a mere 200 years, she now contemplates (oh the horror) getting dressed. Having heard speak of a modern affliction named “fast fashion,” she seeks enlightenment. You are therefore required to bring one item of clothing that you are willing to exchange at that…
_description: Miss Havisham requests your presence at an evening devoted to the exchange of garments and the offering of sartorial wisdom. Confined to her gently rotting bridal attire for a mere 200 years, she now contemplates (oh the horror) getting dressed. Having heard speak of a modern affliction named “fast fashion,” she seeks enlightenment. You are therefore required to bring one item of clothing that you are willing to exchange at that curious altar of cast off selves we call 'fashion'. Inspired by Dickens iconic character, this is an interactive show about getting dressed. The joy of self-expression is set against the impact of global supply chains. And yes, there will be an actual clothes swap. Created and performed by Edinburgh Fringe First Award winner Helen Tennison, supported by Stanley Arts, London Metropolitan University and Actors of Dionysus.

title: The Lady Boys Of Bangkok
summary: Fresh from winning awards at both Perth and Adelaide Fringe. Your favourite show-stopping Brighton Fringe divas, The Ladyboys of Bangkok, are back their spectacular Full Moon Tour. Get ready for an electrifying night packed with dazzling costumes, jaw-dropping choreography, and powerhouse performances that will have you laughing, cheering, and dancing in your seat. Expect chart-topping anthems, side-splitting comedy, and the…
_description: Fresh from winning awards at both Perth and Adelaide Fringe. Your favourite show-stopping Brighton Fringe divas, The Ladyboys of Bangkok, are back their spectacular Full Moon Tour. Get ready for an electrifying night packed with dazzling costumes, jaw-dropping choreography, and powerhouse performances that will have you laughing, cheering, and dancing in your seat. Expect chart-topping anthems, side-splitting comedy, and the unmistakable sparkle of Thailand’s most glamorous Ladyboys bringing the party to life. Whether you’re a life-long fan or joining the celebration for the first time, this is your chance to experience a show bursting with unfiltered joy, fabulousness, and unforgettable entertainment. The Boys are back in town... and the party starts here! Comedy! Cabaret! PARTY!

title: Cowpokes In A Bunkhouse
summary: One story. Three endings. In a cluttered Wild West bunkhouse, three cowpokes try to keep a restless herd calm through the night. When singing fails and tempers fray, they turn to storytelling. Each cowpoke has their own version of the same tale about two brothers, a stolen elixir and a choice that changes everything. But as the stories grow wilder, darker and more revealing, it becomes clear these aren’t just bedtime tales for cattle.…
_description: One story. Three endings. In a cluttered Wild West bunkhouse, three cowpokes try to keep a restless herd calm through the night. When singing fails and tempers fray, they turn to storytelling. Each cowpoke has their own version of the same tale about two brothers, a stolen elixir and a choice that changes everything. But as the stories grow wilder, darker and more revealing, it becomes clear these aren’t just bedtime tales for cattle. Rivalries surface, old wounds reopen and the line between fiction and truth begins to blur. As dawn approaches, the trio must face whether logic, aggression or kindness wins in the end. Blending physical comedy, audience interaction and sudden emotional turns, this offbeat western explores masculinity, memory and the stories we tell to live with ourselves.

title: Losing The Plotkin
summary: Two Plotkins. Two works in progress. Two Brighton Fringe favourites. One Hour. All Brand new material. Fanny Dent (Leicester Comedy Festival nominee 2024) and Simon Plotkin (Do The Thing) team up for a split bill of fresh, work-in-progress comedy. Expect bold new characters, ideas, jokes and beautifully controlled chaos. Fanny Dent is known for her comedy and character cabaret: Playful, theatrical and gloriously unpredictable, her…
_description: Two Plotkins. Two works in progress. Two Brighton Fringe favourites. One Hour. All Brand new material. Fanny Dent (Leicester Comedy Festival nominee 2024) and Simon Plotkin (Do The Thing) team up for a split bill of fresh, work-in-progress comedy. Expect bold new characters, ideas, jokes and beautifully controlled chaos. Fanny Dent is known for her comedy and character cabaret: Playful, theatrical and gloriously unpredictable, her previous show, 'The Burlesque Imposter' (★★★★) sold out fringe runs across the country. Her new material continues in that spirit. Simon Plotkin is a familiar face on the Brighton comedy scene. He is one third of the improvised musical hit 'Do The Thing', which enjoyed a sell-out run at Brighton Fringe last year. Simon will be testing new ideas and test-driving new material. What will they come up with? It'll be loose, lively and full surprises!

title: A Letter To My Parents
summary: Viral sensation Henning Nilsen recalls the emotional journey from leaving his Finance job to making his challenging Edinburgh Fringe debut. What makes an accountant from Norway quit his stable job and move to the UK to pursue a career in stand-up comedy? In this brutally candid show, Henning attempts to answer these questions. Henning is an award-winning stand-up comedian making waves in the UK. He's a viral sensation with over 100…
_description: Viral sensation Henning Nilsen recalls the emotional journey from leaving his Finance job to making his challenging Edinburgh Fringe debut. What makes an accountant from Norway quit his stable job and move to the UK to pursue a career in stand-up comedy? In this brutally candid show, Henning attempts to answer these questions. Henning is an award-winning stand-up comedian making waves in the UK. He's a viral sensation with over 100 million online views and has in a short time gained a large following through his dry humour videos. He regularly performs at the top comedy clubs in the UK and has toured internationally in the US, Europe and Asia. His first solo show '1 And Done' had sold out shows at the Edinburgh and Brighton Fringe in 2025. Don’t miss this one and only chance to catch a rising star on the UK comedy scene at Brighton’s biggest arts festival! Free Entry – Reserve your tickets now to guarantee your seat in advance.

title: Tom Hutchinson Maintaining The Dream Work In Progress
venue_name: Laughing Horse @ a&o Hostel bar (Front Room)
summary: Is Tom Hutchinson a success? In his 20s he achieved the only dream he ever had: to make music full time, and create an album that's an unmitigated success. So...now what? Why does it feel like nobody prepared him for life after the eternal chase? Join red-haired rambler and ADHD-addled orator Tom Hutchinson for a hilarious work in progress stand-up show about the big questions; if all we ever learn is how to chase our dreams, how the…
_description: Is Tom Hutchinson a success? In his 20s he achieved the only dream he ever had: to make music full time, and create an album that's an unmitigated success. So...now what? Why does it feel like nobody prepared him for life after the eternal chase? Join red-haired rambler and ADHD-addled orator Tom Hutchinson for a hilarious work in progress stand-up show about the big questions; if all we ever learn is how to chase our dreams, how the hell do we live on afterwards? A Work In Progress hour about joy, obsession, failure, autism, granddads, Fatman Scoop and somehow The Sims. Red-haired rising star of the South West, Tom Hutchinson is “a joy to watch”, brimming with high energy absurdism and sparky tangents, bringing everything from wild improvisations and witty asides to introspective anecdotes and relatable ramblings. Bristol Comedy Competition Runner Up 2026. Bath Comedy Festival New Act 2022 Finalist. As Heard on BBC Upload.

summary: Newly-widowed Mrs Lovett needs to find a way to survive. Using skills learned from a whaler father, a courtesan mother and a butcher husband, Eleanor “recalibrates” her life - and her relationship with God. An origin story set before her infamous partnership with Sweeney Todd, we discover what would later lead her to turn pie-maker. You may think you already know Mrs. Lovett, but this new play explores what made her: the brutal choices…
_description: Newly-widowed Mrs Lovett needs to find a way to survive. Using skills learned from a whaler father, a courtesan mother and a butcher husband, Eleanor “recalibrates” her life - and her relationship with God. An origin story set before her infamous partnership with Sweeney Todd, we discover what would later lead her to turn pie-maker. You may think you already know Mrs. Lovett, but this new play explores what made her: the brutal choices and disappointments of a life in poverty. When the difference is life or death, morality becomes a moveable feast - and Eleanor is getting hungry... BoonDog Theatre is a multi award-winning collaborative theatre company based in the Midlands, creating exciting new character driven stories that start conversations that last long beyond the audience leaving the theatre. Their work is darkly comic, aims for the heart, and what they describe as “covertly political”. BoonDog Theatre are proud to be Associate Artists of the Royal and Derngate, Northampton.

_description: A new camp comedy pitting England’s most famous consulting detective against France’s most infamous gentleman burglar. Arsène Lupin voyages across the Channel to challenge Sherlock Holmes, with Dr John Watson caught in the middle of a tug-of-war between these literary legends. The Department of Ulterior Motives (DUM) specialises in high-concept, low-budget productions that blend physical performance and accessible storytelling with a mischievously political edge. Their debut production, an adaptation of The Man Who Was Thursday, was selected for SPRINT Festival 2026 at Camden People’s Theatre.

summary: GET BETTER is an hour long, four-hander scripted theatre piece centring themes of disability, gender, and truth. The play follows Hazel, a woman in her 20’s working in a café, grappling with chronic fatigue and trying to maintain her dreams of opening a café of her own with her best friend. The world experiences a shift, and female identifying people inexplicably loose the ability to lie. In the wake of this change, Hazel decides to…
_description: GET BETTER is an hour long, four-hander scripted theatre piece centring themes of disability, gender, and truth. The play follows Hazel, a woman in her 20’s working in a café, grappling with chronic fatigue and trying to maintain her dreams of opening a café of her own with her best friend. The world experiences a shift, and female identifying people inexplicably loose the ability to lie. In the wake of this change, Hazel decides to channel this new-found ability into pursing a diagnosis for the chronic illness she is experiencing but doesn’t yet understand. If she’s telling the truth, she has to be believed — right? If you’re curious to learn more about the play, head over to our Instagram, @getbetterplay.

title: Wench
summary: Martha Tallow is always thinking sideways, smiling inside-out and waking up in other people’s business. She talks too much, loves too freely and refuses to behave. Now the village wants blood. WENCH! is a darkly comic musical about hysteria, harlots and the stories people tell when they feel threatened. Accused of apostasy, impropriety and being generally inconvenient, Martha finds herself on trial before a community desperate to tidy…
_description: Martha Tallow is always thinking sideways, smiling inside-out and waking up in other people’s business. She talks too much, loves too freely and refuses to behave. Now the village wants blood. WENCH! is a darkly comic musical about hysteria, harlots and the stories people tell when they feel threatened. Accused of apostasy, impropriety and being generally inconvenient, Martha finds herself on trial before a community desperate to tidy her away. Blending filthy humour with lyrical ballads, WENCH drags the witch trial into the present day without ever pinning it to a single moment. Who gets labelled unnatural? Who is shamed for desire.? Who is punished for thinking differently? Part musical farce, part courtroom drama, part personal manifesto, WENCH skins the turnip of society to reveal the maggots beneath. Felix Le Freak is the winner of Drag Idol UK, the author of Serving Face and the creator of shows including Fagwartz, Men and Other Problems, and Felix & Friends.

_description: AK Golding’s debut solo show, INTO THE PIT, is an anarchic coal-mining cabaret that drags you headfirst into the grit, graft and glory of coal’s past. Part family history, part identity crisis, AK digs into what it means to come from a working class mining family while being a queer, neurodiverse theatre-making millennial. Expect high-octane skits, surreal character monologues, clowning, Billy Elliot spoofs, shadow puppets and original show tunes in a chaotic whistle-stop tour of the mining industry in the UK. Come into the pit and see if you can help bring a little light to the dark.

title: Dentata
summary: DENTATA is a joyful, riotous, and feral exploration of feminine ferocity and pleasure. Rooted in the tension of a god-fearing upbringing in the American South, the piece weaponizes the grotesque mockery of the Bouffon to interrogate how women have been misunderstood, belittled, and underestimated throughout history. The performance is a high-stakes ritual of presence, conjured through the poetry of the Three Weird Sisters, the…
_description: DENTATA is a joyful, riotous, and feral exploration of feminine ferocity and pleasure. Rooted in the tension of a god-fearing upbringing in the American South, the piece weaponizes the grotesque mockery of the Bouffon to interrogate how women have been misunderstood, belittled, and underestimated throughout history. The performance is a high-stakes ritual of presence, conjured through the poetry of the Three Weird Sisters, the reclamation of a misunderstood teenage Medusa hoping for a date, and direct, playful confrontation with the audience. Centered around a giant, operatic vagina puppet, the work is as theatrically beautiful as it is provocative—prioritizing stunning visual imagery and extreme physicality to transport the viewer for sixty minutes. Directed by Jess Lazar (Funny or Die, Hulu) and co-birthed by SITI Company's Lucy Smith. Written and starring award-winning Julia VanderVeen, The Broad Street Review called it, “A rich expression of female ferocity: joyful and brave."

title: Little Lord Fondleroy

_description: Little Lord Fondleroy is a saccharine nightmare in velvet and lace, whose cherubic curls and perfect manners provide foolproof cover for his constant yearnings - primarily for poetry, pleasure, head boys and complete theatrical chaos.In this riotous hour, whirl through a formative annus eroticus at Flippitts Darstardly Boarding School for Badly Behaved Boys, where forbidden desires flourish amidst a sadistic culture of cruelty cultivated by prefects and masters alike. Here, Fondleroy discovers that masculinity and Empire are stitched together by caning, cunning and carefully repressed desire. Can he retain his innocent charm and boyish over-generosity while the forces around him attempt to stamp all that soppy, frilly, froppy feeling out of him? Enter a world of boarding-school heartbreak, homoerotic hijinks, and imperial intrigue.Fusing drag, theatre and music, this hilariously subversive show rips open hidden histories to reveal the queer desires that have always been lurking beneath

When your own brain feels like the enemy, is falling in love the bravest thing you can do - or the most reckless? Studies show that 1 in 4 adults…
_description: Steph’s battling chronic depression. Jamie’s crashed after pushing himself way too far, way too fast. They meet in a psychiatrist’s waiting room - hardly the stuff that rom-coms are made of - but the spark is instant and undeniable. What follows is messy, electric and unexpectedly beautiful. When your own brain feels like the enemy, is falling in love the bravest thing you can do - or the most reckless? Studies show that 1 in 4 adults in England will experience a mental health problem each year. Darkly funny and deeply moving, Jacob Marx Rice's Chemistry is a frank exploration of how we manage love - and life - when we rely on chemicals to keep us balanced.

summary: Oscar's life was perfectly fine until a drunken encounter with a psychic turned everything upside down. Now he's stuck with mind-reading powers he didn't ask for and definitely can't control. Tonight, you'll witness something very unpredictable: Oscar learning to harness his abilities in real-time. Oscar will attempt to read your thoughts (with varying degrees of success), predict futures that haven't happened yet, and subtly influence…
_description: Oscar's life was perfectly fine until a drunken encounter with a psychic turned everything upside down. Now he's stuck with mind-reading powers he didn't ask for and definitely can't control. Tonight, you'll witness something very unpredictable: Oscar learning to harness his abilities in real-time. Oscar will attempt to read your thoughts (with varying degrees of success), predict futures that haven't happened yet, and subtly influence decisions you haven't even made. Some moments will astonish you. Others might spectacularly misfire — but that's half the fun. This isn't your typical mind-reading show. It's funnier, messier, and far more honest. Oscar combines comedy with mentalism, weaving everything into a narrative that pulls you from sceptical observer to active participant. You're not just watching someone perform tricks—you're joining him on a journey of coming to terms with his newfound abilities. Will he succeed? Who knows. Will there be chaos along the way? Absolutely.

Director and performer Sammi combines evocative voice, powerful stage presence, and symbolic elements—like water…
_description: A recording of; When I Wake Up Again is an immersive theatre piece exploring a family's journey through love, loss, and resilience. Through intimate storytelling, movement, and live music, the performance takes audiences inside the lives of a couple and their children, confronting the joys and heartbreaks that shape a life. Director and performer Sammi combines evocative voice, powerful stage presence, and symbolic elements—like water and puppetry—to create a poignant, multi-sensory experience. ★★★★½ Audiences are invited to move within the space, following the performers and engaging closely with the narrative. This is a deeply human story, where grief, love, and memory intertwine, leaving us to reflect on our own choices and connections. Please note: this event can be booked from Friday 1st May 2026 and is a pre-recorded show

title: Enduring Violence Bhopals Ongoing Water Disaster

Now known as the world’s worst industrial disaster, it has claimed the lives of more than 25,000 people to date. But few people know of the ongoing ‘second disaster’ in Bhopal: four decades on, toxic waste from the abandoned factory continues to sink into the earth, poising the water of tens of thousands more people. This…
_description: On the night of 2nd December 1984, deadly gas leaked from a Union Carbide pesticide factory in Bhopal, India. Now known as the world’s worst industrial disaster, it has claimed the lives of more than 25,000 people to date. But few people know of the ongoing ‘second disaster’ in Bhopal: four decades on, toxic waste from the abandoned factory continues to sink into the earth, poising the water of tens of thousands more people. This exhibition draws attention to the ongoing water crisis in Bhopal and the activism of water-affected communities. Produced by the University of Leeds’ LivingBodiesObjects project in partnership with the Bhopal Medical Appeal, it is the culmination of a collaborative research project in which we explored new ways of telling the story of groundwater contamination in Bhopal. Our interviews with members of water-affected communities, original artworks, striking photography, and experiments with digital storytelling form the heart of the exhibition.

title: The Families Behind Our Healthcare Heroes
summary: Healthcare workers face high stress and burnout, yet little attention is given to the families who support them. This project explores the experiences of family members through creative, participatory workshops using comic‑making and poetry writing. The sessions introduce these methods & offers a space to reflect on the emotional and practical demands placed on families, including those from marginalised or under‑represented…
_description: Healthcare workers face high stress and burnout, yet little attention is given to the families who support them. This project explores the experiences of family members through creative, participatory workshops using comic‑making and poetry writing. The sessions introduce these methods & offers a space to reflect on the emotional and practical demands placed on families, including those from marginalised or under‑represented backgrounds. This is a facilitated participatory workshop for family members of healthcare workers, who live with them and support them, where the participants will be drawing on their experiences to make poetry and/ or comics. All materials are provided. You may choose whether or not to consent to your artwork being used in future research publications (ethical approval pending at the time of writing, information sheet and consent sheet will be provided on the day); you are welcome to join the workshop even if you prefer not to share your creations for research.

It was that look in his eyes when he saw the blood from his lip. His pupils were so tiny, he looked like an animal" Witch House Dreamers are a fictional British-American band who managed to find success in the late 1990s and early 2000s. With their blend of hard rock and dark psychedelia, nihilistic horror-…
_description: "This wasn’t the guy I’d written music with, performed on stage with... cried with at our friend’s funeral. He was something else. It was that look in his eyes when he saw the blood from his lip. His pupils were so tiny, he looked like an animal" Witch House Dreamers are a fictional British-American band who managed to find success in the late 1990s and early 2000s. With their blend of hard rock and dark psychedelia, nihilistic horror-inspired lyrics and being led by frontman Gary Machen, they seemed unstoppable. Even after two tragedies struck the band and nearly caused a break-up, they still managed to come together to save the band and themselves. However, when the sole female member, guitarist Harley Carter, comes forward to the police, she has a disturbing and potentially explosive story to tell about the band’s lead singer. With this single action, the band’s very existence is suddenly under threat and Harley’s reputation, reliability and even her safety are called into question

_description: Rising stars Steph Cassin and Oro Rose join forces for a split bill of work in progress comedy. Expect cerebral mischief. In an all-new work in progress show - 'Banshee', Steph Cassin explores her fascination with horror films and her complicated relationship with all things supernatural. A Komedia New Comedy Award Finalist 2024 and West End New Women Act of the Year Finalist 2025, Steph Cassin blends her dry wit with her agricultural background with hilarious results. “A very effective piece of world-building” – Chortle ------------ Oro Rose presents a work-in-progress for their debut show: 'Rogue'. Oro asks: Why is your name your name? Are we all gay now? And if all our heroes are wrong 'uns, what does that make us? 'So You Think You're Funny?' Finalist - 2025 'Leicester Comedian of the Year' Nominee - 2026 "Oro is a non-binary stand-up act that gently had us roaring with laughter...This is an act to look out for." Latest Brighton, July 2025

title: Addiction Its A Family Matter Who Cares
summary: Bringing to life the dilemmas and uncertainty experienced by family members in the face of their loved one’s struggle with addiction, through dramatic monologue, autobiography and poetry. This presentation shows the ways addiction reaches into and disrupts the lives of all those related to the addicted person, who may be their child, parent, sibling, partner. Sources range from Amy Winehouse’s father Mitch and footballer Peter…
_description: Bringing to life the dilemmas and uncertainty experienced by family members in the face of their loved one’s struggle with addiction, through dramatic monologue, autobiography and poetry. This presentation shows the ways addiction reaches into and disrupts the lives of all those related to the addicted person, who may be their child, parent, sibling, partner. Sources range from Amy Winehouse’s father Mitch and footballer Peter Shilton’s wife Steph. As well as current auto-fiction writers Julie Myerson and Douglas Stuart, 20th century autobiographical writer Caitlin Thomas, wife of poet Dylan Thomas, & earlier classical authors such as Anne Brontë. Post show Q&A, with time for discussion of the issues raised and to consider how the creative arts can be used to publicise the plight of the family affected by alcohol, drug, gambling and other addictions. Addiction–It’s a Family Matter. Who Cares? is a collaboration between psychologist Jim Orford & poet/writer Christine Hollywood.

title: Cant Kill The Spirit
summary: Can't Kill the Spirit follows Chloe, a former Greenham Common activist who reclaims her voice of protest, forty years after her experiences at the Peace Camp, where during the 1980s thousands of women lived and campaigned against the installation of cruise missiles. When a climate demonstration leads to life-changing consequences for her and her family today, Chloe must decide where her priorities lie. Inspired by women of protest then…
_description: Can't Kill the Spirit follows Chloe, a former Greenham Common activist who reclaims her voice of protest, forty years after her experiences at the Peace Camp, where during the 1980s thousands of women lived and campaigned against the installation of cruise missiles. When a climate demonstration leads to life-changing consequences for her and her family today, Chloe must decide where her priorities lie. Inspired by women of protest then and now, Can’t Kill the Spirit is a moving story of reconciling personal and global choices and a timely reminder of the need for upstanders in a world of bystanders. This "very impressive, engaging and emotive production" (theatreandtonic.co.uk), garnering **** reviews when it premiered in London in July 2025, links a vital part of British radical history with today’s climate concerns and protests.

summary: “Krowdwerk” is exactly what you might think – an unscripted crowdwork show. It will not be an hour of “what’s your name and what do you do?” but will instead be a conversational show with topics suggested by the audience etc. If you’re coming along, be prepared to get involved! Graeme was a semi-finalist in the…

title: Worm
summary: Sophie always thought she was just a girl, living her best life. But recently has come to realise that she might be more of a worry worm than a glass half full kinda gal. In this comedy show, see what’s been on her mind lately and maybe she can ease your worries too - relationship woes? Housemate eating all your cheese again? Just come along to this work in progress show, pop your problem in the worry worm bucket and have a few laughs.

Joined by a line up of eclectic performers, she presents a dazzling mix of cabaret delights. Equal parts absurd, camp and celestial, this is cabaret for those who prefer their future read with sequins, satire and a touch of chaos. **** Please also search for GHOST SONGS TO DIE FOR with MADAME ISOLDE ****

title: Ben Hur
summary: They said it was unachievable! They said it couldn't be done! And yet... The greatest book ever penned is brought to the stage by the towering team who produced One Man Two Guvnors, The Ladykillers, Brief Encounter and last year's smash hit The 39 Steps... and with just four actors! Complete with a bona fide chariot race, an authentic sea battle and a decadent Roman orgy (suitable for all ages), Ben Hur is The Greatest Story Ever Told.

title: The Summer Palace
summary: When an internationally acclaimed theatre director working under a totalitarian regime is brought in for questioning, he thinks his political activism is under scrutiny. But the person facing him across a desk in an otherwise empty room turns out to be an actor he hasn’t seen for nearly twenty years, and they have unfinished business. The director begins to realise that the interrogation isn’t quite what it seems as the personal and…
_description: When an internationally acclaimed theatre director working under a totalitarian regime is brought in for questioning, he thinks his political activism is under scrutiny. But the person facing him across a desk in an otherwise empty room turns out to be an actor he hasn’t seen for nearly twenty years, and they have unfinished business. The director begins to realise that the interrogation isn’t quite what it seems as the personal and the political become dangerously intertwined and the stakes are raised in a deadly game of cat-and-mouse. The twists and turns of darkly comedic stage play mark the return to the theatre of writer Paul Bassett Davies, who began his career in experimental multimedia performance before moving to radio and television, where he worked with some of the biggest names in British comedy and drama. He is author of four published novels and several stories, one of which was praised by Stephen King as “Mordantly funny... Poe would have liked it".

title: Edinburgh Previews Sara Barron And Josh Weller
summary: Join us for an evening of the freshest comedy as comedians Sara Barron (Live At The Apollo, Would I Lie To You) and Josh Weller (The Witcher, The Stand Up Sketch Show) test out brand-new material in their quest to build an hour(ish)-long show each. This is your chance to catch familiar favourites and rising stars at their most daring. Come along, have a laugh, and find the funny together!

summary: The Ghost Walk of the Lanes is an undemanding 80-minute tour of Brighton's oldest and most haunted quarter, visiting eight locations. All the stories are long-established accounts of hauntings in and around The Lanes, alongside historical facts and peppered with comic respite. This is a family-friendly event that…

This is a family-friendly event that has received the TripAdvisor Certificate of Excellence. Concessions for under 16s and for students.

title: Unidentified Fluid Other Gaylien De Mars
summary: SHOWTIMES: Doors 7:30pm - Show 8pm Exploring the feminine and masculine in a binary world to find the answer to the ultimate question: ‘Who am I?’ Unidentified Fluid Other is a ‘one-alien’ multi-media performance art piece with out-of-this-world live vocals by one of Brighton’s top vocalists and Titans Season 1 winner, Gaylien de Mars. An alien who can transform at will into anyone, Gaylien takes on many faces to better understand the…
_description: SHOWTIMES: Doors 7:30pm - Show 8pm Exploring the feminine and masculine in a binary world to find the answer to the ultimate question: ‘Who am I?’ Unidentified Fluid Other is a ‘one-alien’ multi-media performance art piece with out-of-this-world live vocals by one of Brighton’s top vocalists and Titans Season 1 winner, Gaylien de Mars. An alien who can transform at will into anyone, Gaylien takes on many faces to better understand the human experience. In Unidentified Fluid Other, Gaylien reflects on their experiences moving through this often overwhelming world, trying to find belonging and identity. This show holds a mirror to the human spirit through music and stunning projections. Exploring themes of human behaviour, addiction, self-discovery, gender, infertility, misogyny, and feminism. Gaylien de Mars is a versatile drag artist and performer, with incredible vocals, thought-provoking concepts, and clever stage design. LGBTQIA+. Neurodiverse AuDHD.

title: Jordan Jones A Street Mattress Named Desire
summary: Following a sellout work in progress run in Brighton Fringe 2025 - Komedia New Comedy Award 2025 semi finalist Jordan Jones brings his debut standup and musical comedy hour back to the Brighton Fringe for 2026. 2014 was the second worst year of Jordan's life. He was working a dead-end job, his girlfriend left him, his band was breaking up, and he was still living with his mum (and not in a smart, saving money way). Apparently, living…
_description: Following a sellout work in progress run in Brighton Fringe 2025 - Komedia New Comedy Award 2025 semi finalist Jordan Jones brings his debut standup and musical comedy hour back to the Brighton Fringe for 2026. 2014 was the second worst year of Jordan's life. He was working a dead-end job, his girlfriend left him, his band was breaking up, and he was still living with his mum (and not in a smart, saving money way). Apparently, living on a council estate and working at Sainsburys part-time wasn't a suitable backup plan for a failed music career. Come and see Jordan Jones as he explores how he got into that mess in the first place, his attempts to better himself by moving to Brighton, and when 2024 knocked 2014 off the top spot for the worst year of Jordan's life, how a life threatening illness put everything he learned to the test. Expect music, tales from working class suburbia, awful jobs, and overcoming the urge to forage for street furniture.

Expect charming storytelling, sharp one-liners and self-deprecating humour as they unpack growing up in the 90s, navigating culture, religion, sexuality and their parents. These fast-rising London comics have performed across the country –…
_description: Two comedians: one Chinese (Marco), one Arab-Kenyan (EJ). It might sound like an unlikely pairing, but one thing’s certain – they’ve both had hilarious experiences trying to make sense of the world. Expect charming storytelling, sharp one-liners and self-deprecating humour as they unpack growing up in the 90s, navigating culture, religion, sexuality and their parents. These fast-rising London comics have performed across the country – now they’re teaming up to share their stories side by side in this work in progress show. Is it perfect harmony... or a culture clash waiting to happen? You won’t see this mix anywhere else. How could you? These two bring experience, energy and fresh perspectives to the stage. Whether you’re a die-hard comedy fan who loves well-crafted jokes or you’ve just wandered in looking for a laugh, you’re in safe hands.

title: Speakeasy Jazz Club
summary: Join us at Hove’s home for Wednesday night jazz. May 6 –ALAN BARNES One of Britain’s most celebrated jazz musicians, fluent on alto, baritone and clarinet, for bebop from a true great. May 13 –MARK NIGHTINGALE The UK’s leading jazz trombonist; rich tone, dazzling technique for a golden-age Blue Note sound. May 20 –DAVE O’HIGGINS This multi-award-winning tenor saxophonist delivers a sound steeped in the spirit of jazz legends for a soulful, swinging night. May 27 –TONY KOFI A devoted disciple of Charlie Parker and major name in UK jazz, Tony brings his fiery alto sound to channel the spirit of Bird.

Don't worry, it'll make sense in the end! - Sketch Off Quarter Finalist 2026

As he shares tales of exploitation, broken promises, sex, cults, and the fine line between artifice and truth, it transpires there may be a more malevolent eye than that of the camera in the room. But the horrors on screen were never real – were…

Participation is…

title: Supernatural
summary: Supernatural revolves around a single choice - one that changed everything. Over the course of an hour, a room full of strangers is drawn into something intimate and personal - the choices you’ve made, the reality you're shaping, and how one decision, made now, could send ripples far into the future. With an uncanny ability to peer beneath appearances, David Stewart invites you into an experience of mystery, meaning, and possibility.…
_description: Supernatural revolves around a single choice - one that changed everything. Over the course of an hour, a room full of strangers is drawn into something intimate and personal - the choices you’ve made, the reality you're shaping, and how one decision, made now, could send ripples far into the future. With an uncanny ability to peer beneath appearances, David Stewart invites you into an experience of mystery, meaning, and possibility. As the night unfolds, a magic is revealed that feels less like illusion, and more like something present - subtle, flowing through it all. By the end, there is only one question: What will you choose today, that shapes your tomorrow? Your fate is in your hands. Doors open 30 minutes before the show starts. Please make sure you arrive in good time.

title: A Tribute To Ozzy A Classical Guitar Recital
summary: Matthew Sear: A Tribute to Ozzy. From Ozzy to Bach – A Guitar Journey You are very warmly invited to my guitar recital at the Brighton Fringe. I’m a classical guitarist and composer, and my work has featured on everything from Netflix UFO documentaries, too BBC Radio 3, to the Concert Platform. Most people know me for classical music — but my whole journey began at age 13, when I was lent a cassette of Ozzy Osbourne: Randy Rhoads…
_description: Matthew Sear: A Tribute to Ozzy. From Ozzy to Bach – A Guitar Journey You are very warmly invited to my guitar recital at the Brighton Fringe. I’m a classical guitarist and composer, and my work has featured on everything from Netflix UFO documentaries, too BBC Radio 3, to the Concert Platform. Most people know me for classical music — but my whole journey began at age 13, when I was lent a cassette of Ozzy Osbourne: Randy Rhoads Tribute. Ozzy’s music, and especially Randy Rhoads’ extraordinary guitar playing, first drew me toward the world of classical music. With Ozzy’s recent passing, I want to celebrate that connection — performing music that inspired his guitarists, the influences they shared (from The Beatles to Bach), and my own reworkings of iconic Black Sabbath and Ozzy pieces arranged for classical guitar. If you love discovering how heavy metal and classical music inspire one another, I think this will be a recital you’ll truly enjoy.

title: Therapy
summary: tHERapy is a compelling one-woman play that intimately portrays the life of Deola, a Nigerian woman navigating the complexities of identity, family, and societal expectations. The play intricately weaves together vignettes exploring themes of familial relationships—particularly the subtle dynamics within her extended family—love and heartbreak, and the challenges of gender in Nigerian culture, including situations of sexual harassment…
_description: tHERapy is a compelling one-woman play that intimately portrays the life of Deola, a Nigerian woman navigating the complexities of identity, family, and societal expectations. The play intricately weaves together vignettes exploring themes of familial relationships—particularly the subtle dynamics within her extended family—love and heartbreak, and the challenges of gender in Nigerian culture, including situations of sexual harassment and assault. Through Deola's candid storytelling, the play offers a poignant reflection on the strength and vulnerability of womanhood.

title: Sock Party
summary: Tom and Marta arrive at a sock-themed party. They didn’t expect to meet. Marta is bold, and Tom doesn’t know how to have a conversation with anyone, but they end up understanding each other more than they could’ve anticipated. In a colourful expression of drama, clowning and original music, Sock Party is a playful journey through love and connection, asking how as adults we manage to create new friends, celebrate and play. From the…
_description: Tom and Marta arrive at a sock-themed party. They didn’t expect to meet. Marta is bold, and Tom doesn’t know how to have a conversation with anyone, but they end up understanding each other more than they could’ve anticipated. In a colourful expression of drama, clowning and original music, Sock Party is a playful journey through love and connection, asking how as adults we manage to create new friends, celebrate and play. From the Creative Team that brought you Blip, 'a tender meditation on intergenerational tensions' (The Scotsman) that sold out Brighton Fringe in 2025.

title: Seven And A Half Years
summary: SEVEN and a half YEARS is deeply autobiographical, charting a life shaped by early brilliance, profound responsibility, devastating illness and a hard-won return to creativity. It is intimate and – surprisingly – uplifting. It is a powerful blend of songs, dialogue and soundscapes A sudden and unexplained breakdown left Mark with tinnitus, insomnia and muteness. For seven years, he lived in near-total isolation, spending up to 23 hours…
_description: SEVEN and a half YEARS is deeply autobiographical, charting a life shaped by early brilliance, profound responsibility, devastating illness and a hard-won return to creativity. It is intimate and – surprisingly – uplifting. It is a powerful blend of songs, dialogue and soundscapes A sudden and unexplained breakdown left Mark with tinnitus, insomnia and muteness. For seven years, he lived in near-total isolation, spending up to 23 hours a day in silence. Doctors could offer no explanation. Survival itself felt uncertain. And yet, this is ultimately a story of return. SEVEN and a half YEARS is a reminder that creativity can survive silence, that music can return after loss, and that it is never too late to begin again. It is not just a one-man play – it is an act of courage, resilience and creative rebirth, delivered with honesty, humility and, all, beautiful MUSIC. A quietly powerful, musically beautiful and deeply human piece of theatre.

That’s right! It was called ‘Moon Team’! Huh? You were going to say something else? Star Wars? No, never heard of it - you’re probably misremembering... Where was I... ? Ah yes: Moon Team. A film beloved by…
_description: In 1979, a sci-fi movie changed everything. It launched careers, changed Hollywood forever, and left a cultural footprint bigger than Neil Armstrong’s boot on the moon (metaphorically - Neil was a size 7). You all know its name... That’s right! It was called ‘Moon Team’! Huh? You were going to say something else? Star Wars? No, never heard of it - you’re probably misremembering... Where was I... ? Ah yes: Moon Team. A film beloved by all, but not the film we’ll be watching today. No, we’re voyaging to the galaxy’s grimy underside to watch Moon Team’s beloathed prequel sequel: Moon Team IIIV (2, if you think about it!). Hang onto your space hats (technical term) for a surreal sketch comedy odyssey through the best worst film never made, as BBC New Comedian 2025 semi-finalist Will BF presents his award-winning (Buxton Fringe Best Comedy Show 2025) hour of sketches, songs, live video games and sci-fi multimedia havoc.

Follow the Bride through an anarchic hour of love, longing, puppets, lemons & the Radio Times. This is not a Work In Progress. It is a Wedding In Progress.

title: Fearless And Fabulous Women Of Hove
summary: One of the first women to wear a police uniform, a champion of animal rights, a mental health pioneer, a landmark women's hospital, the creator of The Wombles and many, many more! Expect to be surprised and inspired! Accessible route.

title: Witchcraft Magic Tour
summary: Returning after a sold-out run at last year's Fringe, come with us to find out about the mystical and esoteric history of Brighton. The tour will take you from a ley line nexus to the first blue plaque to ever honour a witch, covering stone circles, an alleged occult cult and the satanic panic along the way. Find out about the witchcraft trials in Sussex, Aleister Crowley's link to Brighton and life for witches in Regency England. If…

title: Toast Stories From The End Of The World
summary: Would you savour the last beer on Earth... or save it? At the end of the world, two friends scavenging through the ruins stumble across the last beer (ever?) and maybe wish they hadn't. As they reminisce, we are taken back to look at people, places and events framed by bended elbows, pub sofas and people you know from every night out. Toast is a darkly funny, intimate end-of-the-world play about connection, memory and the last drink on…
_description: Would you savour the last beer on Earth... or save it? At the end of the world, two friends scavenging through the ruins stumble across the last beer (ever?) and maybe wish they hadn't. As they reminisce, we are taken back to look at people, places and events framed by bended elbows, pub sofas and people you know from every night out. Toast is a darkly funny, intimate end-of-the-world play about connection, memory and the last drink on Earth. Adapted from Chance Dean’s original short play Toast, first performed during Witwerks Theatre’s sell-out run of Dramatic Draughts: Four Short Plays About the Pub, this new Fringe-length version artfully expands the story with just two actors playing a cast of vividly drawn characters from before the apocalypse.

summary: 100% Badgers is the sell-out 2025 Edinburgh Fringe stand-up comedy show where Matt Hobs talks about how the worlds of humans and badgers collide. The show takes us on Hobs’ journey from a passing interest in badgers, to an obsession that leads to him standing in a field looking at a caged, frightened badger caught up in the misinformation of the bovine TB debate. We’ll learn about the European badger and its dachshund nemesis, the…
_description: 100% Badgers is the sell-out 2025 Edinburgh Fringe stand-up comedy show where Matt Hobs talks about how the worlds of humans and badgers collide. The show takes us on Hobs’ journey from a passing interest in badgers, to an obsession that leads to him standing in a field looking at a caged, frightened badger caught up in the misinformation of the bovine TB debate. We’ll learn about the European badger and its dachshund nemesis, the presidential American badger, the world-famous internet sensation that is the honey badger and many more.

title: Duro Wait To Be Pampered
summary: DURO! is a spellbinding stage play that weaves mystery, love, and destiny into a rich tapestry of African spirituality. When an Abiku, a spirit-child who has journeyed between worlds thirteen times, decides to stay on earth with her mother, her otherworldly spouse returns to lure her back for a once-in-a-century festival. Torn between eternal love and earthly devotion, Duro faces a heart-wrenching choice that blurs the line between…
_description: DURO! is a spellbinding stage play that weaves mystery, love, and destiny into a rich tapestry of African spirituality. When an Abiku, a spirit-child who has journeyed between worlds thirteen times, decides to stay on earth with her mother, her otherworldly spouse returns to lure her back for a once-in-a-century festival. Torn between eternal love and earthly devotion, Duro faces a heart-wrenching choice that blurs the line between life and the afterlife. Set in a world where music, myth, and politics intertwine, *DURO!* captivates with its poetry, symbolism, and emotional power — a timeless reflection on love, sacrifice, and the spirit’s longing to belong.

Every piece is completely unique to the person who made it, and yours to take home and treasure on the day. Spaces are limited, don't miss out. Book your spot now.

Breaking away from the typical notion that kink performance must rely on fetish fashion and shock value, Transient and Trifling shines light on raw, vulnerable and visceral portrayals of BDSM. A cabaret-theatre show that is as much a love letter as a call for change, this edition features four award-winning performers reflecting on their personal experiences and the history and culture that shapes the community today.

_description: An Indian writer and a Polish actor walk into a bar - and a SPLIT BILL is born. Maja Bloom and Sudeepta Sanyal combine forces for a Work-in-Progress: Forty, Foreign & Ferocious! There will be gossip, accents, and there just might be vodka shots. Is that a bribe? or a threat? Both! We did the math. It’s going to be a riot! Come see what the fuss is all about! Maja Bloom is a multilingual actress, singer and a stand-up comedian best known for playing Carrow in the Fantastic Beasts franchise. "She is funny, with the promise of becoming even funnier' according to Edinburgh Reviews. She charms audiences all around Europe with her frank observations of an immigrant Brit who is never 'British enough'. Sudeepta Sanyal is Runner-up at West End New Old Act 2025, Finalist at West End New Act 2026 & an alum of SOHO Theatre Comedy Labs plus 2026 and Brown Sauce X Tara Theatre 2025. Her stand up is culturally confused. Emotionally raw. Unapologetically herself.

_description: Join us for an evening of original electro pop, bringing together three great electronic acts from in and around the Brighton area. Terahurtz - Brighton based duo Terahurtz, formed early 2019, delivers catchy, soulful and charismatic electro-pop. Terahurtz is a collaboration between synth fan Matt McEwan - (Telesumo and Astroscapes) - and offbeat, wholehearted pop writer & vocalist Emmy Hush - whose solo song catalogue spans 30 years. Both Emmy & Matt share a love of electro, 80s synth-pop & punk and have just released their latest album 'SPEED DIAL'. Pixelstorm - An energetic twist of modern electronica, 80s synth sounds, live keytar and epic vocals. Radio Wigwam best Electro Act 2026. 'Known for a larger than life sound and a distinctive stage presence, Pixelstorm continues to stand out within the electronic scene' Deluxe - Out of the comfort zone, riff heavy synthpop, a dusting of punk attitude & new wave vibes.

title: On Barren Hill Work In Progress
venue_name: Fishing Museum Loft - The Old Net Loft
summary: A dreamlike, visually striking & ultimately hopeful Dance-Theatre show interrogating the relationship to the natural world-the tension between grief & growth-after infertility & loss. Bathe in a heady combination of movement, lyrical text, opera & projection. Devised in & celebrating diverse environments across Europe, join a cyclical journey across a lush Czech climax forest, barren Mediterranean cliff edge & harsh shingle dessert…
_description: A dreamlike, visually striking & ultimately hopeful Dance-Theatre show interrogating the relationship to the natural world-the tension between grief & growth-after infertility & loss. Bathe in a heady combination of movement, lyrical text, opera & projection. Devised in & celebrating diverse environments across Europe, join a cyclical journey across a lush Czech climax forest, barren Mediterranean cliff edge & harsh shingle dessert before plunging into an acrid silver lake. The work will feature co-created elements from creative workshops with women with lived experience of the themes. There will be a post-show discussion at each performance & a small display of photography & artwork relating to the show's development. Fertility Network UK will also be present. *Pine needle syrup cocktail/mocktail with each ticket* Intimate & unique 40 capacity venue- book ahead to avoid disappointment! Work in progress show with intention to tour. R&D supported by South East Dance

title: The Biscuit One Work In Progress
summary: Duncan Hart is a work in progress—but aren’t we all? From almost dying (twice) to second chances, this is a funny, uplifting story of sobriety and starting again. And possibly biscuits. Definitely diabetes. Oh, and heart problems. Duncan burst onto the comedy circuit in the late ’90s at just 18. After years on the road, he stepped away from the mic to build a successful career behind the scenes in television, working with comedy…
_description: Duncan Hart is a work in progress—but aren’t we all? From almost dying (twice) to second chances, this is a funny, uplifting story of sobriety and starting again. And possibly biscuits. Definitely diabetes. Oh, and heart problems. Duncan burst onto the comedy circuit in the late ’90s at just 18. After years on the road, he stepped away from the mic to build a successful career behind the scenes in television, working with comedy heavyweights including Dara Ó Briain, Russell Howard, James Acaster, Rosie Jones and Arthur Smith. Now, after more than a decade away, he’s back—sharper, hungrier, and with plenty to say. Energetic, sharp and effortlessly likeable, Duncan mines his tragic-comic life for every painfully funny moment, blending punchy writing, crowd work, and just the right amount of chaos. With a commanding stage presence and a natural gift for banter, he’s once again being tipped as 'one to watch'. 3x Gong Winner including Funhouse Championship & Up The Creek Blackout (2025)
