title: Anna Dumitriu Bioart Transformations
venue_name: The Regency Town House - Basement Annexe
summary: Art and science combine in “BioArt Transformations”, a cutting-edge new exhibition by internationally acclaimed contemporary artist Anna Dumitriu. A pioneer of the BioArt movement, Dumitriu works with living bacteria, DNA, and technology to create intricate sculptures, installations and textile works that draw threads across time from the history of science and medicine to cutting-edge research in synthetic biology, genomics and AI.…
_description: Art and science combine in “BioArt Transformations”, a cutting-edge new exhibition by internationally acclaimed contemporary artist Anna Dumitriu. A pioneer of the BioArt movement, Dumitriu works with living bacteria, DNA, and technology to create intricate sculptures, installations and textile works that draw threads across time from the history of science and medicine to cutting-edge research in synthetic biology, genomics and AI. Her strange and affecting objects take the form of relics of Dumitriu’s sublime laboratory-based processes created in contemporary biotechnological research settings, interwoven with historic stories of contagion and alchemy. A visually stunning and deeply thought-provoking experience, this exhibition is a must-see for the curious, challenging our view on the future of medicine, technology, and humanity. There will be a series of events throughout the exhibition. Book for these via the artist’s website https://annadumitriu.co.uk/news/

title: Are You Even Indian
summary: Are You Even Indian? follows a British-born Indian woman and an Indian-born man who fall in love. It feels like fate. A meet-cute, wedding dances, Bollywood jokes, and plans for a future they believe they are building together. But as the relationship deepens, so do the cracks. Which country is “home”? What language will their child speak? Who gets to decide what makes someone truly Indian? As personal choices clash with family…
_description: Are You Even Indian? follows a British-born Indian woman and an Indian-born man who fall in love. It feels like fate. A meet-cute, wedding dances, Bollywood jokes, and plans for a future they believe they are building together. But as the relationship deepens, so do the cracks. Which country is “home”? What language will their child speak? Who gets to decide what makes someone truly Indian? As personal choices clash with family expectations and social labels, the couple must confront love, compromise, and the reality of living in between. Neither fully British, nor fully Indian, the show reflects the experience of a generation navigating relationships and heritage. When both your homes ask you to apologise for who you are, how do you decide where you belong? This is a love story about proving your identity, not just to each other, but within the community. Best Show, Bitesize Festival 2025 Riverside Studios, London

title: An Evening Without Kate Bush
summary: Enter Strange Phenomena, Howl with the Hounds Of Love and dance on the moors with Wuthering Heights. Kate's not there, but you are. This award-winning cult cabaret pays glorious tribute to the music, fans and mythology of one of the most influential voices in British music. Featuring classic hits like Running Up That Hill, Cloudbusting and The Man With The Child In His Eyes, ‘An Evening Without Kate Bush’ returns to Brighton after…
_description: Enter Strange Phenomena, Howl with the Hounds Of Love and dance on the moors with Wuthering Heights. Kate's not there, but you are. This award-winning cult cabaret pays glorious tribute to the music, fans and mythology of one of the most influential voices in British music. Featuring classic hits like Running Up That Hill, Cloudbusting and The Man With The Child In His Eyes, ‘An Evening Without Kate Bush’ returns to Brighton after selling out the Corn Exchange last year. Whether you’ve been a fan for decades or recently joined the shoal via ‘Stranger Things’, there’s never been a better time to release your inner Bush and celebrate at this joyful, unique and mind-blowing show. 'You don’t need to be a Kate Bush fan to be transported into something very special' Scotsman ★★★★ Winner Best Cabaret Adelaide Fringe 2025

'Screenshots' maps her work from it's beginnings, tying her poetry to key moments in her life, in history…
_description: Bassie Gracie is a punk performance poet known for her musings as a musician, feel good feminism and general silliness. Frequently found shoeless on the Monday of a music festival telling a 70 year old geezer ‘punk was better in my day’. Her show combines jokes, anecdotes and rhyming couplets, intended to inspire, amuse and excite. 'Screenshots' maps her work from it's beginnings, tying her poetry to key moments in her life, in history and in Brighton. “Bassie Gracie captivates hearts and minds with her powerful words of wisdom and good times” - Beans On Toast Following in the footsteps of John Cooper Clarke and Kae Tempest, Gracie makes poetry accessible, engaging and, somehow, a financially viable career path.

_description: “Opera humour - so different. Fabulous!” Welcome to Ballgowns & Bib Shorts - a musical memoir of an opera-singing triathlete.! Following on from the success of Disgraceful, which debuted at Dorchester Arts in 2023, Grace Lovelass is back with a new show reliving the peaks and plateaus along her climb to becoming a professional opera singer and IRONMAN triathlete. Billed as a musical memoir, Ballgowns & Bib Shorts is the soundtrack to a decade of operatic and athletic adventures. Alongside comedy and candid insights, Grace presents an effervescent mix of classical, show tunes and popular music, as well as irreverent, custom-written parody, to take audiences on a journey that proves dreams really do come true... “Incredible, hilarious, uplifting and amazing' - get ready for a moving and inspirational evening with our wonderful singing triathlete this year, and see what can happen with a little motivation!

_description: From the very towering tips of his perfectly-coiffed quiff down to the tips of his bejewelled toes, Boogaloo Stu is altogether completely ridiculous, simply fabulous and utterly idiotic. Big Quiffy Bingo is his gloriously playful and anarchic bingo experience, where utter filth, joyous fun and flash mob dances collide. Following his fifth annual sell-out season of shows at the Brighton Spiegelgardens, Stu is back at the Spiegeltent once again, this time joined by his very special guests The Two Wrongies. Feel the tension rise as Stu’s balls begin to churn; when they finally drop, you’ll feast on a smorgasbord of smut, outrageous antics, buttock-clenching workouts, hilarious prizes and some very ballsy bingo to boot. Stu’s gift hamper will be stuffed to the brim with all manner of exotic prizes, so come on down and win it big! Please note - this event features sexual swearwords from the outset..

summary: Beyond the Book Festival returns to Brighton Fringe on Saturday 16 May 2026, celebrating the power of stories in all their forms. Set in the historic Friends Meeting House on Ship Street, this vibrant literary celebration brings together readers, writers, and creatives for an unforgettable day of inspiration and connection. Our stellar line-up features global bestselling author Adele Parks in conversation with her movie-producer…
_description: Beyond the Book Festival returns to Brighton Fringe on Saturday 16 May 2026, celebrating the power of stories in all their forms. Set in the historic Friends Meeting House on Ship Street, this vibrant literary celebration brings together readers, writers, and creatives for an unforgettable day of inspiration and connection. Our stellar line-up features global bestselling author Adele Parks in conversation with her movie-producer husband Jim Parks, offering unique insights into storytelling across page and screen. Discover new voices through our diverse programme of panels led by leading authors, industry talks behind the scenes of publishing, and exclusive agent pitching sessions for unagented writers. Beyond the Book Festival creates an inclusive space that celebrates creativity, diversity, and the joy of storytelling. Join us for unforgettable moments that go beyond the page.

_description: “Look. You two need to loosen up a bit, because you might be here for an unbelievably long time, okay? Just a piece of advice. - Do you want a smoothie?” Simon Huxley (corporate hack/ amateur historian), and Annette (Languages Teacher/ yogi) struggle to co-operate whilst trying to escape their unpredictable, health-obsessed kidnapper. Political, blender-adjacent dark comedy written by Harry Smithson. Directed by award-winning director Chris Yarnell. Following a sold out run at the White Bear Theatre, this politically driven piece of new writing explores the difficulty of nuanced discussion, disagreement in the modern age, and how pre-conceptions can blind us even during hostile, bizarre, and life-threatening situations.

title: Boggin
summary: “Generational loss... The older we get the more we lose, the stories, the people and the fragmented memories all get distorted, but what if we could clear this image? What if the VHS spat itself back out, the boogyman strangled us with the tape and sent us down the river? BOGGIN aims to help us navigate this low road.” “BOGGIN” is an experimental, auto-biographical performance that explores the concept of 'Generational Loss' and…
_description: “Generational loss... The older we get the more we lose, the stories, the people and the fragmented memories all get distorted, but what if we could clear this image? What if the VHS spat itself back out, the boogyman strangled us with the tape and sent us down the river? BOGGIN aims to help us navigate this low road.” “BOGGIN” is an experimental, auto-biographical performance that explores the concept of 'Generational Loss' and applying it to the human experience. “BOGGIN” aims to combine elements of family memories with creatures and tales from Scottish Folklore. All while communicating a story recently passed down to me by my Grandmother about our family's history and connections to Ethiopia and my family's part in the Great Abbai Expedition. The show aims to challenge viewers to care more about what their elders have to say, to care about their culture, to care about the documentation of one's life. A love letter to my Scottish heritage, to my Family and to my Uncle.

title: Bluff Creek
summary: In 1967, Bob Gimlin and Roger Patterson went for a ride in the woods with a movie camera and a mission: to catch a Bigfoot on film. And that’s just what they did. To some, it’s the real McCoy; to others, it’s just a man in a monkey suit. To Bob Gimlin, it’s free drinks at the Idle Hour Inn and a flock of fans lining up to hear him tell the story one more time. Now, decades later, an investigator from out of town is starting to ask all…
_description: In 1967, Bob Gimlin and Roger Patterson went for a ride in the woods with a movie camera and a mission: to catch a Bigfoot on film. And that’s just what they did. To some, it’s the real McCoy; to others, it’s just a man in a monkey suit. To Bob Gimlin, it’s free drinks at the Idle Hour Inn and a flock of fans lining up to hear him tell the story one more time. Now, decades later, an investigator from out of town is starting to ask all the wrong questions, and a figure from the past is threatening to let slip what really happened at Bluff Creek all that time ago. Based on real events, Bluff Creek examines the lives of the men behind the Patterson- Gimlin film, the iconic piece of footage that brought us the image of Bigfoot we know today. It’s a story about the cost of lies, the nature of belief, and the enduring fascination with America’s resident Abominable Snowman. Praise for Loose Thread's debut play The Graveyard: ★★★★ -“Dead Good” The Derek Awards, ★★★★- Binge Fringe

title: Brighton Gay Mens Chorus Presents Heroes
summary: Brighton Gay Men’s Chorus is back for the Fringe with a feel-good show about the queer icons who helped shape us as well as the heroes right here in our own city. We’ll be celebrating the heroes many of us grew up with, from David Bowie, Freddie Mercury and George Michael, through to newer heroes like JADE who are carrying that legacy forward today. Alongside choral favourites and a handful of solos, the show also features interviews…
_description: Brighton Gay Men’s Chorus is back for the Fringe with a feel-good show about the queer icons who helped shape us as well as the heroes right here in our own city. We’ll be celebrating the heroes many of us grew up with, from David Bowie, Freddie Mercury and George Michael, through to newer heroes like JADE who are carrying that legacy forward today. Alongside choral favourites and a handful of solos, the show also features interviews with LGBTQ+ heroes from Brighton & Hove, putting the spotlight on those doing brilliant things for our community right now. All the music is arranged by our in-house music team, giving familiar songs a fresh choral twist. Expect to laugh, shed a few tears and to experience plenty of queer joy, as we celebrate the artists who helped us feel seen growing up, and the local heroes still making a difference today.

_description: The Brighton Beach Boys play Bowie: Golden Years- Greatest hits The band recreates David Bowie’s hits and favourites, drawing on material drawn from throughout his illustrious career. David Bowie emerged from London's vibrant 1960s music scene to become one of the most influential artists of the 20th century. His breakthrough came in 1969, leading to an extraordinary run of groundbreaking albums in the 1970s that redefined rock music. Known for his chameleon-like ability to reinvent himself, Bowie's artistic evolution carried him through multiple personas and musical styles across five decades, earning him a place among Britain's most celebrated cultural icons. Featuring the vocal talents of Glen Richardson and Stephen Wrigley, lead guitar by Adrian Marshall, with Dave Barnard on bass, Charlotte Glasson on Sax, Flute, and anything else, Jono Harrison on keys and Vinnie Lammi on drums. Whether it's Major Tom or The Jeanie Genie, Kooks or Ziggy Stardust, this is a show full of stories

title: Buster
venue_name: The Dance Space - The Jamie Watton Creation Space
summary: Through cinematic mime and physical theatre, this high-octane performance follows silent film star Buster Keaton's journey from childhood through to his late career. Focusing on Buster's own perspective on what it means to be a performer, the show explores his highs, his lows, the friends he made along the way, his struggle with alcoholism, and his journey to eventually reaching what he described as "the happiest time of his life". Set…
_description: Through cinematic mime and physical theatre, this high-octane performance follows silent film star Buster Keaton's journey from childhood through to his late career. Focusing on Buster's own perspective on what it means to be a performer, the show explores his highs, his lows, the friends he made along the way, his struggle with alcoholism, and his journey to eventually reaching what he described as "the happiest time of his life". Set to one continuous soundtrack, the ensemble brings the story to life through mime, clowning, lip-sync, puppetry, dance and live onstage lighting control, creating a film-like atmosphere that reimagines mime for a new generation. A celebration of genius, resilience and the timeless craft of physical storytelling. For older audiences, a chance to revisit a screen legend. For younger ones, an introduction to Buster Keaton's brilliance.

Kick off your Fringe Festival the only acceptable way at Cabarave’s Filthy Gorgeous takeover of the iconic Spiegeltent on Opening Saturday. After a sell-out smash last Fringe, Cabarave were invited back to host the opening night in the festival’s most renowned venue — YES PLEASE. Expect an electrifying night of banging big beats, headlined by legendary party starter Krafty Kuts, plus show-stopping cabaret from the cream of the Brighton circuit. Get stuck into immersive fun with walkabout acts, Tarot readings, and a glitter artist ready to filthy you up. Filthy Gorgeous ticket holders you are also the stars of this party. This theme is about expression, not perfection — colourful, experimental, sassy hot messes head to our dancefloor. You can’t be too flamboyant for this party, and the best dressed wins tickets to our next event. Come pristine. Leave undone. Fringe starts here.

title: Clandestino
summary: Clandestino returns with a fiery, genre-defying performance fusing flamenco, jazz and rhythms from across the globe. At its heart is the incisive, impassioned footwork of flamenco dancer, Wafa Paton driving the music with electrifying force. Featuring master cantor Martin Paredes Mansilla and jazz vocalist Rachel Myer, the show unfolds as a powerful celebration of cultural exchange and resilience, revealing the shared human pulse that…
_description: Clandestino returns with a fiery, genre-defying performance fusing flamenco, jazz and rhythms from across the globe. At its heart is the incisive, impassioned footwork of flamenco dancer, Wafa Paton driving the music with electrifying force. Featuring master cantor Martin Paredes Mansilla and jazz vocalist Rachel Myer, the show unfolds as a powerful celebration of cultural exchange and resilience, revealing the shared human pulse that unites diverse musical worlds. Bold, soulful and deeply moving, Clandestino offers an unforgettable, joy-filled experience where global traditions meet and ignite on stage - rich with passion, virtuosity, surprise and rhythm filled connection.

_description: Dashing drag-dandy Cava Charley hosts an all singing, all dancing theatrical extravaganza. You’re invited into the backstage chaos as Cava deals with cast mate crises, management malpractice, and a building that’s crumbling underfoot. You’re an audience looking for a good time, you’re a tired artiste sharing gossip in the dressing room... you might be called upon to save the show! Packed with song and silliness, Backstage Pass is a funny and fearless look at the legacy of mental illness in the arts, our reticence towards self care, and what draws us to the magic and the mayhem of live theatre.

An intimate evening exploring how Chekhov so perfectly captured love, longing and the quiet complexities of the human heart. ​With scenes from The Seagull, Uncle Vanya, Three Sisters…

title: Club De Fromage
summary: It's back! London’s legendary pop time machine returns for another frolic through the decades! Slow Alfie (ex-used car salesman and close friend of Christopher Biggins) and his team of crack performing disco desperados will lead you in a night of dancing, massive sing-alongs, hula hooping, confetti cannons, air guitaring, dance competitions, the finest pop, rock, disco and power ballads known to humanity and the odd, 'what in hell just…
_description: It's back! London’s legendary pop time machine returns for another frolic through the decades! Slow Alfie (ex-used car salesman and close friend of Christopher Biggins) and his team of crack performing disco desperados will lead you in a night of dancing, massive sing-alongs, hula hooping, confetti cannons, air guitaring, dance competitions, the finest pop, rock, disco and power ballads known to humanity and the odd, 'what in hell just happened', moment from the incredibly strange Fromage stage show. Truly the club that time forgot, Club de Fromage celebrates all that was great about the clubs of days gone by but without the smell of fags and the sticky carpet. Always a sold-out Brighton Fringe show, don't leave it too late to get your tickets. If you haven't seen Fromage before at festivals like Glastonbury, Latitude, Reading, Snowbombing and Camp Bestival or attended one of their legendary NYE parties at the Hammersmith Apollo, then welcome to the wacky world of Fromage!

title: Com Edm
summary: TOTAL COSMISCH: COM-EDM Crazy comedy mixed with wild dance music. Come and laugh your head off while getting your groove on. Enter a world where struggling German DJs Fred & Dieter are on a mission to resurrect the original spirit…
_description: TOTAL COSMISCH: COM-EDM Crazy comedy mixed with wild dance music. Come and laugh your head off while getting your groove on. Fresh from a successful run at Leicester Comedy Festival, musical comedy duo Total Cosmisch (Dom Virgo & Matt Dearlove) return with a brand-new barrage of sketches, stories, characters and dance floor beats. Enter a world where struggling German DJs Fred & Dieter are on a mission to resurrect the original spirit of house and techno... while deep below, the Devil and his son Abezethibou attempt to overhaul the music policy in Hell. Blending surreal storytelling with club culture, gospel, rock, rave and soul, Total Cosmisch turn modern life into high-energy chaos — with manic theatricality, ridiculous characters, and harmonies that hit far harder than they have any right to. Part gig, part sketch show, part dance floor sermon. For fans of Flight of the Conchords, The Mighty Boosh & Meatloaf.

title: Comedy Club 4 Kids
summary: Since 2005, the Comedy Club 4 Kids has been getting the best stand-ups, sketch acts and cabaret stars from the international circuit to do their thing for an audience of children (aged 6+) and their families... but without the rude bits! Over twenty years later, the company has expanded from one London residency to being countrywide, running shows and workshops all over the UK from Peebles to Portsmouth, even producing a book on how to…
_description: Since 2005, the Comedy Club 4 Kids has been getting the best stand-ups, sketch acts and cabaret stars from the international circuit to do their thing for an audience of children (aged 6+) and their families... but without the rude bits! Over twenty years later, the company has expanded from one London residency to being countrywide, running shows and workshops all over the UK from Peebles to Portsmouth, even producing a book on how to write and perform stand-up, and hosting our own Radio Nonsense podcast to bring the sillies to families all over the world. In August 2025, we won The Victoria Wood Award, the Edinburgh Comedy Awards’ panel prize, in recognition of our two decades in this glorious endeavour. The show is family friendly, but without any patronising idiocy. In fact, it's just like a normal comedy club, but it's on in the day, kids are allowed in, and thus there is a higher than usual chance of the entire audience claiming to be called Bob.

summary: Welcome to backstage! Here, you will find the Ladies of the Chorus getting ready for opening night of a small-scale production of Verdi's Macbetto. You may have seen them before as Cigarette Girls, Nuns, and Geishas... tonight, they are the Witches! Despite their numerous appearances, would you recognise them in The Lanes? Or know their names? Who really are the people you only seem to see 'upstage'? Set in a theatre broom cupboard,…
_description: Welcome to backstage! Here, you will find the Ladies of the Chorus getting ready for opening night of a small-scale production of Verdi's Macbetto. You may have seen them before as Cigarette Girls, Nuns, and Geishas... tonight, they are the Witches! Despite their numerous appearances, would you recognise them in The Lanes? Or know their names? Who really are the people you only seem to see 'upstage'? Set in a theatre broom cupboard, next to the building’s only toilet, Destination: ‘Old Hag’ is a Buxton Fringe- nominated production that draws on the Ladies’ many years in the world of opera, so join them as they chart the ups and downs of life - on stage and off - with humour, heart, and some live opera singing! Destination: ‘Old Hag’ was created and written by Samantha Houston, Bridget Hardy and Penny Rossano. It stars Bridget Hardy, Penny Rossano and Samantha Houston – three opera singers with more years’ stage experience than they care to mention...

title: Dinner With The Sls

Join the irresistible duo for a delectable hour of stand up comedy. A Dinner with the SL**S is a whimsical blend of religious upbringings, eccentric families, and bizarre dating dynamics, with a focus on…
_description: A Dinner with the SL**S brings together two of the UK comedy circuit's tastiest talents, Laura Moss and Amelia Fritz-Millet, to deliver a show that is delightfully mischievous, ridiculously silly, and effortlessly authentic. Join the irresistible duo for a delectable hour of stand up comedy. A Dinner with the SL**S is a whimsical blend of religious upbringings, eccentric families, and bizarre dating dynamics, with a focus on celebrating ‘sluts’ and everything they encompass. The show also has an interactive element where our delicious audience submit their problems, and we offer our best, sluttiest advice. As featured on: Comedy Central, Sky TV, Channel 4.0, Backyard Comedy Club, Top Secret Comedy Club, Big Belly Comedy Club, Up The Creek Comedy Club

title: Do The Thing Choose Your Own Musical
summary: Where is it set? Who's it about? What is the plot? You tell us. Brighton Fringe stunners Do the Thing are here to give you exactly the musical you want to see. Not only will the audience decide the setting and characters but they can also direct the plot. Should the hero run off with the love of their life or become a monk and fight crime? Will the villain achieve world domination even though they couldn’t really be bothered? Will the…
_description: Where is it set? Who's it about? What is the plot? You tell us. Brighton Fringe stunners Do the Thing are here to give you exactly the musical you want to see. Not only will the audience decide the setting and characters but they can also direct the plot. Should the hero run off with the love of their life or become a monk and fight crime? Will the villain achieve world domination even though they couldn’t really be bothered? Will the hero and villain reject such reductive labelling, realise they’re just people, and hug it out already? Be careful what you wish for as things can and will get very silly indeed. Expect Do the Thing’s full throttle commitment to the daftest ideas, the most farcical characters and tunes you’ll be whistling for weeks after. All made up for you live by a cast of two (Yes Anderson & Stellar Firma’s Tim Meredith and Simon Plotkin) and an orchestra of one (Ian Scott).

title: Do The Thing Choose Your Own Musical Family Friendly
summary: Brighton Fringe favourites present their first ever family friendly show! Where is it set? Who's it about? What is the plot? You tell us. Do the Thing are here to give you exactly the musical you want to see. Not only will the audience decide the setting and characters but they can also direct the plot. Should the hero run off with the love of their life or become a monk and fight crime? Will the villain achieve world domination even…
_description: Brighton Fringe favourites present their first ever family friendly show! Where is it set? Who's it about? What is the plot? You tell us. Do the Thing are here to give you exactly the musical you want to see. Not only will the audience decide the setting and characters but they can also direct the plot. Should the hero run off with the love of their life or become a monk and fight crime? Will the villain achieve world domination even though they couldn’t really be bothered? Any way you want it, that's the way we'll do it, no matter how wild the result! It could be anything from a space adventure, a fantasy quest or a gritty drama (but obviously not too gritty). The sky isn't even the limit. Expect Do the Thing’s full throttle commitment to the daftest ideas, the silliest characters and tunes you’ll be whistling for weeks after. All made up for you live by a cast of two (Yes Anderson & Stellar Firma’s Tim Meredith and Simon Plotkin) and an orchestra of one (Ian Scott).

title: Dont Panic
summary: The year is 2108. Two stagehands, Mani and Kid, get stuck in a rundown New York City theatre as part of a standard nuclear warfare drill. As the threat of the world's end closes in on them, they debate the purpose of living in a world that’s poisoned the human experience, seeking answers to an impossible question: what is the point of trying if there’s nothing left to live for? Don’t Panic is a bold, two-hander dark comedy about two…
_description: The year is 2108. Two stagehands, Mani and Kid, get stuck in a rundown New York City theatre as part of a standard nuclear warfare drill. As the threat of the world's end closes in on them, they debate the purpose of living in a world that’s poisoned the human experience, seeking answers to an impossible question: what is the point of trying if there’s nothing left to live for? Don’t Panic is a bold, two-hander dark comedy about two stage hands who, stuck together during a nuclear lockdown drill, debate the purpose of living in a world that’s poisoned the human experience, seeking answers to an impossible question: what is the point of trying if there’s nothing left to live for? Merging comedy, drama, and magical realism, the play delves into the roles of purpose, ambitions, and individuality in a society that is doomed to fail.

title: Doctor Coppelius
summary: "I am not a bad person. You say we're the bad people, I can't do anything right by you ... But tell me: if all the things I do are bad, what difference does one more bad thing make?" Everyone knows the love story of Swanhilda, Franz, and Coppelia but what is the story of the doll's creator, the sinister and reclusive and genius Doctor Coppelius? Now, he grants you an audience, with his tale of the sinister Wandering People, a woman…
_description: "I am not a bad person. You say we're the bad people, I can't do anything right by you ... But tell me: if all the things I do are bad, what difference does one more bad thing make?" Everyone knows the love story of Swanhilda, Franz, and Coppelia but what is the story of the doll's creator, the sinister and reclusive and genius Doctor Coppelius? Now, he grants you an audience, with his tale of the sinister Wandering People, a woman named Clara. With dehumanisation and humanity, scaremongering and understanding, revenge and forgiveness, despair and hope, it is time for the tragic villain from the Delibes's ballet to give his side of the story.

title: Dru Cripps Juicy Bits
summary: Master clown Dru Cripps presents his phenomenally successful loopstation-based shenanigans. This quadruple award-winner (British Comedy Guide, Malcolm Hardee, Comedy Store King Gong, Solo FX Sponsorship) and BBC New Comedian of the Year nominee uses questionable musical abilities and dangerous levels of confidence to transform audience suggestions into improvised songs, spontaneous musical sketches, and utterly winning chaos in real-…
_description: Master clown Dru Cripps presents his phenomenally successful loopstation-based shenanigans. This quadruple award-winner (British Comedy Guide, Malcolm Hardee, Comedy Store King Gong, Solo FX Sponsorship) and BBC New Comedian of the Year nominee uses questionable musical abilities and dangerous levels of confidence to transform audience suggestions into improvised songs, spontaneous musical sketches, and utterly winning chaos in real-time. A show so good it earned five 5-star reviews, sold out Edinburgh completely, and added extra dates. As seen on BBC, ITV and Dave, Dru was nominated for Edinburgh Fringe Comedian of the Year for this performance that leaves audiences rolling in the aisles laughing at something that really has to be seen to be misunderstood. Every performance is completely different. Every audience becomes part of the beautiful musical carnage.

title: Edinburgh Previews Rob Auton And Huge Davies

_description: Bringing together the elegance of Paris and the strength of femininity, Essense Heels showcases the power and beauty of dance. Standing tall on their stilettos, the Essense cast promises a night of bold movement, grooves, and striking shapes. The cast brings together Brighton heels dancers, celebrating the diverse styles, stories, and energies they each bring to the stage. Directed and choreographed by Essense founder Pearl Makayi, who also takes to the stage throughout the evening, this showcase marks her first full-length production. Pearl’s artistry is a vital part of the Brighton dance scene, known for its confidence, sensuality, and emotional presence. Join us for a good dance and a good time as we celebrate femininity, power, and unapologetic self-expression. Expect fierce performances, high energy, and a night that invites you to feel, move, and leave inspired.

title: Filmpride 3000 Lesbians Launch Party
summary: To celebrate FilmPride's short film screenings in the Light Room across 22 - 24 May, we take over the theatre space with a bang! Please join us for a very exciting launch night party featuring some very special guests, musical entertainment, and free popcorn. With a screening of the documentary celebrating YLAF - the York Lesbian Arts Festival - featuring many famous faces: '3000 Lesbians Go To York'. '3000 Lesbians Go To York' tells…
_description: To celebrate FilmPride's short film screenings in the Light Room across 22 - 24 May, we take over the theatre space with a bang! Please join us for a very exciting launch night party featuring some very special guests, musical entertainment, and free popcorn. With a screening of the documentary celebrating YLAF - the York Lesbian Arts Festival - featuring many famous faces: '3000 Lesbians Go To York'. '3000 Lesbians Go To York' tells the extraordinary true story of how a lesbian bookseller (who also happened to be trans) created the largest gathering of LGBTQ+ women in the UK; and how, from 1998 to 2008, the quietly conservative city of York became the unlikely centre of all things lesbian. For one thrilling decade, thousands of women flocked to the York Lesbian Arts Festival each autumn, to meet their favourite authors, buy books, hear top female artists live on stage and dance the night away. Please note, all our films are captioned. More details released soon!

_description: Eurydice. Presented by Pivotal Performance Company & Red Hot Theatre! Following last year’s 4-star hit of The Seagull, Pivotal Performance Company returns to Brighton Fringe, partnering with our partner emerging theatre company: Red Hot Theatre. This production marks Red Hot’s highly anticipated company debut, a milestone reached after a crowdfunding campaign fuelled by the desire to forge a career within the arts that is on their own terms. Forget the lyres and the robes. This is Eurydice through the lens of gig-theatre. Expect a high-octane blend of live music, visceral movement, and spoken word. We’re descending into the underworld not with a whimper, but with a bassline that thumps in your chest. In a world of fading memories and static, Eurydice must choose between the comfort of the shadows and the painful noise of the living.

_description: Hello there! So... do you fancy Ollie? It’s not a requirement to enjoy the show—but it certainly doesn’t hurt. Everybody's Dating Ollie is Ollie’s debut clown/comedy solo show: their ongoing experiment in dating an entire room of people at once. That’s right—sit back, relax, and enjoy as you all collectively go on a date with Ollie. Along the way, you’ll hear about their sexual orientation, relationships, and the all-too-familiar dating patterns we fall into and the relationship escalator that follows. Expect alternative comedy and clown-fuelled chaos, sprinkled with genuinely wholesome moments of connection. Note: There is a topless scene, but no nudity. Join this clown as they dive headfirst into the messy, joyful world of relationships—with you. Bursary Winner 2025: Katie Reddin-Clancy Solo Treasure Hunt.

title: Face It Till You Make It The Panic Years
company: Shouldn't Have Said That Theatre Company
summary: Face it till you make it - The Panic Years, created by Shouldn’t Have Said That Theatre Company. This bold, unfiltered show dives into the messy, hilarious, and painfully relatable reality of stumbling through your late twenties on the brink of thirty. Through sharp storytelling and brutally honest humour, it exposes the pressures of pretending to have life figured out while juggling careers, relationships, social lives, and the…
_description: Face it till you make it - The Panic Years, created by Shouldn’t Have Said That Theatre Company. This bold, unfiltered show dives into the messy, hilarious, and painfully relatable reality of stumbling through your late twenties on the brink of thirty. Through sharp storytelling and brutally honest humour, it exposes the pressures of pretending to have life figured out while juggling careers, relationships, social lives, and the relentless expectations of social media. From the awkward intimacy of smear tests to the clash between generations, and the chaos of adulthood no one warned us about, this show celebrates imperfection and speaks the truths we all think but rarely dare to say out loud. A love letter and a reality check for anyone learning to survive adulthood one questionable decision at a time. You can support Shouldn't Have Said that Theatre Company and keep up to date on future shows and all back stage shenanigans via their socials - Instagram: @shouldnthavesaidthattheatreco

_description: FOLKS. Theatre x BN1 Arts Centre Graduate Development Scheme 2026 Presents Fate Train Written and Directed by Max & Holly Hopkins Dawn, reeling from the death of their partner, spirals into isolation and makes a deal with fate. Hidden in the sounds of the city, Dawn refuses to fulfill their deal resulting in three strangers arriving on their door step. Split between, longing for the past, avoiding the present, and intimidated by the future, Dawn must unravel the mess to restore order. Fate Train, weaves Norse Gods and the modern day into a surreal tale where time, memory and self become blurred. Powerfully poetic, and unflinchingly human, it explores the myths we cling to when everything feels like it’s falling apart. Both intimate and epic, it invites audiences into a world where the divine and the human collide and asks who are we without the ones we have lost?

title: Forgive Me
summary: This project brings together award winning collaborators. Written and performed by Tamsin Shasha and directed by Helen Tennison with sound design by Matt Eaton, Forgive Me follows on from the critical success of double award winning, Everything I See I Swallow (Shasha & Taylor Productions) and similarly responds to the challenges and turbulence of the world today. Forgive Me is an intergenerational dialogue between a mother and her…
_description: This project brings together award winning collaborators. Written and performed by Tamsin Shasha and directed by Helen Tennison with sound design by Matt Eaton, Forgive Me follows on from the critical success of double award winning, Everything I See I Swallow (Shasha & Taylor Productions) and similarly responds to the challenges and turbulence of the world today. Forgive Me is an intergenerational dialogue between a mother and her autistic son, an avid gamer, and explores our individual and collective need to fight monsters. We follow the journey of a mother as she tries to understand the specialist needs of her child, while all the while navigating her own late ADHD diagnosis. Her son meanwhile navigates a digital world of online gaming, hyperfixating on YouTube and gaming. As the two worlds converge fantasy and reality become intertwine and mother and son discover that they need every weapon in their arsenal to fight an epic battle.

title: Flight One Mans Journey
summary: FLIGHT is a powerful 55-minute one-man show that brings 21 vivid characters to life through storytelling, physicality and voice. Written by Martin Lytton and directed by Jacquie Crago, the piece unfolds as a rapid-fire journey through memory, movement and transformation. Performed by Naz Sheikh, the show shifts between intimate moments and bold, fully realised characters, using minimal staging and direct audience address to create a…
_description: FLIGHT is a powerful 55-minute one-man show that brings 21 vivid characters to life through storytelling, physicality and voice. Written by Martin Lytton and directed by Jacquie Crago, the piece unfolds as a rapid-fire journey through memory, movement and transformation. Performed by Naz Sheikh, the show shifts between intimate moments and bold, fully realised characters, using minimal staging and direct audience address to create a strong connection with the audience. Each transformation is precise and immediate, revealing how identity is shaped, challenged and reclaimed. At its heart, FLIGHT explores themes of belonging, displacement and the search for self. The narrative is rich with imagery and momentum, inviting audiences into a world that is both personal and universal. Like all living theatre, the production continues to evolve through performance and response. FLIGHT is ideal for audiences who enjoy solo performance, contemporary storytelling and ask urgent questions.

_description: Are you a Fringe Performer, experienced or otherwise, who feels they could have a greater knowledge of tech? Join us at Komedia, in partnership with Brighton Fringe where two technicians with more than 30 years' combined experience in venues from 10 to 5000 capacity will walk you through every technical aspect of putting on a Fringe show. Discussion will include: • Basic stage sound: PAs, mixing desks, mics • Show lighting: different types of lighting, lighting desks, terminology, techniques available at different scales • Cues, scripts, playback devices, hiring equipment, assessing what's worth having, and operating technicians • Tech advancing: Knowing what to ask for, knowing how to answer questions, make sense of what's available, and fill out an advance form This workshop will be geared toward Fringe performers and crew. Attendees will receive a link upon reserving their place to register questions ahead of time. Subsidised by Brighton Fringe and Komedia Brighton

summary: Have you been asked for Public Liability certificate and thought “Do I really need this?” This session is for performers, producers, and touring artists and those new to Fringe. Fringe artists are often required to have insurance, but the reasons why (and what’s actually necessary) aren’t always clear. This session is designed to explain it simply. We’ll walk through: - Why venues ask for Public Liability and what it actually covers -…
_description: Have you been asked for Public Liability certificate and thought “Do I really need this?” This session is for performers, producers, and touring artists and those new to Fringe. Fringe artists are often required to have insurance, but the reasons why (and what’s actually necessary) aren’t always clear. This session is designed to explain it simply. We’ll walk through: - Why venues ask for Public Liability and what it actually covers - Why venue insurance doesn’t automatically include your show - When Employers’ Liability becomes relevant (even for small teams) - Other covers you may hear about equipment, cancellation, cyber and when they matter - Using a real-world Fringe-style case study, we’ll show how these situations play out in practice and what difference the right cover makes This is a practical, no-jargon session to help you how to get set up and understand what you do and don't need. There will be time for live Q&A, so you can ask questions specific to your show or setup.

title: From Nightingales To Butterflies English Cello And Piano Music
summary: Cellist, Esther Ward-Caddle and pianist, Evgenia Startseva present a recital of English chamber music inspired by nature, and the transformative healing power of family and legacy. Central to the programme are the cello sonatas of Frederick Delius and Frank Bridge (written, respectively, between 1916-1917 for the celebrated cellist, Beatrice Harrison, who was famed for her live recordings of nightingale and cello duets in the English…
_description: Cellist, Esther Ward-Caddle and pianist, Evgenia Startseva present a recital of English chamber music inspired by nature, and the transformative healing power of family and legacy. Central to the programme are the cello sonatas of Frederick Delius and Frank Bridge (written, respectively, between 1916-1917 for the celebrated cellist, Beatrice Harrison, who was famed for her live recordings of nightingale and cello duets in the English countryside). The programme also features Stephanie Cant’s 'Suite in Ancient Style', composed in 1995 (for Esther) and based on a piece by her daughter, Kate Cooper (who loved butterflies!), and the premiere of Peter Copley’s ‘Nocturnal’, composed in 2021 for his daughter, Maria’s Copley 18th birthday. Esther and Evgenia are honoured to present this event in support of Off the Fence, and in dedication of Kate’s passionate wish to support the homeless. There will be a retiring collection for donations at the end of the concert.

_description: Join Caz Slota from Spark Arts AM&PM and Tom Hooker from Lost Hippo to chat about how to best market your event for Brighton Fringe 2026. Caz began her career in arts marketing and management at The King’s Head Theatre in London before moving on to Norwich Playhouse, spending the next 10 years promoting performances of every possible size and shape, and running the venue as part of the senior management team. Caz mentors early-career arts marketers through the AMA mentoring scheme. Please copy and paste this link into your browser to join the event: https://us02web.zoom.us/j/7800653204?omn=85920504769

title: Funny Women The Glitter Project
summary: Returning to Brighton for a special festival edition of The Glitter Project, featuring an INDUSTRY PANEL with experts covering comedy production, performance, media, mental health and wellbeing. The panel will be hosted by Funny Women founder, Lynne Parker, as part of The Glitter Project – A Safe Place supported by funds raised from The Glitter Challenge. This takes place in partnership with The Komedia, home of Funny Women's longest…
_description: Returning to Brighton for a special festival edition of The Glitter Project, featuring an INDUSTRY PANEL with experts covering comedy production, performance, media, mental health and wellbeing. The panel will be hosted by Funny Women founder, Lynne Parker, as part of The Glitter Project – A Safe Place supported by funds raised from The Glitter Challenge. This takes place in partnership with The Komedia, home of Funny Women's longest running pro-gig in the UK. Funny Women has been supporting and developing new comedy talent at grassroots with ground-breaking Awards, workshops, courses and open mic events for over 20 years. The priority is to build confidence in female and non-binary performers, writers and creators to give them the opportunity to pursue comedy careers. The inspiration for The Glitter Project is Jo Fletcher-Cross, who was diagnosed with a grade 4 glioblastoma brain tumour in summer 2022 on the eve of her her Edinburgh debut.

title: Gone To Look For America
summary: Gone To Look For America is a vivid, funny and immersive journey of stories, songs and striking projected music videos exploring the wild soul of the American backroads. Join Steve Bonham and The Long Road as they delve into outlaw country, desert ghost towns, neon-soaked nights and the extraordinary lives of ordinary people. Drawn from months of trekking and travelling through the southern states, these tales reveal the grit, grace…
_description: Gone To Look For America is a vivid, funny and immersive journey of stories, songs and striking projected music videos exploring the wild soul of the American backroads. Join Steve Bonham and The Long Road as they delve into outlaw country, desert ghost towns, neon-soaked nights and the extraordinary lives of ordinary people. Drawn from months of trekking and travelling through the southern states, these tales reveal the grit, grace and contradictions of a country forever caught between dream and dust. Expect powerful acoustic music, big melodies and riffs, warm humour and storytelling that is both intimate and expansive. The projected music videos add atmosphere and colour, pulling the audience into the landscapes and characters that shaped the songs. At the heart of it all are Steve Bonham – the Vagabond Philosopher – and Chris “The Bishop” Lydon, pianist, composer and fellow wanderer. Two adventurers, many miles and one unforgettable trip into the mythic heart of America.

Geek out with the coolest comedians (who were all probably bullied at school). _description: Join the nerdiest comedians celebrating all nerd culture has to offer. From video games to table top games, LPs to larping, film franchises to fantasy novels, comic books to cosplay – we’ve got your fandom covered! Geek out with the coolest comedians (who were all probably bullied at school). Embrace your geekside, unleash your inner nerd and quite possibly be crowned the biggest geek in the room - the highest accolade we could possibly bestow and your chance to win prizes! Compete with the comedians alongside your fellow audience as our two teams battle it out to determine who knows the most useless information - the audience or the comedians. Find your tribe as you delight in the dorkiest of dweebs and the funniest fanatics. Join your host and uber-nerd Juliette Burton, with her geekiest friends for a fun-filled fanatical comedy hour!

summary: A sharp and heartfelt stand-up show about sexual identity, therapy, dating disasters, and the messy search for happiness, told through the eyes of a gay man just trying to survive with, well, good vibes.

_description: The Silver Starlets return to the Rotunda after last year's sell-out run, as they take you on a musical journey of their favourite movies throughout the decades! Glenda & Rita are two actresses from the Golden Era of cinema, immortalised in black & white and struggling to find work in this brutal, unforgiving technicolour world. They will take you on a journey, as they sing the songs from more of their favourite movies throughout the years, from 'A Star Is Born' to 'Spice World', and 'James Bond' to ''Priscilla Queen of the Desert'. From the award-winning musical comedy duo that brought you 'A History of Horror', 'Glenda & Rita: Live' and 'I'm Dreaming of a Black & White Christmas', this musical soirée is not to be missed!

_description: For the fifth year running, legendary poetry slam, Hammer & Tongue Brighton, bring their epic grand final to Brighton Open Air Theatre! Finalists will come equipped with their unique styles and perspectives to battle it out for the crown. Lucky audience members will hold up scores out of ten to decide the fate our our brave poets. It’s sure to be an epic clash of words, syntax and rhyme like no other! Featuring a headline performance from one of the UK's biggest spoken word artists and hosted by the hilarious Leanne Shorley, join us for the best in performance poetry from Brighton’s longest-running poetry night. Doors open at 6pm.

This testament is a nakedly honest, funny and poignant story of one man’s journey through drugs, sex, abuse and self-acceptance. _description: Growing up in 1980’s rural Wiltshire requires more than a little patience, especially when you're gay, trying to be a good Christian, with a love for George Michael. This testament is a nakedly honest, funny and poignant story of one man’s journey through drugs, sex, abuse and self-acceptance. It’s a heartbreakingly familiar story to anyone who knows that rock bottom is only a Careless Whisper away. The show has already had a successful run at the Etcetera Theatre end of April 2024 where it had an almost sell out 3 day run. It was also performed at the Watford Fringe 2024.

With live music including Hans Zimmer’s film scores for Batman and The Da Vinci Code; John Williams’ Jurassic Park, Jaws, Star Wars, Harry Potter and Schindler’s List; Ennio Morricone’s Cinema Paradiso and Gabriel’s Oboe and Joe Hisashi’s Spirited Away and Howl’s…
_description: Inspired by the film Interstellar (2014), our show time-warps cinema history, intersplicing modern film music with silent cinematography as we travel through cinema history. With live music including Hans Zimmer’s film scores for Batman and The Da Vinci Code; John Williams’ Jurassic Park, Jaws, Star Wars, Harry Potter and Schindler’s List; Ennio Morricone’s Cinema Paradiso and Gabriel’s Oboe and Joe Hisashi’s Spirited Away and Howl’s Moving Castle. Includes digital cameos from Hollywood icon Charlie Chaplin. 'Enlightening' ★★★★★ (EdinburghGuide.com), 'An atmosphere which is all too often missed in our digital world' ★★★★★ (CorrBlimey.com). 'Masters of their crafts' ★★★★★(BingeFringe.com). Director: Matthew Shiel is an award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera.

_description: GREEK MYTHOLOGY IN 69 MINUTES Interactive Musical Comedy by Tenorman WHY WATCH IT: “Rock, Pop, Jazz, Opera collide in this offbeat act.” Time Out London, UK. A show with outrageous humor. The audience isn't just watching – they're participating, taking on roles! Featuring multilingual songs, surreal storytelling, and the irrepressible stage presence of Tenorman, Greek Mythology in 69 Minutes is an original production that turns the classic myths into an accessible and incredibly fun adventure for everyone. THERE IS NOTHING MORE SURREAL, TRAGIC AND FUNNY THAN GREEK MYTHOLOGY. THE STORY: The world is in peril from the stupidity of some mortals and a gang of legendary heroes forms to save it, guided by the god Dionysus – who’s disguised as a mortal named Mr. Greek! A thrilling, chaotic, and unforgettable experience where the audience becomes the hero. Meet Medusa, Odysseus, Zeus, Hercules, Medea, and Hades! Written and performed by the awarded international performer Tenorman.

Now, he can’t sleep, he can’t get published, and... he can’t get laid. At the sleep-study clinic that he’s enrolled at, they observe him talking in his sleep, seemingly an intermediary for a long-forgotten Pompeian…
_description: Professor Nathan Zangari is fading away. Once voted the sexiest professor on BBC2’s Open University, his highly celebrated archeo-linguistics paper on “Graffiti, Women and the Walls of Pompeii” is now but a distant memory. Now, he can’t sleep, he can’t get published, and... he can’t get laid. At the sleep-study clinic that he’s enrolled at, they observe him talking in his sleep, seemingly an intermediary for a long-forgotten Pompeian girl. When overambitious student, Callie, offers to capture his mumblings, they both find themselves sucked into the very real lives of those on the edge of destruction, some 2000 years ago. Best Newcomer (Nom) The Offies 2024 Winner, International Fringe Encore Series 2022 Best New Play, New Writing South 2012 Best New Play(Shortlist) NWS 2013 Bruntwood Prize (Longlisting) 2019 Best Male Performer, Btn Fringe 2013 Best Female Performer, Btn Fringe 2014 Best Female Performer (Nom), Btn Fringe 2013 Best Male Performer (Nom), Btn Fringe 2007

But how did we get there? A place where facts are relative, hate is currency, and consent is for cookies. Investigative journalism's been reduced to people trawling the net to help themselves to whatever’s trending, a stolen showcase to help boost outrage and…
_description: Journalism was a witness to history, a byword for truth, diligence and justice. Now it’s gawping a post-truth, clickbait car crash. Impish knows, because that's her background. But how did we get there? A place where facts are relative, hate is currency, and consent is for cookies. Investigative journalism's been reduced to people trawling the net to help themselves to whatever’s trending, a stolen showcase to help boost outrage and traffic. The race to the bottom is a sprint. We’re all masters of misinformation – left reeling from the personal spin on personal branding. A true twenty first century tale of good vs evil. Find out how it ends.

summary: Hereditary is a riotous deep dive into the contradictions of Irish identity, told through the lens of family, booze, sex, and a misguided stint in the army. From the generational curse of alcoholism to the uniquely Irish knack for being both sexually repressed and wildly promiscuous, no taboo is safe. Just when it seems it can’t get more absurd, along comes the paradox of a peace-loving hippy in the Irish Army. With razor-sharp wit and…
_description: Hereditary is a riotous deep dive into the contradictions of Irish identity, told through the lens of family, booze, sex, and a misguided stint in the army. From the generational curse of alcoholism to the uniquely Irish knack for being both sexually repressed and wildly promiscuous, no taboo is safe. Just when it seems it can’t get more absurd, along comes the paradox of a peace-loving hippy in the Irish Army. With razor-sharp wit and heartfelt chaos, Hereditary explores the things we inherit, the battles we fight—literal and metaphorical—and the joy of embracing the contradictions that shape who we are.

title: Holy Shift
summary: Holy Shift is a comedic, time-travelling dive back into the gloriously chaotic 90s, following a mum who suddenly finds herself raising a teenager whose idea of rebellion is... becoming a saint. Sin, she gets. Skipping school, sneaking out, questionable fashion choices-she's been there, done that, still owns the mixtapes. But a holy uprising? This, she did not see coming. Desperate to decode her daughter's devotion, she cracks open her…
_description: Holy Shift is a comedic, time-travelling dive back into the gloriously chaotic 90s, following a mum who suddenly finds herself raising a teenager whose idea of rebellion is... becoming a saint. Sin, she gets. Skipping school, sneaking out, questionable fashion choices-she's been there, done that, still owns the mixtapes. But a holy uprising? This, she did not see coming. Desperate to decode her daughter's devotion, she cracks open her old teenage diary stuffed with heartbreak, hope, and the kind of melodrama only the 90s could produce, while blasting out the Madonna anthems that once saved her teenage soul - preaching empowerment, reinvention, and the sacred art of surviving adolescence with her eyeliner intact. Through the music, memories, and a few emotional meltdowns, she begins to see her own youth more clearly than ever. And maybe starts to understand her daughter, too.

title: How To Survive And Thrive In An Impossible World With A Piano
summary: Mischievous, subversive, fun and fizzing with wisdom, “How to Survive and Thrive in an Impossible World – with a piano!” invites you to step back, laugh at a world that is quite clearly bonkers, and discover a better way to navigate it all. Award-winning psychologist and songwriter Steve Bonham teams up with master composer-musician Chris “The Bishop” Lydon for a highly original mix of humour, stories, songs and gentle audience…
_description: Mischievous, subversive, fun and fizzing with wisdom, “How to Survive and Thrive in an Impossible World – with a piano!” invites you to step back, laugh at a world that is quite clearly bonkers, and discover a better way to navigate it all. Award-winning psychologist and songwriter Steve Bonham teams up with master composer-musician Chris “The Bishop” Lydon for a highly original mix of humour, stories, songs and gentle audience participation. Part uplifting wellbeing session, part musical adventure, the show explores the “Five Rules of the Road” for living with more resilience, joy and freedom. Expect laughter, toe-tapping tunes, and ideas that sneak up on you - in the best possible way! It’s warm, witty, and just a little bit wild: a chance to liberate your happiness and wellbeing, one chuckle at a time. If you’re ready to rethink, reset and rediscover what makes life worth the ride, don’t miss this unforgettable experience.

title: In Conversation With Duncan Lustig Prean
summary: An informal conversation with much humour and the chance for the audience to ask questions. Join renowned former BBC Radio 4 presenter, Nigel Wrench in conversation with Duncan Lustig-Prean. He led the campaign for LGBT people to serve in the Armed Forces and subsequently changed the law on two further occasions. He investigated racism in the Armed Forces and conceived the idea of women having full career opportunities in the forces…
_description: An informal conversation with much humour and the chance for the audience to ask questions. Join renowned former BBC Radio 4 presenter, Nigel Wrench in conversation with Duncan Lustig-Prean. He led the campaign for LGBT people to serve in the Armed Forces and subsequently changed the law on two further occasions. He investigated racism in the Armed Forces and conceived the idea of women having full career opportunities in the forces and helped the fight to restore their medals. He ensured that the global majority was better represented by English Heritage and runs a charity supporting LGBT veterans. Somehow he finds the time to chair Brighton Fringe. From opera singer to becoming "one of the most influential naval officers of the last 50 years; he did not just change the law, he changed the culture of the United Kingdom's armed forces." (First Sea Lord) The proceeds of this event are donated to the charity, Brighton Fringe.

title: In The Air
summary: Chloe's mom has passed. Her mother has left behind a box of old pictures for her to sort through. As she sees the younger version of her mother and wonders what she was like... she meets Violet. Violet is put together and organized and ambitious... the very opposite of Chloe. But the two of them slowly realize that they have more in common than they think. Told from their adjacent balconies, "In the Air" is a story about friendship,…
_description: Chloe's mom has passed. Her mother has left behind a box of old pictures for her to sort through. As she sees the younger version of her mother and wonders what she was like... she meets Violet. Violet is put together and organized and ambitious... the very opposite of Chloe. But the two of them slowly realize that they have more in common than they think. Told from their adjacent balconies, "In the Air" is a story about friendship, fate, and how looking back can help with moving on. Both actors are prepared to play either role. There will be a coin toss before each performance to determine the casting of the show. Seen at Stratford East's Freelance Royalty Scratch and Sophie's Barn (Patron: Dame Judy Dench). Supported by Theatre Deli & New Diorama Theatre. Fundraising for The Maya Centre, a women-only charity in the heart of Islington, who believe that no one should face trauma or hardship alone.

title: Jo Jarvie Crazy Stupid Funny
summary: Jo Jarvie is an award-winning comedian based in Belfast, Northern Ireland. After a 12-year hiatus, Jo returned to the comedy scene with a vengeance, crafting hilarious anecdotes that resonate with audiences. She was the first-ever English person to place in Ireland’s history at “Show Me The Funny”, (proving that she’s just too funny to ignore!). A runner-up in the competition, Jo is known for her high-spirited performances &…
_description: Jo Jarvie is an award-winning comedian based in Belfast, Northern Ireland. After a 12-year hiatus, Jo returned to the comedy scene with a vengeance, crafting hilarious anecdotes that resonate with audiences. She was the first-ever English person to place in Ireland’s history at “Show Me The Funny”, (proving that she’s just too funny to ignore!). A runner-up in the competition, Jo is known for her high-spirited performances & unapologetically dirty jokes. A graduate of the Guildhall School of Music & Drama and a devoted West Ham United fan, Jo brings a unique blend of theatrical flair and football fanaticism to her comedy. Her fearless approach to stand-up has earned her accolades and a growing fan base. Come on the journey of what you learn when it all goes wrong! “Watch out for Jo Jarvie, she’s funny, fearless and funny as f**k”- Dapper Laughs “Jo is a great act with a great stage presence and delivers hilarious material”- Phil Chapman “Cockney charm with Belfast bite”- Paddy Raff

title: Kidz Silent Disco Jam
summary: Kidz Silent Disco Jam! Perfect for kids who love to dance, have fun, and be themselves! This is not your average silent disco - it's a full-on interactive and entertaining experience that invites kids to step into the spotlight and let loose as they join a fun-filled silent disco party. Rocking silent disco headphones, the kids have a blast and feel like the star of the show! Watch as they come together to show off their moves,…
_description: Kidz Silent Disco Jam! Perfect for kids who love to dance, have fun, and be themselves! This is not your average silent disco - it's a full-on interactive and entertaining experience that invites kids to step into the spotlight and let loose as they join a fun-filled silent disco party. Rocking silent disco headphones, the kids have a blast and feel like the star of the show! Watch as they come together to show off their moves, unleashing their inner stars and expressing themselves through dance. This is all about confidence, creativity, and pure fun! Led by Charli Ohlala, the fun happens inside the headphones as she entertains, plays games, sparks dance-offs, and gets everyone involved - yes even the Mums and Dads, if they are up to it! We'll have glitter and face paints waiting, and parents can jump in to help their kids get party-ready - it's their time to shine! At least 1 adult ticket must be purchased per booking. All attendees must have tickets.

title: Kinder
summary: The Brighton Fringe Excellence award-nominated and List's Best LGBTQIA+ Edinburgh Fringe Show award-winner makes its Brighton Fringe premiere! After an unexpected call derails a performance planned by newly arrived drag artist Goody Prostate, booked to headline the local "reading hour", they are forced to pull their act together in real time and get ready to face a crowd of unruly children and their parents. But as they race against…
_description: The Brighton Fringe Excellence award-nominated and List's Best LGBTQIA+ Edinburgh Fringe Show award-winner makes its Brighton Fringe premiere! After an unexpected call derails a performance planned by newly arrived drag artist Goody Prostate, booked to headline the local "reading hour", they are forced to pull their act together in real time and get ready to face a crowd of unruly children and their parents. But as they race against the clock to pull off a new number in time, they spiral into a chaotic interrogation of childhood, memory, and a long overdue examination of what it means to "grow up". The library is open - sit down, be quiet, and listen. Because sometimes, reading is what? Fundamentalist.

title: Liberace And Liza
summary: Liberace & Liza Minnelli never performed together, but that didn’t stop David Saffert & Jillian Snow from creating this hysterical and wildly successful mashup of two over-the-top icons. Saffert’s dizzying piano skills and Snow’s powerhouse belt team up in Cabaret, New York, New York, Over the Rainbow to surprise covers by Lady Gaga and Britney Spears. There are as many laughs as there are sequins when their Liberace & Liza take…
_description: Liberace & Liza Minnelli never performed together, but that didn’t stop David Saffert & Jillian Snow from creating this hysterical and wildly successful mashup of two over-the-top icons. Saffert’s dizzying piano skills and Snow’s powerhouse belt team up in Cabaret, New York, New York, Over the Rainbow to surprise covers by Lady Gaga and Britney Spears. There are as many laughs as there are sequins when their Liberace & Liza take hilarious (and sometimes mischievous) turns vying for the spotlight. No matter what, they both come out winning and audiences are left in awe, humming and quoting a night they’ll not soon forget.

title: Krapps Last Tape
company: Grist to the Mill Productions Ltd
summary: Every year, on his birthday, Krapp records a tape. This year it is his 69th birthday and he is recording his last tape. As he does he finds himself listening back to one he recorded when he was a much younger man. This one act play is one of Samuel Beckett's greatest works. A witty and compelling reflection of the life of a man and the hopes that he once had. From the team behind Edfringe Sell-Out "The Unknown Soldier" Grist to the…
_description: Every year, on his birthday, Krapp records a tape. This year it is his 69th birthday and he is recording his last tape. As he does he finds himself listening back to one he recorded when he was a much younger man. This one act play is one of Samuel Beckett's greatest works. A witty and compelling reflection of the life of a man and the hopes that he once had. From the team behind Edfringe Sell-Out "The Unknown Soldier" Grist to the Mill. Performed by Award-winning actor and writer Ross Ericson.

title: Laugh To The Death
summary: 8 of the brightest and best from the Brighton comedy scene battle it out over 4 rounds. Who will survive? The audience decides! So come and take part! Includes a live musical interlude from one of Brighton’s most exciting up and coming singers - Poppy Hawkes!

title: Like Lovers Do
summary: Like Lovers Do – An Acoustic Celebration of Annie Lennox and Eurythmics is a bold, stripped-back take on one of pop’s most iconic catalogues. Award-winning performer Paul Diello and his band rework classic Eurythmics and Annie Lennox songs into powerful acoustic arrangements. Driven by rich three-part harmonies, the show brings a fresh edge to familiar favourites, revealing new textures, emotion, and intensity beneath the originals.…
_description: Like Lovers Do – An Acoustic Celebration of Annie Lennox and Eurythmics is a bold, stripped-back take on one of pop’s most iconic catalogues. Award-winning performer Paul Diello and his band rework classic Eurythmics and Annie Lennox songs into powerful acoustic arrangements. Driven by rich three-part harmonies, the show brings a fresh edge to familiar favourites, revealing new textures, emotion, and intensity beneath the originals. Focused, dynamic, and musically sharp, Like Lovers Do honours the spirit of Eurythmics while giving the songs space to breathe, surprise, and hit hard in a live setting.

title: Line Dance Extravaganza

This social Line Dancing party is taking festivals by storm! The best part? No partner, no experience and no pressure! The moves are simple, the music is infectious, its fun, social and a great night out! Laughter, and great times on the dance floor! Our easy-to-…
_description: Line Dance Extravaganza: The Ultimate Social Dance Experience! Simple steps. Big energy. One unforgettable party! Get ready to kick up your heels and hit the dance floor! This social Line Dancing party is taking festivals by storm! The best part? No partner, no experience and no pressure! The moves are simple, the music is infectious, its fun, social and a great night out! Laughter, and great times on the dance floor! Our easy-to-follow dances are perfect for everyone, whether it's your first time or you've danced before. It’s the ideal way to enjoy a social, lively night out with friends, meet new people, and experience the joy of country line dancing. So grab your cowboy boots, we can’t wait to get the party started and make this an unforgettable experience! Bring a hat or buy one at the event - we even have a cowboy hat station where you can get glitzed and glammed – because what’s a country party without a little sparkle?

title: Maddie Hw Gaylord
summary: GAYLORD is the electric UK debut from award-winning Australian comedian Maddie HW, fresh off sold-out tours across Australia and rapidly making her mark on the London comedy circuit. Known for her high-octane energy and inventive style, Maddie blends stand-up and physical comedy into a fast-paced hour about moving countries, navigating relationships, and making fun of those silly British accents (sorry). Described as “charming and…
_description: GAYLORD is the electric UK debut from award-winning Australian comedian Maddie HW, fresh off sold-out tours across Australia and rapidly making her mark on the London comedy circuit. Known for her high-octane energy and inventive style, Maddie blends stand-up and physical comedy into a fast-paced hour about moving countries, navigating relationships, and making fun of those silly British accents (sorry). Described as “charming and vivacious” (Festmag) , Maddie takes the everyday and flips it into something surreal, bold, and hilarious.

title: Locomotive For Murder The Improvised Whodunnit
summary: Welcome aboard Locomotive for Murder: The Improvised Whodunnit, the totally improvised, totally hilarious murder mystery, where killing the cast and cracking the case is in your hands! Offie-nominated Edinburgh Fringe favourites, Pinch Punch, are bringing their five-star, sell-out show back to Brighton Fringe for a fifth year running! So, don your deerstalkers and oil your moustaches for a night of shaky alibis, silver-tongued sleuths,…
_description: Welcome aboard Locomotive for Murder: The Improvised Whodunnit, the totally improvised, totally hilarious murder mystery, where killing the cast and cracking the case is in your hands! Offie-nominated Edinburgh Fringe favourites, Pinch Punch, are bringing their five-star, sell-out show back to Brighton Fringe for a fifth year running! So, don your deerstalkers and oil your moustaches for a night of shaky alibis, silver-tongued sleuths, thrills, spills, and good old-fashioned kills. Four characters board a train, but not everyone will survive... Luckily, a world-famous detective is on board, ready to root out the killer. But who is the murderer? Only one person knows—the murderer themselves! Can you put your wits to the test and solve the case? Using real-life audience stories and suggestions, watch Pinch Punch create a unique, one-of-a-kind, never-to-be-repeated whodunnit. If you love comedy or murder mysteries, then this is the show for you!

title: Lost To Life
summary: Lost to Life is a theatrical improv show about regular people who are just trying to cope with the situations they find themselves in. title: Mandy Sweats Presents The Hole Truth
summary: After a two sold-shows back in October, the papier-mâché menace Mandy Sweats brings their debut theatrical show back for Brighton Fringe. In ‘The Hole Truth’, hysteria mounts as a horrifying figure haunts the toilets and plug holes across a ruthless city. The figure in question is Mandy – and trapped in their little hotel room, you have no choice but to watch them re-enact the shocking, perverse, and bizarre things they have seen.…
_description: After a two sold-shows back in October, the papier-mâché menace Mandy Sweats brings their debut theatrical show back for Brighton Fringe. In ‘The Hole Truth’, hysteria mounts as a horrifying figure haunts the toilets and plug holes across a ruthless city. The figure in question is Mandy – and trapped in their little hotel room, you have no choice but to watch them re-enact the shocking, perverse, and bizarre things they have seen. Expect murderous babies, singing dogs, bloody crime scenes and one very erotic piece of steak. A blend of surreal horror and sketch comedy in the vein of David Firth and The League of Gentlemen, Mandy Sweats Presents: The Hole Truth is a brand new breed of theatrical terror.

title: Mark Row Every Days A School Day
summary: They say that every day's a school day, which is never more true than when you're a teacher stuck in the classroom with no obvious means of escape. Fortunately, Mark Row has discovered that it's not just the kids that can learn something valuable, as he reflects on the lessons he's learned along the way that have kept him out of trouble! Whether it's the good advice he was given by the wise people in his life, or the mistakes that he…
_description: They say that every day's a school day, which is never more true than when you're a teacher stuck in the classroom with no obvious means of escape. Fortunately, Mark Row has discovered that it's not just the kids that can learn something valuable, as he reflects on the lessons he's learned along the way that have kept him out of trouble! Whether it's the good advice he was given by the wise people in his life, or the mistakes that he has realised never should be repeated, there is one thing that always rings true - if you're not learning, you're laughing! Razor sharp, totally original. Had the whole room howling from start to finish. Comedy that speaks to everyone! - Chuckl

Follow Detective Fred Herring and the eccentric Day family as their perfectly pleasant evening spirals into chaos when someone is (GASP!) MURDERED! Described as “Fawlty Towers meets Agatha Christie meets the game Cluedo” (Brighton on the Inside) and “a hilarious, fast-paced comedy that will have you howling with laughter!” ★★★★★ (Reviewing Brighton) - this is a murder mystery seriously off the rails! It would be a crime to miss it!

_description: Join the Brighton Ki Society for an afternoon of Free mindful Ki and Aikido taster sessions with UK Chief Instructor Bob MacFarland Rokyudan, Full Ki Lecturer(Awarded Ki Society HQ, Japan). Please arrive by 2:15 pm, wearing loose, comfortable clothing such as a tracksuit or gi, for the start of the Shin Shin Toitsu Do (Japanese yoga) session at 2:30 pm, where you will learn the unification of mind and body, natural movement, meditation, and breathing. At 3:30 pm, join the SST Aikido workshop to apply Ki principles for harmonious self-defence, health, and self-development. From 4:30 pm to 5pm, there will be a Ki and Aikido demonstration and a Q&A session. For further information telephone Bob on 07769588938.
