A hysterical historical mash-up where Kings clash, therapy endures, and laughter reigns supreme. The Monarchs are not amused. Marie is moping, Mansa Musa’s in a mood, and Tutankhamun has tottered off back to his mummy. Worse still, someone has leaked their secrets to the press! Can our long-suffering therapist, Dr. Thompson,…
_description: What happens when you force Henry VIII, Marie Antoinette, and history's most chaotic royals into group therapy? A hysterical historical mash-up where Kings clash, therapy endures, and laughter reigns supreme. The Monarchs are not amused. Marie is moping, Mansa Musa’s in a mood, and Tutankhamun has tottered off back to his mummy. Worse still, someone has leaked their secrets to the press! Can our long-suffering therapist, Dr. Thompson, stop the session from descending into chaos? Pull up a throne and come face-to-face with the biggest egos of the last 600 years. They are unfit to rule, unwilling to change, and quite frankly, a right royal pain in the past. Audiences are raving: “A riotous hoot! Like a history class with your favourite comedians!” “Hilarious, emotive, thought-provoking – a history lover's dream!” “Funny, original and plain bonkers!” Join us for a royal appointment you won’t forget. No time machine required! Note: the show will run for 90 mins without an interval

title: Mr Sleepybums Dream Machine
summary: Winner Best Kids Show – Leicester Comedy Festival/UK Kids Comedy Festival 2025 Mr Sleepybum is sad. He can’t dream. To solve this problem, he invents a machine that magically brings dreams to life, featuring dinosaurs, robots, car chases, the world's largest blanket, and much more! This award-winning interactive show is filled with sketches, slapstick, and silliness, ensuring maximum fun for the whole family. Mr Sleepybum is a…
_description: Winner Best Kids Show – Leicester Comedy Festival/UK Kids Comedy Festival 2025 Mr Sleepybum is sad. He can’t dream. To solve this problem, he invents a machine that magically brings dreams to life, featuring dinosaurs, robots, car chases, the world's largest blanket, and much more! This award-winning interactive show is filled with sketches, slapstick, and silliness, ensuring maximum fun for the whole family. Mr Sleepybum is a critically acclaimed kids comedy show created by character comedian Jody Kamali. Mr Sleepybum burst onto the scene in 2023 with a sell-out run at the Edinburgh Fringe. He has since toured the UK, appearing at theatres and festivals, which included a 12-show off-West End run. The Dream Machine is the much-awaited sequel show.

Mrs Smith was an ordinary teacher in an ordinary school until she went viral on Facebook and accidentally became a stand-up comedian. 1,2,3, Eyes on Me! is the follow up to her sellout tour of 2023 that debuted at the Edinburgh Fringe.

(Helen & Linda may, or may not, be played by two gay men in drag!) Winning Best Comedy at Manchester Fringe 2024. Natter is written by, and stars, award-winning artist, Joe Henry-Evans (Best Newcomer, Manchester Fringe 2021) alongside John Thacker, and directed by Qweerdog's artistic director, Stewart Campbell (Jump, Brighton Fringe 2024). Natter was also nominated for the Write for the Stage Award, Canal St LGBTQ+ Award, as well as the Audience Choice Award at GM Fringe. Join us to find out if Helen is having it off with the vicar; just what links the Didsbury mafia with Linda's gay son; and what exactly does Helen have against poor old Cilla Black!

summary: Welcome to Murder!, Brighton’s live Traitors-style experience. Take part in a game of deception, deduction, and betrayal, where nothing is as it seems and trust is fragile. The goal is simple: survive. But it won’t be easy. Some players are Traitors, secretly murdering the Innocents, while the Innocents must uncover the hidden Traitors and stop them before it’s too late. Strangers can quickly become close allies, and your friends might…
_description: Welcome to Murder!, Brighton’s live Traitors-style experience. Take part in a game of deception, deduction, and betrayal, where nothing is as it seems and trust is fragile. The goal is simple: survive. But it won’t be easy. Some players are Traitors, secretly murdering the Innocents, while the Innocents must uncover the hidden Traitors and stop them before it’s too late. Strangers can quickly become close allies, and your friends might turn out to be your worst enemies. Along the way you’ll complete devious challenges, uncover clues to the Traitors’ identities, and fight to stay alive as the murders unfold - all while enjoying free popcorn! You’ll take part in tense debates at the round table to vote off players you suspect are Traitors. Those who remain at the end will be up for winning our grand prize! Could you get away with murdering your friends? Think you’re the next great detective? Come play Murder! WITH FREE POPCORN FOR ALL PLAYERS!

title: Musica Moderna Monteverdi Moving Fast And Breaking Things
summary: Founded in 1971, Brighton Consort is a local a cappella choir of around 20 singers. We specialise in 'Early Music' (1400s–1600s), with a large focus on the Renaissance period. With charitable status, we aim to make the music of the past accessible to all, performing several concert programmes each year and hosting public workshops. The choir is led by Greg Skidmore, a renowned expert in Renaissance music. This is our first time at…
_description: Founded in 1971, Brighton Consort is a local a cappella choir of around 20 singers. We specialise in 'Early Music' (1400s–1600s), with a large focus on the Renaissance period. With charitable status, we aim to make the music of the past accessible to all, performing several concert programmes each year and hosting public workshops. The choir is led by Greg Skidmore, a renowned expert in Renaissance music. This is our first time at Brighton Fringe! We'll be bringing to you the works of Italian composer Claudio Monteverdi. At the turn of the seventeenth century, Monteverdi challenged the strict conventions of Renaissance choral music, and embracing dramatic, text-driven expression. In this programme, Brighton Consort traces the creative tension between tradition and innovation, exploring how Monteverdi’s revolutionary approach reshaped music and paved the way for the Baroque era.

title: Nadav Tabak Live Organic Trance
summary: Experience the electrifying world of Nadav Tabak, the guitarist and composer who first made waves with Opal Ocean and now unleashes a solo show bursting with energy, emotion and pure musical wizardry. Armed with an array of instruments, a looping pedal and fearless creativity, Nadav blends acoustic organic trance, tribal rhythms, exotic melodies and fiery Spanish-rumba techniques into a genre-bending sonic journey. Every performance is…
_description: Experience the electrifying world of Nadav Tabak, the guitarist and composer who first made waves with Opal Ocean and now unleashes a solo show bursting with energy, emotion and pure musical wizardry. Armed with an array of instruments, a looping pedal and fearless creativity, Nadav blends acoustic organic trance, tribal rhythms, exotic melodies and fiery Spanish-rumba techniques into a genre-bending sonic journey. Every performance is improvised, immersive and intensely personal, a hypnotic soundscape that evolves in real time. He has captivated crowds at festivals worldwide and shared stages with icons like Manu Chao, Hilight Tribe and many more Get ready for a performance that’s explosive, intimate and unlike anything you’ve heard.

title: Nocturne Musical
venue_name: The Dance Space - The Jamie Watton Creation Space
summary: East of the sun and West of the moon comes a tale of finding your courage and staying true to yourself. The musical is rooted in Norwegian folklore and folk music, filled with puppetry and beautiful set design, ready to transport you into the fairy-tale forest. Join our protagonist…
_description: East of the sun and West of the moon comes a tale of finding your courage and staying true to yourself. Nocturne is a new original musical created by composer and lyricist Hedda Rustad Carlsen, writer and art director Hannah Debansi and writer Leah Sperring. The musical is rooted in Norwegian folklore and folk music, filled with puppetry and beautiful set design, ready to transport you into the fairy-tale forest. Join our protagonist Solveig as she delves into the forest of ancient fairy tales, encountering familiar characters and dark mysteries. We invite you to get lost in the magical worlds and fables we often forget as we grow older. ★★★★★ "Every song had a lyric that sent shivers down the spine and unexpected comedy moments that entertained and delighted all who saw it. " - Theatre in Brighton (2025) ★★★★★ "If I had a magic flute of my own, I’d whistle up a ninety-minute version and a transfer to the West End." - The Wee Review (Edinburgh Fringe 2025)

title: Narrative Songwriting Workshop Friends Families And Communities
summary: We are all exposed to a myriad of anecdotes and stories – overheard at bus stops, told at the dinner table, past on through families and communities. These can be a rich mine of material to help us explore feelings, motivations, relationships. Stories of specific people can be a way in for others to reflect on their own experiences – love stories, overcoming adversity and tales of adventure. In this workshop we will look at turning…
_description: We are all exposed to a myriad of anecdotes and stories – overheard at bus stops, told at the dinner table, past on through families and communities. These can be a rich mine of material to help us explore feelings, motivations, relationships. Stories of specific people can be a way in for others to reflect on their own experiences – love stories, overcoming adversity and tales of adventure. In this workshop we will look at turning these stories into songs. How do we keep an eye out for anecdotes and make sure we capture then? What are some of the conventions of folk, rock and pop songs? How do we distil some of the ideas that come out, making them into ear catching hooks and choruses? Phil Jones is an experienced workshop facilitator and the writer behind Fringe production, “Snow Along the Line”, a multimedia show about the life of a woman born in 1928 and the life and times she lived through. Bring pads, instruments and any photographs.

title: Neddy Goes To Glasto
summary: Neddy Goes to Glasto is a high-voltage, one-woman rollercoaster through grief, sisterhood, and the magic of losing — and maybe finding — yourself in a field of strangers. Thirty years after her first Glastonbury, Neddy returns to celebrate her sister’s 50th birthday. The only problem? Her sister's dead. Determined to outrun her grief for one wild weekend, Neddy dives headfirst into a mud-soaked world of chaos, connection, and unlikely…
_description: Neddy Goes to Glasto is a high-voltage, one-woman rollercoaster through grief, sisterhood, and the magic of losing — and maybe finding — yourself in a field of strangers. Thirty years after her first Glastonbury, Neddy returns to celebrate her sister’s 50th birthday. The only problem? Her sister's dead. Determined to outrun her grief for one wild weekend, Neddy dives headfirst into a mud-soaked world of chaos, connection, and unlikely companions. And in the beating heart of Shangri-La, she stumbles across what might just be her salvation... Fierce, funny and gloriously unfiltered - Neddy Goes to Glasto is a riotous sprint through the festival of dreams.

Join Nerine in her second work in progress as she asks for your help to decide what's in her show. Stand up, songs and character comedy. This could change! Nerine's been featured on SKY News, BBC Radio 2,4,5, Richard Herring's RHLSTP, Laura Kuenssberg and Britain's Got Talent (The Dots) and was recently shortlisted as a writer for SNL UK. "A tumbling ball of invention and spontaneity" Jon Culshaw

title: Niusia
summary: Following a sell-out, Scotsman Fringe First award-winning Edinburgh premiere, the debut work from Filipa Bragança Award​​-nominated artist Beth Paterson makes its Brighton Fringe debut! Niusia was a Holocaust survivor. She saved people’s lives in the camps, set up a new life for her family as a refugee, and was an iridescent entertainer. But her granddaughter, Beth, only remembers an angry, dying woman. She's ready to learn her…
_description: Following a sell-out, Scotsman Fringe First award-winning Edinburgh premiere, the debut work from Filipa Bragança Award​​-nominated artist Beth Paterson makes its Brighton Fringe debut! Niusia was a Holocaust survivor. She saved people’s lives in the camps, set up a new life for her family as a refugee, and was an iridescent entertainer. But her granddaughter, Beth, only remembers an angry, dying woman. She's ready to learn her stories, but what she discovers is all the questions she didn't know existed (and wasn't allowed to ask). Through NIUSIA, Beth weaves together memories, handed-down stories and interviews to examine the precarity of identity and the haphazard cultural legacy second, third and fourth generation immigrants are handed. She asks: what does remembrance look like when all I remember is the space where questions should go?

_description: Fancy dancing to some classic indie like you did back in the day? Then join Old School Indie, the U.K's biggest indie party (currently in 18 cities) for a night of floppy-fringed floor-fillers! Whether you are a child of the New Wave, Britpopper, rock n roller or 00s sleazer, we have some tunes for you. Taking in the whole glorious gambit with one or two ska & northern soul classics thrown in for good measure. Brought to you by the people behind Feeling Gloomy & Afternoon of Indie, we've been putting on indie events for nearly 21 years in the U.K, America & Germany, played the likes of Glasto, Isle of Wight, Latitude, Camp Bestival, Green Man, even main stage at Knebworth (not that year...) & worked with the likes Jarvis, Cocker, Suede and Brett Anderson, are often on BBC Radio 6 & do loads of fun indie-related stuff. You are in safe hands.

She's about to play the most important concert of her career: a complete performance of Bach's Chaconne for solo violin - but unfortunately, Amelia is easily distracted. Expect silliness, musical puns, historically questionable facts about Bach and (if she gets round to it) some beautiful classical music.

title: Offgridland
summary: Flight. Mode. The two most wonderful words...but even with his mobile blocking the outside world, can our desperate, overworked hero ever switch off? It's time to go to his favourite place: Offgridland. Just ignore the knocks on the door. And the constant ringing of his landline - a phone he can't even find because he hasn't used it since 2007. And that slightly worrying internal narrative that he's actually just living in a sitcom.…
_description: Flight. Mode. The two most wonderful words...but even with his mobile blocking the outside world, can our desperate, overworked hero ever switch off? It's time to go to his favourite place: Offgridland. Just ignore the knocks on the door. And the constant ringing of his landline - a phone he can't even find because he hasn't used it since 2007. And that slightly worrying internal narrative that he's actually just living in a sitcom. And his fear that his car, which no longer has a handbrake, could come crashing through the walls at any moment. No, it's fine. It's all totally fine. Just give the guy a break, yeah? Just for an hour? A frantic, claustrophobic comedy play that blends theatre and stand-up, told in real time by multi-award winning writer/performer, Paul Richards, Offgridland is the true story of a man trying to switch off from the perils of technology and, you know, other people.

title: On Voids
summary: Delilah Sykes is a multidisciplinary artist who primarily works in sound, text and installation. Sykes’ exhibition On Voids uses silence as a way to examine deep human truths. She builds intimate worlds that explore presence and non-presence as a way to understand love, loss, and the often amusing nature of our world. With On Voids, Sykes builds an archive of silence. Unlike most archives however, she fills it not with documents and…
_description: Delilah Sykes is a multidisciplinary artist who primarily works in sound, text and installation. Sykes’ exhibition On Voids uses silence as a way to examine deep human truths. She builds intimate worlds that explore presence and non-presence as a way to understand love, loss, and the often amusing nature of our world. With On Voids, Sykes builds an archive of silence. Unlike most archives however, she fills it not with documents and artefacts, but with a collection of ‘nothings’. How else might we find evidence that we have lived? Sykes has a magpie's eye for stories, objects and histories that can tell us an often forgotten version of ourselves. With projected text, an assemblage of shells, sound, and artist film, she asks: What is silenced? What was never said? What might we have left behind?

Hailed by "Opera Now" magazine as one of their favourite top ten sopranos in “a new generation of sopranos who are destined to have impressive careers”, Nadine won the inaugural Fulham Opera Robert Presley Memorial Verdi Prize in 2015. Gilles Van der Linden is a talented tenor who celebrated his debut at La Monnaie in the role of Rodriguez in Massenet's Don Quixote. The same year he was selected for the "Académies du Festival d'Aix-en-Provence" where he obtained the title of "Laureate" and was invited in that capacity by the festival of Verbier (Switzerland).

title: Other
summary: A stand-up show about the experiences of being different, trying/pretending to fit in, and learning how to embrace your otherness. Have you ever felt like you don’t belong? Do you know just enough football lingo to get by at work? Do you find yourself quietly nodding along during group conversations about Love Island instead of joining in? Do you not understand, nor care, about social media trends? We get you. If you feel like an…
_description: A stand-up show about the experiences of being different, trying/pretending to fit in, and learning how to embrace your otherness. Have you ever felt like you don’t belong? Do you know just enough football lingo to get by at work? Do you find yourself quietly nodding along during group conversations about Love Island instead of joining in? Do you not understand, nor care, about social media trends? We get you. If you feel like an ‘other’ in your own way(s), this show is for you! Rohan and Mani explore the challenges and triumphs of not fitting in - from finding out what happens when you throw a chair at school, to watching in horror as innocent poppadoms are smashed to pieces at a ‘curry night’, to still being the gamer kid in your 30s, and more... Individual stand-up sets from each comedian are followed by a joint final section where Rohan and Mani are onstage together for an interactive audience quiz, asking the important questions: How ‘other’ are you, and have you embraced it?

title: Parody Of The Rings
summary: At the local cinema, all hell breaks loose when the ushers misplace the DVDs of The Lord of the Rings trilogy – every single copy! With only the soundtracks left in hand, the team of hapless ushers must somehow improvise an entire three-film saga in front of a live audience. Get ready for an uproarious evening of comedy, chaos, and utter nonsense, as they attempt to recreate the epic tale of hobbits, elves, and orcs – without any…
_description: At the local cinema, all hell breaks loose when the ushers misplace the DVDs of The Lord of the Rings trilogy – every single copy! With only the soundtracks left in hand, the team of hapless ushers must somehow improvise an entire three-film saga in front of a live audience. Get ready for an uproarious evening of comedy, chaos, and utter nonsense, as they attempt to recreate the epic tale of hobbits, elves, and orcs – without any visuals, any planning, or any clue what they're doing. Will Frodo ever make it to Mordor? Will Aragorn get crowned king? Will Gollum stop stealing the show? Will anyone even remember who’s supposed to be who?! It’s a show that’s more fun than a barrel of hobbits, and definitely more chaotic than a Gandalf speech. If you’ve ever wanted to see Gollum fight a battle with sound effects, or Gandalf desperately trying to remember what happens next, then you’re in for a hilarious treat!

A chaotic takedown of hustle culture, heartache, and the corporate-to-cult pipeline. Polly: Is a little more wildcard energy than lovely Grace. Characters could include a thirty-something egg, a ghost hunter with telephonic tits and a horny hardware store worker. Or not. Perhaps come along and find out?

_description: Clown duo Plowman and Cummins present a surreal trip to the hairdressers. Expect bouncy blow-dries, falling over, mess, beauty, violence and classical singing. Using absurd visual comedy, physical theatre and splintered narrative, Plowman and Cummins herald a slapstick revival. The salon dissolves into moments of chaos, danger and silliness as objects, costumes and bodies morph in and out of reality. Part-clown part-performance art, ‘Plowman and Cummins are Plowman and Cummins’ is their debut hour-long comedy show, appearing at Brighton Fringe for the very first time. For fans of foolishness, laughing and the feeling you get when you watch someone run for the bus as it drives away.

Follow their lead, embrace the weird, and discover a space where curiosity, movement, and…

Gravity gets funky, time hiccups, and bodies move in ways usually reserved for dreams. Guided by headsets and a glitchy digital assistant, you’re led through the chaos by a troupe of seasoned world-hoppers. Follow their lead, embrace the weird, and discover a space where curiosity, movement, and playful exploration are not just allowed, but encouraged.

The absurdities get political, historical and sometimes to outright denial. "Commentary on racism within his native India is territory much less explored in comedy, which he cleverly manipulated in smart political analogies" - Chortle “He took us on an informed, interesting journey around the world, using absurd…
_description: A stand-up comedy show about the everyday absurdities of India, the US, and the UK where Prateek has lived and performed. The absurdities get political, historical and sometimes to outright denial. "Commentary on racism within his native India is territory much less explored in comedy, which he cleverly manipulated in smart political analogies" - Chortle “He took us on an informed, interesting journey around the world, using absurd logic with his endearing self-effacing humor laced with sharp insights into language and traditions from a seldom heard perspective.” - Express and Ham "His act is a unique breath of fresh comedy air. Prateek has a delightfully novel way about him, as he delivers his hilarious material, probing cultural mores and very original sparky political analogies." - Jill Edwards, Comic Boom Comedy Club Awards: Komedia New Comedy Award - Finalist West End New Act of the Year - Runner up BBC New Comedy Award - Shortlisted

_description: Jim Hooker is from Romford where the stereotypes are true, the family secrets are questionable, and everyone's on the rob. A storyteller from the cockney-Essex borderlands, Jim tells us the he chaos of his upbringing, eccentric relatives with colourful pasts, parents dispensing wildly unqualified advice, and the silliness of everyday life. His storytelling blends observation with unexpected twists. Expect vivid character voices, escalating absurdity, and jokes your nan would pretend to be offended by before sharing them in the group chat. Fresh from the Edinburgh Fringe 2025, Jim is road testing new material about family, identity, and why he can't go to Thailand on his own. ★★★★ Edinburgh Reviews ("A strong debut") - Bald 2-in-1 Show West End New Act of the Year 2025 – Finalist Get Up, Stand Up 2025 - Finalist So You Think You're Funny? – Semi-Finalist "Super funny and smart" – Judy Carter, The Comedy Bible

title: Reflections
summary: Reflections is an immersive interdisciplinary performance combining digital animation with live sound and music performed on an analogue synthesiser. The narrative follows Martha through ordinary yet layered moments where reflections become the main carriers of story — a computer screen, car mirrors, sunglasses, a lake’s surface, and finally a mirror at home. These reflective surfaces reveal and distort reality, exploring themes of…
_description: Reflections is an immersive interdisciplinary performance combining digital animation with live sound and music performed on an analogue synthesiser. The narrative follows Martha through ordinary yet layered moments where reflections become the main carriers of story — a computer screen, car mirrors, sunglasses, a lake’s surface, and finally a mirror at home. These reflective surfaces reveal and distort reality, exploring themes of perception, memory, and subjectivity. Accompanied by an atmospheric soundscape of composed music and environmental recordings — including cars, footsteps, rustling leaves, and birdsong — Reflections creates a multi-sensory environment that invites audiences to slow down, listen, and reconsider how images shape their understanding of the world. Margareta is a visual artist and art theorist. Her interdisciplinary practice spans painting, installation, animation, video and sound. Her works are exhibited internationally (New York, Toronto, Paris, Belgrade).

title: Rob Preston Is Bobby Prezinsky Unity Concert For Peace
summary: The show is a character comedy hour following Bobby Prezinsky: a hungry, wannabe presenter desperate to host something - anything - of global significance. Convinced the world is irreparably broken, Bobby decides the only solution is to stage a vast, world-changing Unity Concert for Peace, despite having absolutely no qualifications or experience. We follow Bobby’s attempts to pull the concert together. Along the way, he encounters a…
_description: The show is a character comedy hour following Bobby Prezinsky: a hungry, wannabe presenter desperate to host something - anything - of global significance. Convinced the world is irreparably broken, Bobby decides the only solution is to stage a vast, world-changing Unity Concert for Peace, despite having absolutely no qualifications or experience. We follow Bobby’s attempts to pull the concert together. Along the way, he encounters a series of characters keen to exploit his naivety and take advantage of his perceived momentum: Jacques De Klerk, the slick CEO of influencer marketing agency VibesTalent; Gregory Onions, a former Royal Marine turned motivational speaker; and Pieter Verkels, a Belgian dating coach, among others. The show blends extended character monologues with quickfire sketches, all anchored by Bobby’s delusional but sincere optimism and belief that his concert can change the world.

title: Rhapsody In Blue Jazz Sessions By Candlelight
summary: Award-winning Scottish pianist Matthew Shiel leads his jazz band on a swinging, toe-tapping odyssey through blues, Berlin cabaret, ragtime and New York jazz piano improv with poignant storytelling – by candlelight. Programme includes Joplin’s The Entertainer, Brubeck’s Take Five, Gershwin’s showstopper Rhapsody in Blue and cabaret music by the Jewish composer Erwin Schulhoff (1894-1942) – who was murdered by the Third Reich for the…
_description: Award-winning Scottish pianist Matthew Shiel leads his jazz band on a swinging, toe-tapping odyssey through blues, Berlin cabaret, ragtime and New York jazz piano improv with poignant storytelling – by candlelight. Programme includes Joplin’s The Entertainer, Brubeck’s Take Five, Gershwin’s showstopper Rhapsody in Blue and cabaret music by the Jewish composer Erwin Schulhoff (1894-1942) – who was murdered by the Third Reich for the "crime" of composing jazz. Fringe Classics are ★★★★★ rated: 'Enchanting... amusing, enlightening stories' ★★★★★ (EdinburghGuide.com). 'An atmosphere which is all too often missed in our digital world' ★★★★★ (CorrBlimey.com). 'Ripping good yarn' ★★★★★ (BingeFringe.com). Matthew Shiel is an award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera.

title: Redlights Work In Progress
summary: Last year after a series of hospitalisations Nayonica (BBC Upload festival, Sky Comedy Rep shortlist, Chortle Student comedy semi finalist) read Matthew McConaughey's memoir Greenlights, which was marketed as a manual to turn life's "red lights" into "Greenlights". Only it turned out the red lights in Matthew's' life were that he was too handsome, too successful and had too many millions of dollars. So Nayonica, has created this hour…
_description: Last year after a series of hospitalisations Nayonica (BBC Upload festival, Sky Comedy Rep shortlist, Chortle Student comedy semi finalist) read Matthew McConaughey's memoir Greenlights, which was marketed as a manual to turn life's "red lights" into "Greenlights". Only it turned out the red lights in Matthew's' life were that he was too handsome, too successful and had too many millions of dollars. So Nayonica, has created this hour of stand up in response for her fellow uglies and undesirables, looking back at all the redlights in her life. During this comedy show Nayonica revisits all the times in her life when planning and doing the sensible thing ended up going wrong (like when she took a gap year to go to Glastonbury and then Glastonbury took a gap year), then eventually turned out really well. Now she’s wondering if she should continue being logical and planning things or if she should just surrender to the chaos. This question is to complex for a stand up comedian to answer.

title: Rules Schmules How To Be Jew Ish
summary: Suzie Depreli – not a typically Jewish name, is it? – brings you one woman's passive aggressive mission to educate the world about what it means to have an orthodox family that ate sausages, an Asian Catholic husband that uses more Yiddish words than her Nana, and celebrate Passover without believing in God. And that this is all ok! With original songs performed live and anecdotes that will have Jewish and gentile audiences alike…
_description: Suzie Depreli – not a typically Jewish name, is it? – brings you one woman's passive aggressive mission to educate the world about what it means to have an orthodox family that ate sausages, an Asian Catholic husband that uses more Yiddish words than her Nana, and celebrate Passover without believing in God. And that this is all ok! With original songs performed live and anecdotes that will have Jewish and gentile audiences alike feeling like they were present for it all, this is a heartfelt, humorous musical journey through the pressures and pitfalls of understanding your identity in a world full of prejudice. This is how to be Jew-ish in Britain today.

Drop by any time, stay for a song or the whole day, and soak up the feel-good festival vibes.

title: Running With Scissors
summary: Running with scissors is a one-woman show that slices into the high stakes world of women surgeons: where precision is everything, authority is questioned, and one wrong move can change a life. With dark humour, raw honesty and scalpel-sharp storytelling, the show explores what it costs to cut your way into a profession that wasn’t built for you. From history to present day, the theatre of war to the NHS, from ambition to burnout and…
_description: Running with scissors is a one-woman show that slices into the high stakes world of women surgeons: where precision is everything, authority is questioned, and one wrong move can change a life. With dark humour, raw honesty and scalpel-sharp storytelling, the show explores what it costs to cut your way into a profession that wasn’t built for you. From history to present day, the theatre of war to the NHS, from ambition to burnout and confidence to doubt, Running with scissors exposes the blood, bone and bravery behind the mask. Told by an NHS surgeon turned storyteller, this is a show about hands that heal, systems that wound, and women who refuse to be careful.

_description: Salma is a Brit Pakistani stand-up comedian from a working-class background, known for blending sharp political commentary with personal stories drawn from her formidable Pakistani mother and Irish family. Laugh & explore with Salma how the core of our funny lives are the great and awful humans who make us—especially the one who tried to raise her. With sass, heart, and well-aimed political punches, her material is both fearless and refreshingly original.

We’ll attempt to answer the big questions (can enemies really become lovers? Is working in print media the real secret to finding love? Can you choose yourself and still get a happily ever after?), while coming up with a love…
_description: Ever wished your life was more like a movie? Join romantic comedy scholar Sarah Bradley as she takes you through a journey of comforting cliches, timeless tropes, falling-in-love montages and predictable endings. We’ll attempt to answer the big questions (can enemies really become lovers? Is working in print media the real secret to finding love? Can you choose yourself and still get a happily ever after?), while coming up with a love story all our own. Reality is overrated anyway.

title: Saul Henry Church Of Saulentology
summary: Saul Henry returns with his latest solo comedy show where he asks: 'Why join a cult when you can start your own?' Raised by atheists (with a Mormon Nana), Saul has spent his life chasing happiness, exploring oddball beliefs and cults, and dabbling in spiritual practices—until he accidentally almost joined one himself. Now, when companies, billionaires and tech bros command followings that would make cult leaders weep with envy, what…
_description: Saul Henry returns with his latest solo comedy show where he asks: 'Why join a cult when you can start your own?' Raised by atheists (with a Mormon Nana), Saul has spent his life chasing happiness, exploring oddball beliefs and cults, and dabbling in spiritual practices—until he accidentally almost joined one himself. Now, when companies, billionaires and tech bros command followings that would make cult leaders weep with envy, what isn't a cult these days? In 'The Church of Saulentology', Saul reaches his enlightened conclusion: true happiness means inventing your own ridiculous doctrine. And he's here to show you how- featuring a deeply unsettling Childcatcher-Colonel Sanders-Noseybonk-like figure who serenades unsuspecting chickens into the fold. Irreverent, thought-provoking, and absurdly funny. Tithing not required, but blind devotion is encouraged.

title: Sameera Bowers What Is Going On
summary: Sameera’s mum was proud when she graduated with a degree in Economics from Cambridge. Less so when she went to clown school. Now Sameera needs to find a job. On LinkedIn, she is a “changemaker, global innovator and value-driven impact leader.” But in life, she has no idea what she’s doing. In this multimedia comedy hour, Sameera searches for clarity through psychic guidance, grapples with the science of perfectly spherical cows, plots…
_description: Sameera’s mum was proud when she graduated with a degree in Economics from Cambridge. Less so when she went to clown school. Now Sameera needs to find a job. On LinkedIn, she is a “changemaker, global innovator and value-driven impact leader.” But in life, she has no idea what she’s doing. In this multimedia comedy hour, Sameera searches for clarity through psychic guidance, grapples with the science of perfectly spherical cows, plots her rise as the leader of an Indian tutoring empire and wants to make her grandma proud. If all else fails, she plans to become the first 22-year-old living in an old-age nursing home. Sameera was a member of The Cambridge Footlights (2025) and this year trained at the École Phillipe Gaulier clown school in France. In 2025 What Is Going On? completed a sellout run at the Camden Fringe and Sameera performed in the 5-star, award-winning comedy play Penelope Quadrangle at the Edinburgh Fringe, in which critics called her performance “genius” (Varsity).

At the centre is a hilariously delusional, self-declared genius who is absolutely certain she can do everything better than everyone else. Packed…
_description: 'Energetic and vivacious', Freya Bardell performs her Brighton Fringe debut. Part satire, part existential meltdown, Shine is a high-energy comedy about clawing your way out of the rat race. It follows one woman’s wildly overconfident quest to “shine” in a system designed to grind her down. At the centre is a hilariously delusional, self-declared genius who is absolutely certain she can do everything better than everyone else. Packed with laughs and sharp social critique, this show takes the toxic girlboss off her pedestal and asks if ambition is even worth the burnout.

title: Shadow Light Songs My Brother Taught Me Exhibition
summary: My first exhibition of new work, since I took part in the 2nd Art & Science Symposium & Exhibition at the Tsinghua Academy of Arts in Beijing in 2006. The seeds for this show were sown that same year when a treasured acoustic guitar of mine got broken and instead of ditching it (a luthier informed me it was beyond repair) I began photographing it. 20 years on, I've created 20 works – prints, collage, assemblage – that are inspired by…
_description: My first exhibition of new work, since I took part in the 2nd Art & Science Symposium & Exhibition at the Tsinghua Academy of Arts in Beijing in 2006. The seeds for this show were sown that same year when a treasured acoustic guitar of mine got broken and instead of ditching it (a luthier informed me it was beyond repair) I began photographing it. 20 years on, I've created 20 works – prints, collage, assemblage – that are inspired by and a tribute to my late brother Stephen who tragically took his own life at age 20. Fifty years on, I’m still shining a light into those shadows.

title: Snow Along The Line
summary: Snow Along the Line traces the life of an extraordinary yet ordinary woman whose journey spans nearly a century of change. Born in 1928, only months before the Equal Franchise Act granted women full voting rights, her story begins in the industrial heartland of North East England and traces the decline of the mining industry to South Wales. Through songs, poems, archival images, backing tracks and narration, theatre-maker and musician…
_description: Snow Along the Line traces the life of an extraordinary yet ordinary woman whose journey spans nearly a century of change. Born in 1928, only months before the Equal Franchise Act granted women full voting rights, her story begins in the industrial heartland of North East England and traces the decline of the mining industry to South Wales. Through songs, poems, archival images, backing tracks and narration, theatre-maker and musician Phil Jones, and actor Hannah Barrie, captures the rhythm of a life shaped by both sweeping change and everyday triumph. The production explores themes relating to death of a loved one which some audience members may find upsetting.

title: Sid Ozalid Amazing Wonders Of Scotland

_description: If you like silly you will love Sid. High energy, interactive nonsense for the whole family. From the Edinburgh Fringe to international stages in New York, Paris, Melbourne and beyond, Sid has built a legendary reputation for shows that are unpredictable, magical and utterly unforgettable. A wonderful blend of mayhem, magic, poetry, dad dancing and mentalism. This is your chance to learn all about Scottish culture, why Scotland invented all the cool stuff in the world and finally learn what is hiding under the kilt. You will not believe your eyes or your ears, and may end up being more Scottish than you were on the way in! Expect the impossible. Sid doesn’t just perform magic — he plays with reality. Engaging nonsense for everyone from the age of eight to 80!

_description: All aboard - the skysteamer is about to depart! Join C.J. Carter-Stephenson as he brings his steampunk novelette SKYSTEAMER TO ATHENS vividly to life. A lavish sensory experience, which will transport you back to a fictionalised version of the 19th Century, where top hats and corsets are the height of fashion and the wonder of steam is transforming the world. Cast off your mooring ropes and prepare to soar into the clouds.

_description: Join us for a total celebration of the northern soul scene. Get ‘out on the floor’ with 3 hours of Northern Soul classics, Motown favourites and a host of monster soul tunes. title: Spalien Acecraft Speaningless Moonerisms
summary: Fourteen years after their official final gig, Spalien Acecraft are back on planet Earth with their unique streak of interactive intergalactic latter-day cabaret for pop-sensible extra-terrestrials. Featuring original celestial minstrel Johnny Acecraft, three authenticated relations and one tolerated acquaintance: sisters Maliniverse and The Elbot on harmonies harvested from shared childhoods and questionable life choices, wife Jopiter…
_description: Fourteen years after their official final gig, Spalien Acecraft are back on planet Earth with their unique streak of interactive intergalactic latter-day cabaret for pop-sensible extra-terrestrials. Featuring original celestial minstrel Johnny Acecraft, three authenticated relations and one tolerated acquaintance: sisters Maliniverse and The Elbot on harmonies harvested from shared childhoods and questionable life choices, wife Jopiter on genuine musical talent, and key-stabber extraordinaire Nathanova. The band will emulate their past daftness with favourites including Orange for a Head, Man with the Magical Armpits, Speedy Bikini and more, and they may also get round to writing some new stuff. Audience participation will be gently encouraged and enthusiastically misinterpreted. Bring your offspring: the band will censor their extremest egregiousnesses. This is a pay-what-you-can, bequeath-what-you-believe, share-what-you-dare affair before they re-disappear into the shimmery nowhere.

title: Stuart Thomas Bad Fatty Work In Progress
summary: Stuart Thomas returns with Bad Fatty, a bold, fast-paced stand-up show from a slightly chaotic Welsh man who’s done being polite about how the world sees fat people. Stuart tears into the clichés and lazy stereotypes—especially the way fat men get flattened into the wheezing sidekick, the punchline, or the cautionary tale. He’s had enough, and he’s taking no prisoners. Alongside the big stuff, Stuart dives into the simple joys that…
_description: Stuart Thomas returns with Bad Fatty, a bold, fast-paced stand-up show from a slightly chaotic Welsh man who’s done being polite about how the world sees fat people. Stuart tears into the clichés and lazy stereotypes—especially the way fat men get flattened into the wheezing sidekick, the punchline, or the cautionary tale. He’s had enough, and he’s taking no prisoners. Alongside the big stuff, Stuart dives into the simple joys that keep him going—most importantly, his undying love for Premier Inn. The purple haven. The land of perfect pillows, identical hallways, and a breakfast buffet that feels like a hug. It’s not glamorous, but God it’s good. Bisexual, unapologetic, and buzzing with energy, Stuart unpacks what it means to live joyfully in a body the world loves to misunderstand. Bad Fatty is funny, fiery, and full of bite—a celebration of refusing to shrink yourself just to make other people comfortable.

title: Tactile
summary: “What will be left when I die?” As our lives become more virtual, tangible objects, the things that carry our personal histories, are increasingly disappearing. The play follows 3 siblings, Saf, Chris and Jem, as they reunite to sort through their late mother’s belongings. What begins as a practical task becomes a deeply emotional excavation of memory, love and loss. Through humour, tenderness and confrontation, Tactile examines how…
_description: “What will be left when I die?” As our lives become more virtual, tangible objects, the things that carry our personal histories, are increasingly disappearing. The play follows 3 siblings, Saf, Chris and Jem, as they reunite to sort through their late mother’s belongings. What begins as a practical task becomes a deeply emotional excavation of memory, love and loss. Through humour, tenderness and confrontation, Tactile examines how grief reshapes the bonds between us and how the things we hold both physical and emotional connect us across generations. At its core, Tactile explores society’s ‘return to the analogue’ as coined on social media, in a culture that is becoming ever more digitised. It examines what “real” connection means at a time when so much of our communication and memory storage happens online. By focusing on the intimate and the material, the piece offers audiences a space to reflect on the value of touch, presence and what we can inherit and learn from each other.

title: Tadiwa Mahlunge Hakuna Ma Tad Tad
summary: “I'm back with a new show about how being a refugee forged my ambition, self belief and why I believe you deserve the same Kanye-level confidence I have (without the life-altering trauma). I have in no way cynically leveraged my life story and timed this tour to profiteer off the tense mood on immigration in the nation, and any such accusation will be met with litigation from my mother." - Tadiwa Tadiwa Mahlunge is one of the fastest-…

I have in no way cynically leveraged my life story and timed this tour to profiteer off the tense mood on immigration in the nation, and any such accusation will be met with litigation from my mother." - Tadiwa Tadiwa Mahlunge is one of the fastest-rising stars in British comedy, named by The Times as one of their “Top 3 Stars of Tomorrow”. Tadiwa has appeared on Stand Up Sketch Show (ITV2), Am I The A**hole (Comedy Central), Comedians Giving Lectures (Dave), Comedy Central Live, The Football’s On (TNT Sports), and Live from Aberystwyth Pier (BBC Wales). On radio, he’s been heard on The News Quiz (BBC Radio 4), Best of the Fringe (BBC Radio 4), and What Just Happened (BBC Radio Wales). His stand-up has earned rave reviews across the board: ★★★★ “Expect great things from this charming comic.”

title: Thats Just The Way Its Undone
summary: “When I was a child I dreamt of happily ever after. When I was 27 my dreams came true. Somewhere along the way, I realised my dreams were not my own.” That’s Just The Way It’s (un)Done is an abstract autobiographical theatrical cabaret that dances through the dangers of heteronormative conditioning into a world of queer fluidity. Join Rowan For Now as they explore what it means to outgrow yourself – shedding the skins of strict…
_description: “When I was a child I dreamt of happily ever after. When I was 27 my dreams came true. Somewhere along the way, I realised my dreams were not my own.” That’s Just The Way It’s (un)Done is an abstract autobiographical theatrical cabaret that dances through the dangers of heteronormative conditioning into a world of queer fluidity. Join Rowan For Now as they explore what it means to outgrow yourself – shedding the skins of strict narratives and peeling back everything they knew to be true. Through nostalgic imagery, storytelling, lip-sync, humour, and chaos, Rowan reveals the freedom and grief that emerge when normative paradigms begin to unravel. Developed as part of Rowan’s MA in Queer Performance, this piece continues to evolve, much like its creator. This iteration is both a performance and a process, part ritual, part revelation. It invites audiences into an experience of untangling and reimagining. A queer Happily Never After.

title: The Double G Comedy Rodeo
summary: A mash-up of chaos, charisma, and killer comedy, the Rodeo flips everything you think you know about a comedy show. It’s in-your-face, up-close, and happily smashes the fourth wall, pulling the audience right into the action. Expect fast laughs, fearless performances, and a show as unpredictable as it is unforgettable. Spontaneity rules; comics bounce off the crowd, moments spiral into mayhem, and somehow it all lands as a perfectly…
_description: A mash-up of chaos, charisma, and killer comedy, the Rodeo flips everything you think you know about a comedy show. It’s in-your-face, up-close, and happily smashes the fourth wall, pulling the audience right into the action. Expect fast laughs, fearless performances, and a show as unpredictable as it is unforgettable. Spontaneity rules; comics bounce off the crowd, moments spiral into mayhem, and somehow it all lands as a perfectly timed, delightfully anarchic experience. Think mad meets bad, led by a ringmaster with a wicked grin and a talent for finding hilarity in the chaos. Bold, raw, and joyfully unfiltered, it’s a celebration of live comedy at its most explosive. At its heart, the Rodeo is about connection, between performer and audience, between laughter and surprise. Those in-the-moment sparks, sharp quips, playful interactions, and unexpected outbursts keep you on your toes and grinning all night. Wild, raucous, and unapologetically fun.

title: The Doris Show
summary: The Doris Show starring Doris Daydream celebrates the music and the life of Doris Day, the legendary famous movie star and singer. The one hour tribute show gives an insight into Doris’ life - how she started, her husbands and leading men (there were lots of those!) and backgrounds to the songs. The show features all of Doris’ big hits including Secret Love, Deadwood Stage, Black Hills of Dakota, Perhaps, Perhaps, Perhaps and of course…

The show features all of Doris’ big hits including Secret Love, Deadwood Stage, Black Hills of Dakota, Perhaps, Perhaps, Perhaps and of course the acclaimed Que Sera, Sera.

title: The Changing Room
summary: A vivid, fast-paced portrait of teenagers on the brink, set in and around a swimming pool, The Changing Room follows a group of young people as they navigate identity, friendship, and the restless energy of growing up. Moments collide and overlap – worries are shared, friendships formed as the ground begins to shift beneath their feet. This is a play about losing your footing and stepping into who you might become... The award-winning…
_description: A vivid, fast-paced portrait of teenagers on the brink, set in and around a swimming pool, The Changing Room follows a group of young people as they navigate identity, friendship, and the restless energy of growing up. Moments collide and overlap – worries are shared, friendships formed as the ground begins to shift beneath their feet. This is a play about losing your footing and stepping into who you might become... The award-winning Kaleidoscope Theatre Company brings this vibrant, funny, and touching play to life with its trademark ensemble work and bold staging, offering an honest look at the exhilaration and uncertainty of youth – and one magnificently embarrassing dad.

title: The Elephant In The Room
summary: Step into the extraordinary life of Joseph Merrick in a moving new musical that tells the true story of the Elephant Man. Through powerful songs and heartfelt storytelling, this production shines a light on his courage, humanity, and determination to live life on his own terms. More than a tale of adversity, it is a celebration of resilience and the universal desire to belong.

A cautionary tale to those who do not believe in the supernatural. When the mediocre Scholar Wang drunkenly takes on a dare to invite the Red Judge of the Underworld to dinner, he finds his fortune magically turning for the better. But be careful of what you wish for. A Chinese Grimm tale of greed, lust and vengeance, based on the colourful ghost and fox tales in Strange Tales from a Chinese Studios by Pu SongLing in C18th. From the teams behind "The Ballad of Mulan" and "Moby Dick".

The Human Statue begins literally and metaphorically stuck. Through the show they will attempt to make sense of the world. The stories will be gory, tragic, beautiful and surreal and find meaning in what can be overlooked. This work-in-progress comedy show is for people who, when chopping the…
_description: A Human Statue tells an anecdote about a couple on holiday taking a selfie on London Bridge. A world is built using stories, video and props. The Human Statue begins literally and metaphorically stuck. Through the show they will attempt to make sense of the world. The stories will be gory, tragic, beautiful and surreal and find meaning in what can be overlooked. This work-in-progress comedy show is for people who, when chopping the onions, it is the onions who are crying. It will blend comedy and performance art to achieve a mixed result.

As usual we will also play a wide range of other material old and new demonstrating the versatility of a mandolin orchestra in the modern age.

title: The Music Of George Harrison Live The All Things Must Pass Orchestras Farewell Tour
company: The All Things Must Pass Orchestra
summary: The ALL THINGS MUST PASS ORCHESTRA, a ten-piece band devoted to the music of George Harrison, has been filling theatres with sell-out performances since 2013. For over a decade, the Brighton-based ensemble – led by singer and multiinstrumentalist Alex Eberhard – has lovingly recreated Harrison’s kaleidoscopic sound world, from the ground-breaking All Things Must Pass album to treasured classics like Something, While My Guitar Gently…
_description: The ALL THINGS MUST PASS ORCHESTRA, a ten-piece band devoted to the music of George Harrison, has been filling theatres with sell-out performances since 2013. For over a decade, the Brighton-based ensemble – led by singer and multiinstrumentalist Alex Eberhard – has lovingly recreated Harrison’s kaleidoscopic sound world, from the ground-breaking All Things Must Pass album to treasured classics like Something, While My Guitar Gently Weeps, Here Comes The Sun, and My Sweet Lord. The next run of shows will be especially poignant: In 2026, the ALL THINGS MUST PASS ORCHESTRA will embark on their final tour. “Thanks for paying my dad so much respect” Dhani Harrison

title: The Night Ali Died
summary: Ali is wanted dead by a London gangster and is placed under police surveillance for his protection, yet for some reason, on one night, Ali decides to sneak out into the night and leave his family behind, sparking a bloody domino effect that ricochets through the sleepy streets of his rural city. Through the brutal accounts of Ali, a henchman, a detective and a mob boss, find out what on earth happened on the night Ali died. The Night…
_description: Ali is wanted dead by a London gangster and is placed under police surveillance for his protection, yet for some reason, on one night, Ali decides to sneak out into the night and leave his family behind, sparking a bloody domino effect that ricochets through the sleepy streets of his rural city. Through the brutal accounts of Ali, a henchman, a detective and a mob boss, find out what on earth happened on the night Ali died. The Night Ali Died premiered at Prague Fringe 2025 and won three awards in its first ever run including a vote as audience favourite. Described as "like watching a Tarantino film onstage" by Theatreandartsreviews.com, its cinematic style and bold and punchy soundtrack took over the Prague festival with an electric buzz. ★★★★★ "A GRIPPING CRIME THRILLER THAT UNFOLDS WITH CINEMATIC PRECISION" Colchester Gazette ★★★★★ "LIKE WATCHING A TV CRIME DRAMA CONDENSED INTO ONE HOUR" Broadway Baby

Certainly not Sash, so join them and find out!

Expect immersive myths brought to life through metaphor, music and dance. Meet the gods, walk in their footsteps and revel with them as you are guided through the esoteric to find just how deep the mysteries go! Audience participation is required so you really ought to dress…

Expect immersive myths brought to life through metaphor, music and dance. Meet the gods, walk in their footsteps and revel with them as you are guided through the esoteric to find just how deep the mysteries go! Audience participation is required so you really ought to dress for the occasion. The dress code is ancient Greek myths although it is not mandatory. LGBTQIA+ friendly with music by the Olympic gods of jazz; local legends, Hill Collective. The event will be in 4 parts with regular intervals in-between. The last hour will be a celebration party. We hope you will stay with us for the full journey, but you are welcome to depart, as you wish!

title: This Is How I Got Arrested
summary: "This Is How I Got Arrested... After Smuggling Drugs Across The Border But Never Actually Getting Caught With Any Drugs" is a hysterically funny, dark-humoured & courageous one woman show that breaks the fourth wall and leaves you questioning what is fact and what is fiction. Meet Sophie, a young girl from a working class world who takes the phrase "girls just wanna have fun" too far. This epic fast-paced story starts with tales from…
_description: "This Is How I Got Arrested... After Smuggling Drugs Across The Border But Never Actually Getting Caught With Any Drugs" is a hysterically funny, dark-humoured & courageous one woman show that breaks the fourth wall and leaves you questioning what is fact and what is fiction. Meet Sophie, a young girl from a working class world who takes the phrase "girls just wanna have fun" too far. This epic fast-paced story starts with tales from Sophie's upbringing, through to the moment of her arrest in a foreign country. Lies will be uncovered, stories will be told, and mischief will be coursed. All under the pressure of a ticking clock, only time will reveal the truth. Themes of Drug's Use, Toxic Femineity, Alcohol & Strong Language.

_description: Delve into the dysfunctional lives of the employees of... We've not got that far yet! After sold-out shows in Cambridge, London and at the Brighton (2024 & 2025) and Edinburgh (2025) Fringes, The Workplace has come back to Brighton! Join us for your new favourite workplace sitcom. Based on suggestions from the audience, a new cast of eclectic characters live out the every day of working the 9 to 5, with ego clashes, office politics and absurd workplace situations. If you're a fan of The Office, Parks and Recreation, or comedy in general, this is a show you don't want to miss.

title: Theatre Of Wholiness
summary: A queer cosmic cabaret of myth, mischief and metamorphosis... Part theatre, part ritual, part glitch in the matrix - Theatre of Wholiness invites you into an intimate space where identity is shapeshifting, joy is medicine, and the ordinary loosens its grip. Lumi the Host - glitch-witch storyteller and sassy steward of the in-between - guides you on a journey through archetype, embodiment, humour and presence. Co-created with the room…
_description: A queer cosmic cabaret of myth, mischief and metamorphosis... Part theatre, part ritual, part glitch in the matrix - Theatre of Wholiness invites you into an intimate space where identity is shapeshifting, joy is medicine, and the ordinary loosens its grip. Lumi the Host - glitch-witch storyteller and sassy steward of the in-between - guides you on a journey through archetype, embodiment, humour and presence. Co-created with the room as a one-off collective moment. This is a space where wholeness isn’t something you chase - it’s something you activate. Playful. Tender. Disarming. A celebration of queer becoming and the art of returning to yourself.

title: Therapist Zero
summary: “Therapist Zero” is a poignant and comedic one-person show by Brian Leonard that explores the tumultuous journey of parenting a spirited daughter, Emily, who challenges traditional norms and expectations. Set against the backdrop of countless therapists and mental health professionals, Leonard navigates the complexities of familial love, resilience, and the quest for understanding. With humor and heart, the show unpacks the trials of…
_description: “Therapist Zero” is a poignant and comedic one-person show by Brian Leonard that explores the tumultuous journey of parenting a spirited daughter, Emily, who challenges traditional norms and expectations. Set against the backdrop of countless therapists and mental health professionals, Leonard navigates the complexities of familial love, resilience, and the quest for understanding. With humor and heart, the show unpacks the trials of raising a child with behavioral challenges, reflecting on his own upbringing and the lessons learned along the way. Join Brian as he takes you on an emotional rollercoaster filled with laughter, insight, and the transformative power of connection.

title: Trauma An Exhibition
summary: Trauma: An Exhibition Trauma isn’t just a moment we experience, feel, carry — it’s a texture. A glitch. A bruise on the everyday. This art event explores what we carry, what we hide, and what is handed down through bodies, rooms, families, borders, and institutions. Through work that’s bold, raw, and sometimes sardonic, we’ll explore trauma as both intensely personal and quietly collective—the kind that lives in memory, language,…
_description: Trauma: An Exhibition Trauma isn’t just a moment we experience, feel, carry — it’s a texture. A glitch. A bruise on the everyday. This art event explores what we carry, what we hide, and what is handed down through bodies, rooms, families, borders, and institutions. Through work that’s bold, raw, and sometimes sardonic, we’ll explore trauma as both intensely personal and quietly collective—the kind that lives in memory, language, movement, paperwork, and silence. Expect an experience that doesn’t “inspire” you out of pain or tidy it into a neat ending. This is art that remains complex: fractures, repairs, repetitions, the politics of who is believed, and the strange creativity of survival. Some pieces will pull you in softly. Others might catch you off guard. All of them ask the same question: what happens when we stop treating trauma as a private problem — and start seeing it as a social one? Come for the artistic vulnerability. Art by Blood N Guts Murphy and Broken Glass.

title: Transatlantic
summary: Nominated last year for Best New Show, Trans*Atlantic is back at Brighton Fringe on tour! Join the acclaimed transgender American comedian Dian Cathal at the Komedia on Saturday, May 2nd, 2026 for the Brighton stop of his international tour. Cathal (As seen on BBC, Apple TV, HBO) presents his explosive, 5-star reviewed hour of stand-up exploring the messy crossroads of identity, culture, and politics. Which is it: Americans or trans…
_description: Nominated last year for Best New Show, Trans*Atlantic is back at Brighton Fringe on tour! Join the acclaimed transgender American comedian Dian Cathal at the Komedia on Saturday, May 2nd, 2026 for the Brighton stop of his international tour. Cathal (As seen on BBC, Apple TV, HBO) presents his explosive, 5-star reviewed hour of stand-up exploring the messy crossroads of identity, culture, and politics. Which is it: Americans or trans people the British dislike more? Hailed as "Combative, clever, and very, very charming" (Reviews Hub), Trans*Atlantic uses razor-sharp wit to dissect the cultural tension between his New York roots and his life as a "gender immigrant" in the UK. Set against a backdrop of global chaos, this is vital, fearless, and necessary comedy. It's a powerhouse hour blending sharp political takes with hilarious personal anecdotes. What: Dian Cathal: Trans*Atlantic When: Saturday, May 2nd, 2026 Where: Komedia, Brighton

title: Two Stroke The Northern Soul Musical
summary: Two-Stroke is “a lively and enjoyable trip through the world of Northern Soul music” (Isle of Wight County Press), a fast-paced new musical inspired by the electric buzz of the 1986 National Scooter Rally. Three teenagers pile onto the ferry in search of the best weekend of their lives — chasing music, freedom, and the thrill of escape. Over one unforgettable weekend, friendships are forged, tensions rise, and plans take unexpected…
_description: Two-Stroke is “a lively and enjoyable trip through the world of Northern Soul music” (Isle of Wight County Press), a fast-paced new musical inspired by the electric buzz of the 1986 National Scooter Rally. Three teenagers pile onto the ferry in search of the best weekend of their lives — chasing music, freedom, and the thrill of escape. Over one unforgettable weekend, friendships are forged, tensions rise, and plans take unexpected turns, all set to the driving beat of Northern Soul. Featuring iconic tracks including Beggin', I’m On My Way, and Out On The Floor, Two-Stroke captures the joy, heartache, and rebellion of youth, celebrating the unifying power of music and the moments that shape who we become. Written by award-winning playwright Joe Plumb, this “musical delight” (Isle of Wight County Press) is fun, heartfelt, and bursting with soul.

title: Ultimate Cabaret Champion
summary: Step into the Fringe’s cabaret showdown, where artists from wildly different disciplines battle to prove that their art form reigns supreme. Cheer on drag divas, burlesque babes, and mystifying magicians as they face off in challenges chosen by you! Will it be glamour, filth, or pure chaos? Come crown the Ultimate Cabaret Champion. _description: Step into the Fringe’s cabaret showdown, where artists from wildly different disciplines battle to prove that their art form reigns supreme. Hosted by multi-award winning cabaret reprobate Aidan Sadler, this is a brand new concept show for 2026 bringing a competitive edge to the cabaret landscape.

title: When Gary Left Annie Almost
summary: It started with an offer of a gherkin on a bench during lunchtime, it ended with a relationship that dragged well beyond it's sell-by date. Gary adores Annie but he absolutely has to leave her. Annie "quite likes" Gary but really shouldn't have ever entered a relationship with him in the first place. It's obvious to everyone that they need to part ways, but sometimes real life just gets in the way; why would you dump somebody you're…
_description: It started with an offer of a gherkin on a bench during lunchtime, it ended with a relationship that dragged well beyond it's sell-by date. Gary adores Annie but he absolutely has to leave her. Annie "quite likes" Gary but really shouldn't have ever entered a relationship with him in the first place. It's obvious to everyone that they need to part ways, but sometimes real life just gets in the way; why would you dump somebody you're still so, well, 'comfortable' with? A comedic exploration of relationships, companionships and the little things that keep couples together...and the really annoying things that drive them apart! A brand new comedy play written and performed by Helen Pain and Paul Richards.

It’s a night to celebrate life’s winners, including but not limited to: best original song, product of the year, soup of the day, oldest man, funniest in the office, Sunday Best, Monday Worst and longest time survived in a lion cage (spoiler: it’s the lion). Under our roof, all winners are…
_description: Roll out the red carpet and dust off your medals. Will & Noah invite you to a sketch show set in the world's furthest-reaching awards ceremony. It’s a night to celebrate life’s winners, including but not limited to: best original song, product of the year, soup of the day, oldest man, funniest in the office, Sunday Best, Monday Worst and longest time survived in a lion cage (spoiler: it’s the lion). Under our roof, all winners are created equal. No achievement is too small. After a successful Edinburgh Fringe run with ‘Will & Noah: Too Much Time on Their Phones’ (although not successful enough to win any awards) Will and Noah have, by complete chance, designed a show format in which they win loads and loads of awards. Blending musical comedy, video and audience participation, this fast-paced dynamic sketch show is a wholesome slice-of-life look at what it means to win.

_description: From shameless show off to opera singer, step into a world of captivating musical storytelling as Georgia Mae Bishop takes you on a journey through music and theatre’s most iconic Witches, Britches, and B*tches. In this enchanting and intimate evening of stereotypes and surprises, expect a thrilling fusion of operatic arias, show tunes, and pop anthems, ranging from Mozart and Verdi to Beyoncé and Lady Gaga. The magic of theatre as you’ve never seen it before, with killer vocals that defy genre and touching anecdotes of the life of a wannabe theatre kid turned professional opera singer.

title: With All My Fondest Love
summary: While hoping to reconnect with his ex-girlfriend, Noah discovers a dusty box of unread diaries and letters in his attic. Hidden inside is the forgotten story of his grandparent’s marriage. This tender true story is one grandson’s attempt to piece together their forgotten lives, from Frank Sinatra and amateur cricket to burnt letters and unspeakable loss. Noah’s family history entwines with his own messy romance, challenging him to find…
_description: While hoping to reconnect with his ex-girlfriend, Noah discovers a dusty box of unread diaries and letters in his attic. Hidden inside is the forgotten story of his grandparent’s marriage. This tender true story is one grandson’s attempt to piece together their forgotten lives, from Frank Sinatra and amateur cricket to burnt letters and unspeakable loss. Noah’s family history entwines with his own messy romance, challenging him to find hope and forgiveness within the chaos of heartbreak and memory. Celebrated by the Oscar-winning writer Simon Beaufoy (The Full Monty, Slumdog Millionaire) as a 'quietly devastating, beautifully written piece of drama'. Bring your own family together for this moving and hopeful one-person play told across three generations. We'll remember those we have loved and lost and celebrate how joyful remembering them can be.

title: Yes Anderson The Budapest Grand Piano
summary: The films of Wes Anderson occupy a unique and vivid universe unto themselves: love and yearning; complicated family dynamics; existential angst. Binoculars. Maps. Suitcases. This year, to celebrate their fifth Brighton Fringe, Tiny Dynamite are bringing you Wes Anderson’s art deco aesthetic, with an iconic hotel setting and concierge experience not to be forgotten. Enter the whimsical, colourful, idiosyncratic, world of Wes. Based on…
_description: The films of Wes Anderson occupy a unique and vivid universe unto themselves: love and yearning; complicated family dynamics; existential angst. Binoculars. Maps. Suitcases. This year, to celebrate their fifth Brighton Fringe, Tiny Dynamite are bringing you Wes Anderson’s art deco aesthetic, with an iconic hotel setting and concierge experience not to be forgotten. Enter the whimsical, colourful, idiosyncratic, world of Wes. Based on audience suggestions, the performers will conjure a fully improvised, never to be seen again, medley of rooted characters and story fragments. Our show will also include a live improvised soundtrack. So pack your leather valise, leave a laconic note, and join us in the ballroom for The Budapest Grand Piano, a show that will make you laugh, make you contemplate missed opportunities, and provide a bar so you can actually have a cognac this time. Ticket holders are eligible for discount codes at the Mercure Brighton Seafront, including 15% off your room.

title: The Covetousness
summary: Adapted from a rarely performed scene in Tang Xianzu’s Ming Dynasty masterpiece. The Dream of Nanke, this production brings back a nearly lost gem of Chinese theatre. With only fragmentary scripts and oral accounts surviving, our team spent months researching ancient scores and historical materials, reconstructing the piece with scholarly care and artistic integrity. It is a dream on the edge of waking, where loyalty clashes with…
_description: Adapted from a rarely performed scene in Tang Xianzu’s Ming Dynasty masterpiece. The Dream of Nanke, this production brings back a nearly lost gem of Chinese theatre. With only fragmentary scripts and oral accounts surviving, our team spent months researching ancient scores and historical materials, reconstructing the piece with scholarly care and artistic integrity. It is a dream on the edge of waking, where loyalty clashes with destiny. Performed by a huadan (female warrior) and hualian (painted-face male role), the piece breaks conventional casting, blending intense physicality with emotional depth — a rare portrayal in the world of Chinese opera (Kunqu). This is not just a revival, but a reawakening of a forgotten cultural dream. Performed in a traditional Chinese operatic vocal style with Chinese and English surtitles. Workshops are also offered — an opportunity to explore Kunqu up close; details via the Rotunda Theatre.

title: Chunxiangs Schoolroom Prank
summary: Discover one of China’s most charming stage traditions in Chunxiang’s Schoolroom Prank, a lively Kunqu comedy full of wit, elegance and playful chaos. This production brings classical Chinese opera to the Fringe in a fresh, joyful and accessible way. Cheeky, clever Chunxiang turns a strict schoolroom upside down with sharp humour, quick timing and irresistible comic energy. Live singing, graceful movement and rhythmic dialogue create a…
_description: Discover one of China’s most charming stage traditions in Chunxiang’s Schoolroom Prank, a lively Kunqu comedy full of wit, elegance and playful chaos. This production brings classical Chinese opera to the Fringe in a fresh, joyful and accessible way. Cheeky, clever Chunxiang turns a strict schoolroom upside down with sharp humour, quick timing and irresistible comic energy. Live singing, graceful movement and rhythmic dialogue create a vibrant world where tradition meets contemporary theatrical flair. The show also features the refined artistry of male dan performance, a classical cross-gender technique adding expressive beauty and a rare glimpse into the imaginative gender traditions of early Chinese theatre. This is an ideal introduction to Kunqu for newcomers and a treat for lovers of world theatre. Performed in traditional Chinese operatic vocal with Chinese and English surtitles. Workshops are also offered, an opportunity to explore Kunqu up close, details via Rotunda theatre.

title: These Roots Are Made For Walking
summary: When women's stories are recorded, who gets the last word? Three original solo plays illuminate untold stories from history, exploring themes of queerness, feminism, bodily autonomy, gender roles, and activism throughout the 20th century. The triple bill opens with "She," a fictional tale set in 1941 filled with tap dancing, sapphic waltzes, and undercover nuns. Inspired by Sarah Waters’ Tipping the Velvet, "She" explores the beauty…
_description: When women's stories are recorded, who gets the last word? Three original solo plays illuminate untold stories from history, exploring themes of queerness, feminism, bodily autonomy, gender roles, and activism throughout the 20th century. The triple bill opens with "She," a fictional tale set in 1941 filled with tap dancing, sapphic waltzes, and undercover nuns. Inspired by Sarah Waters’ Tipping the Velvet, "She" explores the beauty and risks of queer love during WWII. "Lesbians Eat Fire" follows Joanne, a fictional character created from the lived experiences of the Lesbian Avengers and lesbian activists from the early 1990s. The play demonstrates carrying the fire for those who came before us and staying alive when hope feels impossible. "The Eye of Dawn" is a take on Mata Hari, a 1920s Dutch exotic dancer accused of espionage. A woman caught between myth and truth, this play gives a voice to the multiplicity of her existence with a healthy serving of absurdism, irony, and show.

title: Kahloca
summary: KAH’LOCA is a 20 minutes family friendly outdoor work. Women dressed in bright colours with flamboyant funky headpieces use dance-theatre to challenge the narrative of what it means to be a woman and bring to public attention the scale of violence against women. KAH’LOCA's costumes symbolise a desire for everyone to have their own voice, to speak out, be bold and step up for themselves, empowering the harmed and shaping once mute…
_description: KAH’LOCA is a 20 minutes family friendly outdoor work. Women dressed in bright colours with flamboyant funky headpieces use dance-theatre to challenge the narrative of what it means to be a woman and bring to public attention the scale of violence against women. KAH’LOCA's costumes symbolise a desire for everyone to have their own voice, to speak out, be bold and step up for themselves, empowering the harmed and shaping once mute spaces. A combination of voices, classical guitar song, gritty percussive sounds and protests creates the overall soundscape, overlapping with the breath of the dancers in motion to create a multi-sensory live performance which celebrates female strength and resilience.
