title: Between Strands
summary: Between Strands is a performance about hair, memory, and the bonds that endure beyond loss. Drawing on East Asian cultural symbolism, it follows the tender relationship between a granddaughter and her grandmother, weaving in Taiwanese traditions, stories, and the rituals that shape their farewells. In many Asian cultures, hair carries lineage, identity, and the thread that connects generations. This physical performance explores how a…
_description: Between Strands is a performance about hair, memory, and the bonds that endure beyond loss. Drawing on East Asian cultural symbolism, it follows the tender relationship between a granddaughter and her grandmother, weaving in Taiwanese traditions, stories, and the rituals that shape their farewells. In many Asian cultures, hair carries lineage, identity, and the thread that connects generations. This physical performance explores how a single strand of hair can hold memory, intimacy, and hope. Using shadow work, gesture, and breath-led movement, the performers trace the shifting space between holding on and letting go, revealing the invisible threads that keep us connected. Blending contemporary performance with cultural heritage, Between Strands opens a quiet yet powerful space to reflect on belonging, transformation, and the ties we continue to carry.

title: Out Of The Bin
summary: Out of the Bin is an interactive theatre piece that transforms overlooked rubbish scattered across our streets into sound, movement and art — revealing stories of homelessness that too often go unnoticed. Blending physical play, object manipulation and audience involvement, the performance dives into everyday waste we discard without thought. Rubbish is produced endlessly, yet for some, a bin becomes a place of survival — even an…
_description: Out of the Bin is an interactive theatre piece that transforms overlooked rubbish scattered across our streets into sound, movement and art — revealing stories of homelessness that too often go unnoticed. Blending physical play, object manipulation and audience involvement, the performance dives into everyday waste we discard without thought. Rubbish is produced endlessly, yet for some, a bin becomes a place of survival — even an unexpected source of hope. Through these fragments, the piece uncovers moments of resilience, humour and humanity hidden in our cities. As discarded objects are reimagined, Out of the Bin questions what happens when something — or someone — society has thrown away suddenly becomes “valuable.” When trash turns into treasure, does the world truly see it differently? And in that shift, can a person begin to lose themselves? Playful, tender and socially urgent, Out of the Bin invites audiences into a world where waste becomes wonder.

title: Hes Six Years Older Than Me
summary: She is doing an At-home STI kit. Her train of thoughts goes to where they met the first time, what music they were playing and what they were talking about. She is still doing the STI kit. She is thinking of all the sexual encounters she has been in. Their eye colours, their star signs, their ages. She is trying to find the pattern. She is trying to find where it all started. And she is still doing the kit, thinking the instructions…
_description: She is doing an At-home STI kit. Her train of thoughts goes to where they met the first time, what music they were playing and what they were talking about. She is still doing the STI kit. She is thinking of all the sexual encounters she has been in. Their eye colours, their star signs, their ages. She is trying to find the pattern. She is trying to find where it all started. And she is still doing the kit, thinking the instructions are not so easy to follow. This play is a conversation about consent, power dynamics and privilege with arguments for and against the act of stealthing. It is a young woman’s self-reckoning or self-sabotage confronting the questions arising from the sexual encounters she has been in. Within the belief of fixing the problem or whatsoever, she digs into every detail and all things in common that these men had and/or did. ‘He’s six years older than me.’ So was he and so was him. How is that relevant in this situation, in this conversation, in this play?

title: For The Record

Through creating a timeline of music from their lifetime of memories, they uncover whether their lives and loves could have ever worked out differently. They quickly learn that if they are not honest with their feelings, the way out of the room will lead somewhere... unusual, certainly not the…
_description: Nancy and David are dead. And even worse, they are stuck in the same room together for all eternity, unless they can come to an agreement. Through creating a timeline of music from their lifetime of memories, they uncover whether their lives and loves could have ever worked out differently. They quickly learn that if they are not honest with their feelings, the way out of the room will lead somewhere... unusual, certainly not the afterlife they'd expected. For The Record is an emotional and comedic exploration of the afterlife, secrets, and regrets. With a mystical door, cryptic vinyl player, and mysterious puppets, could the-one-that-got-away have ever been The One, if only they had met at the right time? Salt Air Stories is a brand new production company, made up of passionate storytellers who want to explore myths and fables in all of their forms. Join us around the campfire. From the creative minds behind OffFest Nominated comedy, Rambunctious Scallywag:

title: Ryan Mold Resistant
summary: A funny, honest look at what happens when life changes faster than you can keep up. It’s about trying to find your place in a world that won’t stop changing, and laughing about it along the way. My knees click, my patience for influencers has…
_description: A funny, honest look at what happens when life changes faster than you can keep up. From technology that's confusing, to government rules that make no sense, to the creeping reality of getting older — it all feels a bit harder than it used to be! But Resistant isn’t just a rant. It’s about trying to find your place in a world that won’t stop changing, and laughing about it along the way. My knees click, my patience for influencers has gone, and apparently my phone is “retro.” If you’ve ever felt left behind by technology, confused by modern life, or just tired of being told to “embrace the future,” this show is for you. Join me for a night of relatable, warm, and sharply funny stand-up about surviving the madness of modern times — one reluctant update at a time. "Absolutely brilliant" – Nick Helm "Full of very funny and original jokes, a laugh-a-minute" – London Theatre "Keeps a room onside being laugh-out-loud funny. He’s a classic British comedian" – theQR.co.uk

Every performance is unique—chaotic, heartfelt, and created with the whole room. Marc is a West End Old Act of the Year finalist (“naturally funny” – Mark Simmons; “a very funny dude” – Mike OT). Nick is a long-time performer with The Free Association, known for his warmth, quick wit, and razor-sharp improv instincts. Fresh from Lambeth Fringe and now running a popular monthly residency in Elephant and Castle, the show consistently sends audiences home smiling, energised, and a little bit in love with the world again.

When comedy and board games collide, there’s no chance of being bored!

In this new work in progress show, Alice India wants to peel back the secrets we keep from each other and ourselves whether we mean to or not. Exploring what happens when we have no choice but to wear what is different about us on our sleeves and whether change ever really changes us. "Simultaneously goofy and sincere... sharp observations and fast paced ADHD fuelled story telling." ★★★★★ - Neurodiverse Review "Witty and engaging" - The Skinny

title: Stephen And Hamlet Blind And Mad
summary: Let’s face it, we’re all nuts. When you’re a middle-aged, blind obsessive compulsive, though, life can be particularly odd, not to mention confusing and more than a little rude. Having previously traipsed around Edinburgh, Hove, Plymouth and Brighton, London’s gentlest comedian, Stephen Portlock, accompanied by his impeccably well behaved dog Hamlet, and bringing a cuddly Minion in tow, returns to Brighton to leave residents and…
_description: Let’s face it, we’re all nuts. When you’re a middle-aged, blind obsessive compulsive, though, life can be particularly odd, not to mention confusing and more than a little rude. Having previously traipsed around Edinburgh, Hove, Plymouth and Brighton, London’s gentlest comedian, Stephen Portlock, accompanied by his impeccably well behaved dog Hamlet, and bringing a cuddly Minion in tow, returns to Brighton to leave residents and visitors bewildered, befuddled and bemused as he delivers a very ramshackle guide through the highways and byways of life’s weirdness, exploring his ambivalent feelings about identity politics, and musing on how we can just learn to be nicer to one-another. Sweet, sometimes filthy, often embarrassing and surprisingly often true, this is one sentimental journey you will definitely remember.

title: Phil Kay
summary: Legendary Scottish comedian, Phil Kay, returns to Brighton Fringe for 2 nights only. His unforgettable style of live standup will leave you wondering what just happened, as his whirlwind-like humour rips through the room like a tornado, yet tenderly tickles your funny bones. Phil has been crashing onto stages since 1989 with all the energy and fervour of an illegal rave, the improvisation skills of a professional shoplifter, and the…
_description: Legendary Scottish comedian, Phil Kay, returns to Brighton Fringe for 2 nights only. His unforgettable style of live standup will leave you wondering what just happened, as his whirlwind-like humour rips through the room like a tornado, yet tenderly tickles your funny bones. Phil has been crashing onto stages since 1989 with all the energy and fervour of an illegal rave, the improvisation skills of a professional shoplifter, and the comedy timing of a showbiz giant. His crackpot stories, unpredictable asides, and maniacal stage presence set him apart from mainstream comedy circuits, earning him a core following of lifelong devotees. Come and be converted, this one is not-to-be-missed! 'An enthralling, edge-of-the-seat high wire act, when he delivers, the laughs are all the louder' (Guardian) 'It's a case of gripping the side of the seat and holding on' (The Herald) 'Fertile, feral, other-worldly and exploding with energy' (The Scotsman) 'A master of the improvised' (The Skinny)

The Funny Women Award winner 2023, Chortle Best Newcomer Nominee 2024 and DIVA Magazine Queer Comedian of the Year 2025 ponders this and other things. Like should she have just married her teenage boyfriend and become a tradwife instead of trying…

What does it mean to be a feminist while Western civilisation collapses around us? The Funny Women Award winner 2023, Chortle Best Newcomer Nominee 2024 and DIVA Magazine Queer Comedian of the Year 2025 ponders this and other things. Like should she have just married her teenage boyfriend and become a tradwife instead of trying to be a strong independent woman. Come help Kate figure it out. ''Political comedy may yet have found its new messiah'' (The Guardian)

title: Phenomonopoly The Surreal Estate Game Presented By The Mother Wolf Club
summary: Hosted by the pun-loving Minotaur, three audience members become contestants in this surreal estate game show, racing to write the most unhinged fridge magnet poem imaginable. Why? To stop the future of language from devolving into emoji soup, and to topple the evil Uncanny Valley Girl from her dairy-based dystopia: the Tower of Babybel. On their journey they’ll duel with fowl-mouthed giant punk chicken John Cooper Cluck, slow-dance…
_description: Hosted by the pun-loving Minotaur, three audience members become contestants in this surreal estate game show, racing to write the most unhinged fridge magnet poem imaginable. Why? To stop the future of language from devolving into emoji soup, and to topple the evil Uncanny Valley Girl from her dairy-based dystopia: the Tower of Babybel. On their journey they’ll duel with fowl-mouthed giant punk chicken John Cooper Cluck, slow-dance with eel sexologist Pink Freud and his slippery-slick cousin Elvers Presley, and be serenaded by Hal—the anthropomorphic fire alarm with Broadway dreams and rage issues. Part interactive musical, part high-brow panto, 100% the weirdest entertainment of your life.

title: Camilla Borges Be Not Afraid
summary: Camilla has a theory that whoever you played in your school Nativity shapes how your life turns out. She played Mary, so surely when she grew up she became the most important person in the world? That is not quite what happened... Now she is in her 30s and life isn’t quite shaping up to what she was promised. Maybe now if she goes back, she will be able to turn things around. *Queue Little Donkey* Mary is everything, but she is also…
_description: Camilla has a theory that whoever you played in your school Nativity shapes how your life turns out. She played Mary, so surely when she grew up she became the most important person in the world? That is not quite what happened... Now she is in her 30s and life isn’t quite shaping up to what she was promised. Maybe now if she goes back, she will be able to turn things around. *Queue Little Donkey* Mary is everything, but she is also just a girl. Through characters, games and lots (and lots) of audience interaction, Camilla re-tells the story of the Nativity from a slightly different perspective. Expect familiar faces, and a few surprising ones- there definitely was a Lobster present at the birth of Christ? She asks the important questions, such as: Did Mary shave her legs? Was Joseph the original cuck? And, what exactly is Myrrh?? Hopefully, by re-visiting Mary Camilla will finally be able to properly grow up and stop being afraid. A show of pure silliness.

title: Wheel Of Failure
summary: A washed-up game-show host lost his big break in a lawsuit. His wife left. His kids hate him. Now he’s clinging to one last shot at redemption. With only his ten-year-old indentured sidekick, Orphan, and a cardboard cut-out of his ex-wife for support, he hosts a disastrously homemade game show packed with questionable challenges like Whose Drink Is Mine? and Wheel of Shit From My House. Created by real-life father/son duo William and…
_description: A washed-up game-show host lost his big break in a lawsuit. His wife left. His kids hate him. Now he’s clinging to one last shot at redemption. With only his ten-year-old indentured sidekick, Orphan, and a cardboard cut-out of his ex-wife for support, he hosts a disastrously homemade game show packed with questionable challenges like Whose Drink Is Mine? and Wheel of Shit From My House. Created by real-life father/son duo William and Jimmy Kontos, this interactive comedy invites the audience to play along, compete for points, and chase victory—only to discover that in this world, success is impossible and failure is guaranteed. Absurd, dark, and painfully funny, this is a show about pride, desperation, family, and the terrifying hope that maybe, just maybe, this time it will all work out. (It won’t.)

title: Jamie Byrnes Delightful History Of Cigarettes
summary: In Britain today, cigarettes are threated in all directions. Vaping, smoking bans, some other third thing I haven't thought of yet: they all have it in for cigarettes. Please (PLEASE) join Jamie Byrne in learning about the history of cigarettes and why they are a cornerstone of our culture. You'll have fun along the way, and you just might learn something. Jamie is an award winning character comedian who has written for the BBC.

title: Calls From Castlemar
summary: Calls for Castlemar explores the difficult dynamics between three young children's home residents with snappy dialogue, comedic levity, and youthful play. Themes of identity, belonging, forced family, and jealousy are explored in a world where adults just don't seem to care, let alone exist. Based on Canadian play Home for Boys, this story makes it way across the pond with altered narrative to suit a UK audience. It will appeal to…
_description: Calls for Castlemar explores the difficult dynamics between three young children's home residents with snappy dialogue, comedic levity, and youthful play. Themes of identity, belonging, forced family, and jealousy are explored in a world where adults just don't seem to care, let alone exist. Based on Canadian play Home for Boys, this story makes it way across the pond with altered narrative to suit a UK audience. It will appeal to those with or without nostalgia for the 90s, and the hearts of those who have ever lost or longed for someone important in their lives.

title: Racists Recessions And Revolutions
summary: "Democracy isn't power to the people - it's power to the story. And why let the truth get in the way of a good story?" Nadica has it all; a Balkan Mum from a country everyone seems to think shouldn't exist, a British dad who's never around and a post-uni life timed perfectly to be cancelled by Covid. What more could a girl ask for? Well a bit of cash wouldn't hurt. With the rest of her life in ruins, one Lockdown hustle beckons like a…
_description: "Democracy isn't power to the people - it's power to the story. And why let the truth get in the way of a good story?" Nadica has it all; a Balkan Mum from a country everyone seems to think shouldn't exist, a British dad who's never around and a post-uni life timed perfectly to be cancelled by Covid. What more could a girl ask for? Well a bit of cash wouldn't hurt. With the rest of her life in ruins, one Lockdown hustle beckons like a siren's call; lying online, for fun and for profit. Join Nadica as she masterminds some of the Internet's most viral headlines: Could Covid Be A Lie? Was The Election Stolen? And What Really Went Down In That Prison Cell... As her work takes her from the ideological fringes and into the halls of power, truth and lies blend and bash and the combined powers of Racists, Recessions and Revolutions roll together to destabilise the very fabric of society as we know it - no spoilers, but we all knew it would happen eventually right?

title: Mile Die Club
summary: Your evening begins with a flight. As the Brighton i360 lifts you 450ft above the city, fizz flows, the view is breath-taking... and something is very wrong. You’ll descend into the i360 dining room for an indulgent three-course dinner, where the suspects mingle between the tables. Between courses, you’ll interrogate them, challenge their alibis, uncover clues and compare notes with your fellow guests. And before the final course is…
_description: Your evening begins with a flight. As the Brighton i360 lifts you 450ft above the city, fizz flows, the view is breath-taking... and something is very wrong. You’ll descend into the i360 dining room for an indulgent three-course dinner, where the suspects mingle between the tables. Between courses, you’ll interrogate them, challenge their alibis, uncover clues and compare notes with your fellow guests. And before the final course is cleared, you’ll need to decide: who deserves to be exposed as a murderer? This is not theatre you watch. This is a crime you solve. What’s included: A premium three-course dinner An immersive, story-led murder mystery experience Exclusive ascent on the Brighton i360 Live interaction with a professional cast Fizz on arrival and wine with the meal A final reveal — and bragging rights if you get it right Perfect for thrill-seekers, special occasions, corporate entertaining, or anyone who believes dinner should come with a body count.

title: Spiralizers
summary: Spiralizers is a chaotic, cathartic four-way split bill born straight out of a WhatsApp group. When comedians Oni, Caroline, Vyome and Selam realised their group chat was basically a live feed of collective spiralling, they did the only sensible thing — turned it into a show. Expect sharp stand-up, unfiltered oversharing, and three comedians processing life in real time. From existential dread to everyday absurdities, Spiralizers takes…
_description: Spiralizers is a chaotic, cathartic four-way split bill born straight out of a WhatsApp group. When comedians Oni, Caroline, Vyome and Selam realised their group chat was basically a live feed of collective spiralling, they did the only sensible thing — turned it into a show. Expect sharp stand-up, unfiltered oversharing, and three comedians processing life in real time. From existential dread to everyday absurdities, Spiralizers takes the kind of thoughts that usually stay in the group chat and throws them onto the stage. Come watch the spiral unfold live at Brighton Fringe.

_description: James used to live in Russia. Just packed up and went. Now he's back and can't decide what to have for lunch. Sharp, uncomfortable stand-up about when being emotionally intelligent just means you can explain exactly why you're fucked. Russia stories that make your life seem normal. Relationship material that hits like a personal attack. When did growing up become giving in? Work in progress.

It’s packed full of personal and professional stories and tales about friends family and his particular take on the world. There are pearls of wisdom weaved into the show, all be it accidentally it must be said, within the funny stories, jokes and general mischief that this show is . He’s a very serious none serious person, and is uniquely qualified to be…
_description: Fear of Failure is a race through comedian Rodney Beckford’s life to this point. It’s packed full of personal and professional stories and tales about friends family and his particular take on the world. There are pearls of wisdom weaved into the show, all be it accidentally it must be said, within the funny stories, jokes and general mischief that this show is . He’s a very serious none serious person, and is uniquely qualified to be your host and guide for this most personal of stand up comedy shows. You're going to be amused, surprised and laugh out loud as you're guided through the comedians trails and tribulations. You'll be sorry it's over, delighted you came and perplexed as to why he's not on a bigger and better stage.

title: Almost Fine
summary: I’m fine... I think. A smile appears. A body follows—or tries to. Almost Fine is a 30-minute solo contemporary dance created and performed by Taiwanese dancer A-Yao (Peishao Huang). If you like physical theatre or contemporary dance, come experience this intimate, close-up performance. Small actions—walking, sitting, scrolling, breathing—begin to slip. The work reveals the cracks beneath how we try to “be normal.” It shifts between…
_description: I’m fine... I think. A smile appears. A body follows—or tries to. Almost Fine is a 30-minute solo contemporary dance created and performed by Taiwanese dancer A-Yao (Peishao Huang). If you like physical theatre or contemporary dance, come experience this intimate, close-up performance. Small actions—walking, sitting, scrolling, breathing—begin to slip. The work reveals the cracks beneath how we try to “be normal.” It shifts between control and impulse, clarity and chaos. Presented at the 2024 Taiwan Jumping Islands Dance Festival: “Stunning movements! Even his fingertips radiate sensuality and tension — raw and honest.”- from Dance Critic “I couldn’t look away. Every second was utterly gripping.” - from audience At its core, Almost Fine explores identity—gender, culture, and how we are shaped by forces we rarely question. It is not about breaking. It is not about control. It is about living in the space of “almost”— the fragile, honest moment before we find our footing again.

Eclectic, positive and dance inducing - our aim is to introduce you to the very best independent artists from the UK festival circuit. We’re so…
_description: Welcome to the Scallywag Party, a home for all lovers of good times and upbeat live music. Headlining our fringe spectacular is the fantastic high energy, brass driven 9 piece, Chainska Brassika plus Bars Against Humanity, Belver, DJ Soleil, Lord Disorderly, Laura Loops and more acts tba. Eclectic, positive and dance inducing - our aim is to introduce you to the very best independent artists from the UK festival circuit. We’re so pleased to have been invited back to Caravanserai on this first Saturday of the festival and we are pulling out all the stops and create a truly spectacular scallywag party not to be missed. Alongside amazing live music and DJ’s expect face painting, walkabouts, laughter, tomfoolery and togetherness. Scallywag Party have over a decade of experience running festivals, stages and events, championing grass roots acts and building a light hearted, live music loving community. Come join the family and get involved, WE bring the music but YOU make the party!

Let Courtney penetrate you with her newest stand up hour and take you on a journey of what it means to have and to lose a friendship. A show that will make you laugh, cringe and text your best friend immediately.

title: Ratmageddon
summary: Dive headfirst into RATMAGEDDON (Work in Progress), a surreal, high-energy comedy adventure from RatBag. Fresh from Soho Theatre’s Comedy Lab, this work-in-progress blends sketch, clown, and character comedy to create a feral, apocalyptic world where the audience becomes gloriously complicit in the chaos. Expect big characters, bold choices, rapid transformations, and joyful theatrical mayhem as two performers push each other right to…
_description: Dive headfirst into RATMAGEDDON (Work in Progress), a surreal, high-energy comedy adventure from RatBag. Fresh from Soho Theatre’s Comedy Lab, this work-in-progress blends sketch, clown, and character comedy to create a feral, apocalyptic world where the audience becomes gloriously complicit in the chaos. Expect big characters, bold choices, rapid transformations, and joyful theatrical mayhem as two performers push each other right to the edge. With musical madness, playful rule-breaking, and unpredictable twists, RATMAGEDDON (Work in Progress) invites you not just to watch but to join the disorder. Unashamedly silly, wildly imaginative, and dangerously fun, this is cult comedy in the making.

summary: The last remaining members of international pop sensation Get Real suffer a final humiliation at the dilapidated Funworld Holiday Park, and it’s the final straw for founding members Chaz and KK. It’s time for the mother of all comebacks...a musical! Follow the group as they reform, overcome their past differences, and reclaim the glory of stardom! All references to 90s pop bands are purely circumstantial! This new comedy follows hot on…
_description: The last remaining members of international pop sensation Get Real suffer a final humiliation at the dilapidated Funworld Holiday Park, and it’s the final straw for founding members Chaz and KK. It’s time for the mother of all comebacks...a musical! Follow the group as they reform, overcome their past differences, and reclaim the glory of stardom! All references to 90s pop bands are purely circumstantial! This new comedy follows hot on the heels of three previous sell-out productions by Out Cast. Their spring 2025 show was a Bespoke Black Books pick of the Fringe: “You’ll be in for a treat... a riot of original songs, comedic chaos and exaggerated characters.” Additional preview show on Thursday 30th April - tickets at buytickets.at/outcastbrighton

title: Saving Myself
company: 20 South Street in association with Grey Swan
summary: Set over one night, SAVING MYSELF centres on Lilly, a young woman alone in her flat, surrounded by books, half-remembered rituals, and an unlit candle. Since childhood, Lilly has been blamed for things going wrong: pets that disappeared, toys that broke, the uneasy silences that followed her through her family home. Over time, these stories became fact, shaping how others treated her and how she learned to understand herself. After a…
_description: Set over one night, SAVING MYSELF centres on Lilly, a young woman alone in her flat, surrounded by books, half-remembered rituals, and an unlit candle. Since childhood, Lilly has been blamed for things going wrong: pets that disappeared, toys that broke, the uneasy silences that followed her through her family home. Over time, these stories became fact, shaping how others treated her and how she learned to understand herself. After a violent assault, Lilly reaches breaking point. With no one left to turn to, she fixates on the idea that something inside her - a demon, an inheritance, or the damage left by trauma - has to be destroyed. Moving between realism, poetry, folklore, and ritual, the play unfolds in real time as Lilly fights to take control of her own body and story. Raw, darkly comic, and unsettling, SAVING MYSELF asks what happens when care is replaced by superstition, and what it costs a woman to save herself.

The boys (Brad, Chad, and Sven) are forced to forego their frivolities when a fallen tree blocks their route, forcing them to spend the night in a mysterious haven: Little Town Blue. At midnight, this seemingly idyllic town rears its ugly head, and the three boys must battle their way through til morning. Little Town Blue is a characterful, hilarious, and relentless hour of physical comedy that takes the audience on a madcap journey through rural America at breakneck speed.

_description: This interactive comedy-cabaret-poetry extravaganza blends sharp wordplay, visual gags and mind-boggling trivia into an unmissable hour of laughs, learning and surprises. Performed by neurospicy Renaissance man, Ben Dali, the show draws on a life spanning many careers, from hypnotist to primary teacher. Expect pieces to shift rapidly between styles and moods without warning, from infectious sincerity to unapologetic filth. Highlights include a journey through English’s finest ‘Humdinger Words’; innocent childhood poems juxtaposed with depraved adult sequels; a visual comedy treat comparing country outlines to their outlandish doppelgängers. Along the way, Dali relives his diverse skillset, including genuine hypnotic effects and mentalism delivered in improvised rhyme. Designed for attentive, intellectually curious audiences who celebrate playful language, HWfL&F is ideal for anyone who enjoys comedy that subverts genre and expectations, delivering knockout punchlines in every bout.

title: Humdinger Words For Whippersnappers And Guttersnipes
summary: Step into the poetic classroom of the UK's most entertaining supply teacher, as he welcomes you into a world of witty wordplay, tantalising trivia, megaLOLs and even rhyming magic! Humdinger Words for Whippersnappers and Guttersnipes is a PG hour of comedy-cabaret poetry led by stage hypnotist turned teacher, Ben Dali. Presented as a school day, this is a fast-paced journey through elements of the KS2 and KS3 curriculum. 'Mr Dali'…
_description: Step into the poetic classroom of the UK's most entertaining supply teacher, as he welcomes you into a world of witty wordplay, tantalising trivia, megaLOLs and even rhyming magic! Humdinger Words for Whippersnappers and Guttersnipes is a PG hour of comedy-cabaret poetry led by stage hypnotist turned teacher, Ben Dali. Presented as a school day, this is a fast-paced journey through elements of the KS2 and KS3 curriculum. 'Mr Dali' combines poetry, comedy and insights into his eclectic background to deliver epic poems he wrote aged 7, a balloon-modelling tutorial poem, a joyful exploration of English's finest 'Humdinger Words', and genuine improvised rhyming mind reading. Designed for intellectually curious and creative 7–14 year-olds, the whole family will leave entertained, educated and inspired. The show includes a supportive Show-and-Tell segment, where children are invited to perform their own poems. So, budding young wordsmiths desirous of a platform: come prepared to share.

title: Kept
summary: KEPT: What Remains When the Noise Stops A visceral theatre piece tracing a life rebuilt through rhythm, memory, and art. From a young man “kept” in the charged intimacy of a Chicago summer to the grief that follows years later in Amsterdam, KEPT journeys through desire, loss, and restoration. Drawing on years in percussive theatre and storytelling, it transforms lived experience into sound — using rhythm and story to explore the ways…
_description: KEPT: What Remains When the Noise Stops A visceral theatre piece tracing a life rebuilt through rhythm, memory, and art. From a young man “kept” in the charged intimacy of a Chicago summer to the grief that follows years later in Amsterdam, KEPT journeys through desire, loss, and restoration. Drawing on years in percussive theatre and storytelling, it transforms lived experience into sound — using rhythm and story to explore the ways we break, stray, and begin again, and the things that refuse to disappear.

title: A Marriage Of Inconvenience
summary: Join best friends and rising comedy stars Harsh and Abi for a split bill that explores the intersection of Type-A ambition, queer identity, and the chaotic search for love. United by their nerdy neuroses and a spectacular track record of romantic failures, the pair present A Marriage of Inconvenience: a show about the logistical nightmare of modern dating, and their pact to enter a sexless lavender marriage if they're still single by…
_description: Join best friends and rising comedy stars Harsh and Abi for a split bill that explores the intersection of Type-A ambition, queer identity, and the chaotic search for love. United by their nerdy neuroses and a spectacular track record of romantic failures, the pair present A Marriage of Inconvenience: a show about the logistical nightmare of modern dating, and their pact to enter a sexless lavender marriage if they're still single by 40. Until then, they're saying "I do" to the next best thing: awkwardly sharing the stage for half an hour each.

_description: I'm sorry to disappoint you all: Two friends tangled together. Cocooned in their high rise flat and swallowed by the vastness of the city, an unravelling begins. Dynamic contemporary dance unfolds against swirling projected landscapes, set to an eclectic, layered sound score by composer Phoebe Coco. Drawing on the duo’s shared mental health struggles and frustrations with the current climate for artists, I’m sorry to disappoint you all is a touching exploration of isolation, futility and the search for deeper connection. In This Room: An inquisitive, intergenerational group of local artists, makers and creators have teamed up with Charis and Mette of The 12th Floor to experiment, create and connect. What you see is the result of 3 workshops full of play, hard work and dedication. The 12th Floor thanks this wonderful group for taking a leap of faith with them. The 12th Floor are proud recipients of the South East Dance Fringe Roots Award.

title: Brighton School Of Samba
summary: Brighton School of Samba – Carnival Comes to the Streets A high-energy explosion of Brazilian rhythm and dance, bringing the spirit of Rio Carnival to Brighton. Brighton School of Samba (BSS) is the city’s original Rio-style samba school, delivering vibrant, high-energy performances inspired by the world-famous Brazilian carnival. Formed in 2007, BSS is a not-for-profit, community-led group built on the traditions of Rio’s “escolas de…
_description: Brighton School of Samba – Carnival Comes to the Streets A high-energy explosion of Brazilian rhythm and dance, bringing the spirit of Rio Carnival to Brighton. Brighton School of Samba (BSS) is the city’s original Rio-style samba school, delivering vibrant, high-energy performances inspired by the world-famous Brazilian carnival. Formed in 2007, BSS is a not-for-profit, community-led group built on the traditions of Rio’s “escolas de samba”, combining powerful percussion (bateria), dance, and carnival performance. The group regularly performs at major events across Brighton and beyond, including Brighton Pride, Burning of the Clocks, and Brighton Fringe. This performance features a full samba bateria of drums alongside dancers, creating an immersive street-carnival atmosphere. Expect driving rhythms, colourful costumes, and an infectious energy that gets audiences moving. BSS is open to all and reflects Brighton’s inclusivity.

Gareth did not spend the First World War in the Merchant Navy, nor has he ever been shipwrecked, run a farm or tried to emigrate to Australia - but he's found a book of poems by his great-grandfather, and he's determined to find something they have in common. _description: Cyril Gwynn (1897-1988), also known as The Bard Of Gower, was “a tall, sunburnt, unsmiling farmer”. Gareth Gwynn (1983-Present) is none of these things. Gareth did not spend the First World War in the Merchant Navy, nor has he ever been shipwrecked, run a farm or tried to emigrate to Australia - but he's found a book of poems by his great-grandfather, and he's determined to find something they have in common. This is also a work-in-progress comedy show about living in a city when your ancestors thought tarmac was all a bit much. Gwynn (1983 ed.) has written for The News Quiz, The Now Show, Have I Got News For You and many more. He is the co-writer of Ankle Tag (BBC Radio 4), Tourist Trap (BBC One Wales) and The Goodies: The Big Ben Theory (An Audible Special). On BBC Radio 4 he presented Gareth Gwynn Hasn’t Fin- ("Hilarious" - The Guardian) and is the co-presenter of the multi-award nominated comedy/history podcast I'm So Not Over It.

title: Mitch Benn The Lehrer Effect
venue_name: Laughing Horse @ a&o Hostel bar (Front Room)
summary: When, years ago, a teenage Mitch discovered American comic songwriting legend Tom Lehrer, the course of his life was changed forever. Mitch looks at the legacy of the elusive Lehrer, the creative debt he, and all those who mix humour and melody, owe to him and performs some of the more Lehreresque of his own songs. Since Mr. Lehrer placed his entire catalogue in public domain, expect a few Mitch Benn-ified covers of some of Tom’s most…
_description: When, years ago, a teenage Mitch discovered American comic songwriting legend Tom Lehrer, the course of his life was changed forever. Mitch looks at the legacy of the elusive Lehrer, the creative debt he, and all those who mix humour and melody, owe to him and performs some of the more Lehreresque of his own songs. Since Mr. Lehrer placed his entire catalogue in public domain, expect a few Mitch Benn-ified covers of some of Tom’s most famous creations as well. Tom Lehrer died at the age of 97, immediately before the premiere of the show in Edinburgh, which means that the show is now a heart-felt eulogy rather than living tribute to the artist.

summary: Dr. Kara Kibara is a self-proclaimed and successful online dating expert. She has helped thousands of people find love, but she herself is still single in her 40s. In this interactive cabaret show, Dr. Kara goes back to her past and reflects on what went wrong (or maybe right) that caused her to still be loveless. She shares her insights on dating, relationships, and love, and she offers her advice on how to find the one. This is a…
_description: Dr. Kara Kibara is a self-proclaimed and successful online dating expert. She has helped thousands of people find love, but she herself is still single in her 40s. In this interactive cabaret show, Dr. Kara goes back to her past and reflects on what went wrong (or maybe right) that caused her to still be loveless. She shares her insights on dating, relationships, and love, and she offers her advice on how to find the one. This is a funny, interactive, heartwarming, and thought-provoking cabaret show featuring original songs, snippets of familiar 80's and 90's tunes as well as choreography, projections and monologues that will leave the audience laughing, crying, and inspired. It's A must-see for anyone who has ever been on a date or who has ever dreamed of finding love.

title: 2Shoulderpads Galaxy Train
summary: A musical that sold out its entire run at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe and won the Spirit of the Fringe Award. Based on classic Japanese literature, this poetic work uses an extreme minimal costume concept: male performers wear only “two shoulder pads.” There is nothing obscene—laughter at the wiggling bottoms soon gives way to imagination, and by the final scene many are moved to tears. Exploring universal themes of life, death and…
_description: A musical that sold out its entire run at the Edinburgh Festival Fringe and won the Spirit of the Fringe Award. Based on classic Japanese literature, this poetic work uses an extreme minimal costume concept: male performers wear only “two shoulder pads.” There is nothing obscene—laughter at the wiggling bottoms soon gives way to imagination, and by the final scene many are moved to tears. Exploring universal themes of life, death and parting, the show blends humour, live music and kabuki-inspired physicality into a joyful theatrical experience for audiences of all ages.

summary: The Quest for Love, Murder and Revenge in Space is a fantasy adventure showcasing the epic– oh, wait, no! It's actually a dramatic romance demonstrating the power of– oh, still wrong? That's right, it's a murder mystery in which– Wrong again? A rollicking western, perhaps? A sci-fi saga? The truth is it's none of them... and all of them! Each night our protégé arrives to find the seasoned cast in shambles. It appears that, despite…
_description: The Quest for Love, Murder and Revenge in Space is a fantasy adventure showcasing the epic– oh, wait, no! It's actually a dramatic romance demonstrating the power of– oh, still wrong? That's right, it's a murder mystery in which– Wrong again? A rollicking western, perhaps? A sci-fi saga? The truth is it's none of them... and all of them! Each night our protégé arrives to find the seasoned cast in shambles. It appears that, despite perfectly reasonable instructions from the director, the show hasn't quite been settled on yet. One wants a romance while the other wants sci-fi and, in the end, it's easier to let the audience decide. So, we let them. The result? Usually, a very endearing, fully-improvised tale about the power of friendship, predictable romances, magical items, gunslinging, and a wild host of one-night-only characters. You couldn't write the stuff! Main image courtesy of Pete Burnett.

title: Hive Work In Progress
summary: Welcome to the HIVE. This darkly comedic clown show with live electronic soundtracking takes you on an eerie and whimsical journey through the life cycle of a bee. Guided live on Zoom by the passionate teaching team at the Jing Institute, this uplifting mini session gives you a powerful introduction to the foundations of massage. In a supportive space, you will learn simple, effective techniques you can use immediately to boost…
_description: Step away from the everyday and dive into The Art of Touch, a dynamic one hour online workshop that brings the excitement of learning massage straight to your living room. Guided live on Zoom by the passionate teaching team at the Jing Institute, this uplifting mini session gives you a powerful introduction to the foundations of massage. In a supportive space, you will learn simple, effective techniques you can use immediately to boost wellbeing for yourself and those you care about. This is not just relaxation; it is your first taste of a world where mindful touch, creativity and clinical skill come alive. Whether you are curious, craving something new, or exploring a future in massage therapy, this lively online experience is the perfect place to begin. With expert guidance from Jing Directors Rachel Fairweather and Meghan Mari, you will feel inspired, energised and supported as you learn, connect and unwind. Everyone is welcome and no experience is needed.

_description: Come on a jungle adventure with 'Melody Mermaid' from 'Ukeboxbaby' and her 'Ukeboxbaby' friends. There will be chances to play the ukulele on stage, giant balloons, ribbon sticks, water guns, bubbles and more. In addition, at this show, Melody will invite you to play along to her songs as an audience, for the first time, so if you have any shakers, ukuleles or any instrument at home, please bring them along. Pls note this is optional. This show is designed to inspire children to enjoy the magic of music by learning an instrument or singing. Music is proven to be so good for children's cognitive development - even more so in today's screen-time heavy world. 'Melody Mermaid' has starred in 'Ukeboxbaby' shows on main stages at Camp Bestival, Latitude Festival, Elderflower Fields Festival, in Soho Beach House, at Komedia Brighton and many more. 'Melody Mermaid' from 'Ukeboxbaby' is a believer that music is the strongest form of magic. She has also launched a ukulele story kid's book.

title: Know Yourself

Directed by dance artist Zhiyuan HUANGcentred on the question of what it means to truly know oneself, the piece explores the individual’s journey through pressure, memory, uncertainty, and the quiet voices of the inner self. In this work, the body becomes both the medium of expression and the object of observation. Movement…
_description: Know Yourself is a contemporary dance work about self-reflection, bodily awareness, and inner reconstruction. Directed by dance artist Zhiyuan HUANGcentred on the question of what it means to truly know oneself, the piece explores the individual’s journey through pressure, memory, uncertainty, and the quiet voices of the inner self. In this work, the body becomes both the medium of expression and the object of observation. Movement begins with subtle gestures, breath, touch, and shifts of weight, gradually developing into more intense physical tension, resistance, and spatial exploration. These changing movement qualities reflect the struggles, confusion, confrontation, and awakening that often accompany the process of self-discovery. Rather than offering a fixed answer, Know Yourself invites the audience into an open inner journey. As external expectations, social identities, and imposed labels begin to fall away, the work asks: can we truly listen to the voice within the body?

title: Tom Whiston Night Mail Work In Progress
summary: In the sorting room of a remote post office, a naughty 1940s postman passes time with pranks, letters and telegrams from the future, while waiting for news of the war. It's a fast-talking, absurdist, character comedy with elements of clowning, and packed with jokes, both sensical and non. Tim Whiston of the GPO (like his favourite singer: "G'Peter Ondre") plays games and pranks with the audience to pass the time, while checking his…
_description: In the sorting room of a remote post office, a naughty 1940s postman passes time with pranks, letters and telegrams from the future, while waiting for news of the war. It's a fast-talking, absurdist, character comedy with elements of clowning, and packed with jokes, both sensical and non. Tim Whiston of the GPO (like his favourite singer: "G'Peter Ondre") plays games and pranks with the audience to pass the time, while checking his wireless for news of his sister's RAF squadron caught behind enemy lines. Alas, instead, it's a broadcast of The Pirates of Penzance - and not even one with a conductor he respects. Ultimately, the show is a commentary on the way in which we consume information in the modern era - either anxiety-inducing, distressing news from mainstream sources (Tim's wireless), or bizarre stream-of-consciousness absurdity, teeming out of social media (the telegram machine). How we cope - through jokes, through drugs, through despair - is up to us.

_description: 'A show for burnt-out millennials who now understand why they liked Mulan so much as a child to feel seen, as well as their Guardian-reading aunt who will come away with a catchy definition of what non-binary actually means.' The Whole Time is a standup comedy show, with a mix of autobiographical and observational humour. The show focuses on the recurring theme of realising things about my identity far later in life than I should have done, covering topics like queer dating / avoiding rock climbing, awkward therapy appointments, tedious gender clinic forms, being a nonbinary mess and just wishing to be a rat.

_description: Distinguished guests, lovers and exes, dreamers and ghosters – hold on tight as we try to let go. Tonight, Razmatastique, your hopelessly romantic French-accented emcee, brings past lovers back to the Closure Cabaret for one final performance. From bad-boy playground pirates to emotionally unavailable illusionists, these heartbreakers return to the stage – jazz-handed into public inspection – to flaunt their most dazzling skills and questionable charm. Created and performed by Maria Ansdell, this absurd clown show blends character comedy, heartfelt storytelling and riotous audience participation to turn longing into laughs. "The room thrums with laughter... and encourages uplifting musings on the importance of self worth" - ★★★★ A Youngish Perspective

Picking apart stories from history and her own life - and occasionally bursting into song - from Queen…
_description: After a sell-out Edinburgh debut, Kate-Lois Elliott (British Writers' Guild Award Nominee, British Comedy Guide Awards, Funny Women Content Creator Nominee, The Times Best Jokes Of The Fringe) is back with a new show about modern monogamy, fertility, Gingham fetishes and how 'the good old olden days’ weren’t even that good, actually. Picking apart stories from history and her own life - and occasionally bursting into song - from Queen Victoria’s alleged sex mania, to the real origin of “the average Joe,” to Elliott’s own deranged obsession with making it into the tabloids, this is a show for anyone who’s ever wondered whether to lean in, burn it all down, or take up cross-stitch and see what happens. Music by Katie Pritchard & Kate-Lois Elliott

title: Saxophone Piano Classics Duo Contrasts
summary: Duo “Contrasts” returns to Brighton Fridge with new program covering fiery classics, lyrical gems, and original compositions. Formed by saxophonist and pianist with extensive chamber and solo experience, the duo focuses on expanding the classical repertoire for saxophone while preserving the clarity and balance of traditional chamber music. The program features carefully curated works from the 19th and 20th centuries alongside newly…
_description: Duo “Contrasts” returns to Brighton Fridge with new program covering fiery classics, lyrical gems, and original compositions. Formed by saxophonist and pianist with extensive chamber and solo experience, the duo focuses on expanding the classical repertoire for saxophone while preserving the clarity and balance of traditional chamber music. The program features carefully curated works from the 19th and 20th centuries alongside newly written pieces by the performers, created to highlight rhythmic precision and expressive depth. Each composition has been selected to demonstrate the versatility of the saxophone within a classical setting, from cantabile lines reminiscent of vocal arias to agile, technically demanding passages. The piano part is treated as an equal partner, shaping texture and structure rather than simply accompanying. Duo “Contrasts” is aiming to make classical music accessible without compromising artistic standards, welcoming questions after the concert.

Bigger than the stetsons in Dallas and with more more energy than if you chugged a year's supply of Tizer, we are going large on the eighties! Expect 80s pop, disco, indie, dance, alt, power ballads plus 80s visuals, dance-offs, confetti and all the usual chaos that those funsters at Age Against The Machine and Club de Fromage bring to all their shows. Bring your leg warmers, bring your crew (don't bring your boom box), it will get messy as we sing, dance, laugh (and maybe cry) through the best tunes that the 80s had to offer. So get ready to have the time of your life and remember: nobody puts Baby in a corner (especially here, as the venue is entirely round). Please note, while this is Age Against The Machine event. we are not applying our usual 30 plus age limit, in keeping with the policy of the Fringe

_description: Cut A Shine Ceilidh Klub Kaput at Caravanserai Get ready for 3 hours of ceilidh, hoedown and barn dances with the Cut A Shine Barn Dance Band. The band host monthly dances in London, and are taking to the road to tour our dance show across the country. About The Dancing Although called many things; Ceilidh, Country Dance, Barn Dance, Ceili Dance, Square Dance, Set Dancing - one thing is certain, it's all "traditional" and how people would entertain themselves before TV and raves. We have a live band on stage to provide the music, and a "Dance Caller", who is expert at teaching you all the simple and fun moves. First we learn the dance by walking through it with the caller, then the band strike up, and we dance to the music. You don't need any experience, just willingness to have a go and have fun.

title: Embassy Court Tours
summary: Walking tours of Embassy Court lasting around 90 minutes. Includes visits to a flat, the roof terrace, a reinstated modernist kitchen, a mural, a collection of artefacts in an 11th floor meeting room, as well as the front and rear of the building. The entrance of Embassy Court includes a mural, the blue plaque of former resident, Edward Zeff MBE, and Croix de Guerre awarded for his bravery after WW2.

"rousingly enjoyable" ★★★★ Three Weeks Playfully straddling categories of circus and a ‘serious’ classical music concert, our innovative ensemble take you on world tour of rarely heard music for three pianists playing one piano TOGETHER!!! Our musical programme visits Russia, Turkey, Switzerland and Hollywood USA before returning to our ‘bonnie wee’ Scotland for the GRANDE finale. Assembled from award-winning alumni of the Royal Conservatoire of Scotland, a captivating spectacle pushing the limits of what a piano can do! Programme includes a toy piano and 6-hand arrangements of Bach’s Toccata and Fugue in D minor, Mozart’s Rondo alla Turka, Scottish folk songs and a comic performance of Sousa’s all-American Stars and Stripes Forever. Matthew Shiel is award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera. Matthew will conduct his acclaimed Candlelight Orchestra from the piano as Mozart and Bach did for an unforgettable evening of musical elegance.

title: Ellie Ross Mrs Gary Breath
summary: Annie Hughes is happily single, gainfully employed and financially... fine. But all that changes on the night of her 34th birthday when her parents make her an offer she can't refuse. Even if she really, definitely should. Part romcom, part social autopsy, this brand new solo play from Ellie Ross (BBC Radio 5 Live, i Paper's 50 Best Jokes of the Fringe, Leicester Square Theatre's SketchOff!) satirises our obsession with marriage and…
_description: Annie Hughes is happily single, gainfully employed and financially... fine. But all that changes on the night of her 34th birthday when her parents make her an offer she can't refuse. Even if she really, definitely should. Part romcom, part social autopsy, this brand new solo play from Ellie Ross (BBC Radio 5 Live, i Paper's 50 Best Jokes of the Fringe, Leicester Square Theatre's SketchOff!) satirises our obsession with marriage and ticking life's boxes. Big-hearted, big-character storytelling about the cost of fitting in - and the freedom of opting out.

_description: Louie Falls loves videogames. He's written a quiz book, he has gaming tattoos and even has a useless degree in videogame culture. So join him for his videogame stand up show, as he shares his knowledge of the greatest hobby in the world, and its unusual history. What was the first videogame? Are there actually millions of copies of E.T. buried in the desert? Can Doom really run on a pregnancy test? Come and find out! Maybe that videogame degree wasn't so useless after all.

title: Natal Attraction
summary: The debut solo show from the creator of THE MOTHERHOOD PROJECT (Battersea Arts Centre) ★★★★ The I Paper ★★★★ The Stage ★★★★★ West End Best Friend Winner of the Applause Bursary 2026 “I want to spend my forties eating poppadoms in my knickers, not pretending to care that someone went down a slide.” Natal Attraction is a wickedly funny solo show about motherhood, “barrenhood,” and the expectation that women should want to nurture at all…
_description: The debut solo show from the creator of THE MOTHERHOOD PROJECT (Battersea Arts Centre) ★★★★ The I Paper ★★★★ The Stage ★★★★★ West End Best Friend Winner of the Applause Bursary 2026 “I want to spend my forties eating poppadoms in my knickers, not pretending to care that someone went down a slide.” Natal Attraction is a wickedly funny solo show about motherhood, “barrenhood,” and the expectation that women should want to nurture at all costs. Through five vividly drawn women from across the UK, the show rips into what happens when desire, doubt, and duty collide with the ticking clock. Mischievous, sharp, and quietly resonant, Natal Attraction shines a light on the messy, contradictory realities of the female experience - and asks who we’re really trying to please, and why.

title: Tabula Rasa
summary: Four people. One room. Four chairs. One table. No doors. No exit. No memory of how they arrived, and no certainty they’ll ever leave. An absurd, psychological pressure cooker where identities slip, alliances dissolve, and the familiar turns feral. Panic fizzles into laughter; play becomes ritual; every choice counts. As time ticks on, the four discover that the greatest threat might not be the room at all, but the versions of…
_description: Four people. One room. Four chairs. One table. No doors. No exit. No memory of how they arrived, and no certainty they’ll ever leave. An absurd, psychological pressure cooker where identities slip, alliances dissolve, and the familiar turns feral. Panic fizzles into laughter; play becomes ritual; every choice counts. As time ticks on, the four discover that the greatest threat might not be the room at all, but the versions of themselves it drags to the surface. People are hell. There will be blood. A comedy. Kind of. TABULA RASA invites you to witness a world wiped clean, where meaning is unstable, reality is negotiable, and starting again is never as simple as it sounds.

title: Alice Keys Rocket Fan
summary: Alice Keys has fallen in love. And it’s going well! And now it isn’t. And now it is again. Join Alice as she guides you through the highs and lows of love, told in the most universal language of all. A language that runs through our blood from the moment we are born. This language is, of course, the songs of Elton John. Expect humour, silliness, tears, glittering glasses and plenty of (optional) singalongs drawn from Elton’s iconic…
_description: Alice Keys has fallen in love. And it’s going well! And now it isn’t. And now it is again. Join Alice as she guides you through the highs and lows of love, told in the most universal language of all. A language that runs through our blood from the moment we are born. This language is, of course, the songs of Elton John. Expect humour, silliness, tears, glittering glasses and plenty of (optional) singalongs drawn from Elton’s iconic back catalogue. Where will Alice’s yellow brick road lead her? Will her story end happily ever after? Will her costumes fit behind the piano? Find the answers to all this and more at Rocket Fan, a love story.

title: The Art Of Touch A Journey Into Massage
company: Jing Institute of Massage and Complementary Medicine
summary: Step away from the rush of everyday life and immerse yourself in The Art of Touch, a blissful Brighton Fringe workshop celebrating the beauty of connection and the craft of massage. Guided by the renowned Jing Institute, you’ll spend three restorative hours discovering the principles of professional massage in a warm, nurturing space. Give and receive deeply soothing treatments while learning effective, hands-on techniques you can use…
_description: Step away from the rush of everyday life and immerse yourself in The Art of Touch, a blissful Brighton Fringe workshop celebrating the beauty of connection and the craft of massage. Guided by the renowned Jing Institute, you’ll spend three restorative hours discovering the principles of professional massage in a warm, nurturing space. Give and receive deeply soothing treatments while learning effective, hands-on techniques you can use straight away. More than relaxation, this is an invitation to explore a new path, whether you’re curious or considering a career in massage therapy, it’s the perfect introduction to the skills, philosophy, and joy of holistic touch. Under the expert guidance of Jing Directors Rachel Fairweather and Meghan Mari, you’ll be supported every step of the way. Come alone or with a friend; everyone is welcome. Leave feeling inspired, grounded, and gently transformed, a few radiant hours that could spark a whole new journey.

title: Ghost Light
summary: “I think if you’re going to write a ghost story... you must experience ghosts for yourself.” In 1865 Henry Webster joins the notorious London Ghost Club, hoping to meet and be inspired by Charles Dickens. There he meets Edward Price who draws Henry into investigating a haunting at his own lodging house, belonging to the widowed Mia Sands. At night, the ghosts of two young children wander the house in search of light and safety - while…
_description: “I think if you’re going to write a ghost story... you must experience ghosts for yourself.” In 1865 Henry Webster joins the notorious London Ghost Club, hoping to meet and be inspired by Charles Dickens. There he meets Edward Price who draws Henry into investigating a haunting at his own lodging house, belonging to the widowed Mia Sands. At night, the ghosts of two young children wander the house in search of light and safety - while fearing what waits for them in the darkness. But why would a ghost be afraid of the dark?

title: Buddhism For Today And Tomorrow
summary: In the mid-1970s, The Triratna Buddhist Order was emerging as a bold new Buddhist movement. Sangharakshita set out the movement's offering in four lectures given in the Royal Pavilion in 1976- later published as 'Buddhism for Today and Tomorrow'. The lectures set out the breadth and depth of the Buddha’s teaching, while directly applying it to a modern European life and society. 50 years later, the Triratna Order and community– now an…
_description: In the mid-1970s, The Triratna Buddhist Order was emerging as a bold new Buddhist movement. Sangharakshita set out the movement's offering in four lectures given in the Royal Pavilion in 1976- later published as 'Buddhism for Today and Tomorrow'. The lectures set out the breadth and depth of the Buddha’s teaching, while directly applying it to a modern European life and society. 50 years later, the Triratna Order and community– now an international Buddhist movement– stand as legacy to Sangharakshita, who passed away in 2018 aged 93. Throughout the festival we are joined by four senior members of the Triratna Buddhist Order to revisit the theme of the relevance of Buddhism and its role in the world. The Four Gifts: Wednesday 6th May– ‘A Method of Personal Development’, with Mahamati Wednesday 13th May– ‘A Vision of Human Existence’, with Vishvapani Wednesday 20th May– ‘The Nucleus of a New Society’, with Parami Wednesday 27th May- 'A Blueprint for a New World', with Subhuti

_description: This festival concert is a highlight of the year with Roedean’s talented young musicians performing solos with orchestra. This year the programme features a stunning line up of music and will include movements from Elgar’s Cello Concerto, Sibelius’ Violin Concerto, Glière’s Harp Concerto, Rachmininov’s Piano Concerto No.2, Dvořák’s Song to the Moon, Massenet’s Va! laisse couler mes larmes and Waxman’s Carmen Fantasy. Roedean Choir will be joined by staff and musicians from our wider community to perform four beautiful and atmospheric choral works: Elgar The Snow, Ešenvalds Stars, Grieg Spring and Whitacre Cloudburst. This promises to be a truly uplifting event.

title: Squelch
summary: Would you jump down the toilet to rescue something you loved? Complete opposites and nothing alike - two brothers are stuck in a bitter sibling rivalry. When they lose their goldfish down the toilet, they embark on a bombastic adventure down the drain, meeting strange monsters and menacing creatures... Split Hairs return to Brighton Fringe with their unique and tender story of love, family, and finding your way through the unknown.…
_description: Would you jump down the toilet to rescue something you loved? Complete opposites and nothing alike - two brothers are stuck in a bitter sibling rivalry. When they lose their goldfish down the toilet, they embark on a bombastic adventure down the drain, meeting strange monsters and menacing creatures... Split Hairs return to Brighton Fringe with their unique and tender story of love, family, and finding your way through the unknown. With riotous slapstick comedy, enchanting puppetry, and an exuberant live score - join us for a bizarre and exciting journey where even in the messiest moments we can find hope, joy and kindness. For ages 5-105.

title: Every Single Sound In The World
summary: An alien arrives on Earth with a simple task: collect every single sound on the planet. Unfortunately, humans and their animals, vegetables and minerals make a lot more sounds than she anticipated. Please help her! She can’t go home until she’s collected them all. In this live, reactive show, Natalie Patuzzo uses voice, objects, and audience interaction to construct (and dismantle) a world one sound at a time. Described as an…
_description: An alien arrives on Earth with a simple task: collect every single sound on the planet. Unfortunately, humans and their animals, vegetables and minerals make a lot more sounds than she anticipated. Please help her! She can’t go home until she’s collected them all. In this live, reactive show, Natalie Patuzzo uses voice, objects, and audience interaction to construct (and dismantle) a world one sound at a time. Described as an ‘endearing yet wickedly funny physical performer’, Natalie Patuzzo’s debut solo clown show is chaotic, playful and full of sounds you may have heard before and may never want to hear again.

title: Perfect Little Flirt
summary: "16th March 2003, 11:40pm Note to self: A NEW START! Here I am born again, to live again to try again to start again. The old me is dead and on the stroke of midnight, I will be reborn." Perfect Little Flirt is a chaotic and hilarious ride into Sal’s actual teenage diary — unfiltered, gender confused and deeply earnest. Come for the mega highs and desperate lows, stay for the absurd social commentary that comes with being socialised as…
_description: "16th March 2003, 11:40pm Note to self: A NEW START! Here I am born again, to live again to try again to start again. The old me is dead and on the stroke of midnight, I will be reborn." Perfect Little Flirt is a chaotic and hilarious ride into Sal’s actual teenage diary — unfiltered, gender confused and deeply earnest. Come for the mega highs and desperate lows, stay for the absurd social commentary that comes with being socialised as an early-2000s “good girl.” Expect excrutiatingly dull lists, so much “getting off” and a relatable dose of cringe. With joy and sorrow, the show cracks open the diary as a tool of gendered self-surveillance and asks what it really costs to be the Perfect Little Flirt.

summary: Ben and Chris have discovered the internet and now they can’t stop. Previous events have delved deep into detective fiction, conspiracy theories, power ballads, dystopian sci-fi, weird animals and the eating habits of politicians. This…
_description: Ben and Chris have discovered the internet and now they can’t stop. Join them for a whimsical tour of pop culture’s lesser-known niches as they brain-dump their gratuitous "research" in a series of hilarious PowerPoint lectures, with different bizarro topics each night. Previous events have delved deep into detective fiction, conspiracy theories, power ballads, dystopian sci-fi, weird animals and the eating habits of politicians. This Brighton Fringe special features new talks and a fresh format allowing for even more amusing and intriguing minutiae. Each talk concludes with a Q&A and the opportunity to put your hosts on the spot. Doors at 7pm for a 7.30pm start. Some tickets will be available on the door.

summary: The third Brighton Fringe edition of the popular Black Brighton Pop Up Market bringing you an exciting family-friendly day festival featuring a vibrant array of arts & craft stalls, performances, storytelling, workshops, and a cultural café - something for everyone! Addressing some of the limited opportunities for Black People/People of Colour, the pioneering Black Brighton Market venture creates a unique space for more communities…
_description: The third Brighton Fringe edition of the popular Black Brighton Pop Up Market bringing you an exciting family-friendly day festival featuring a vibrant array of arts & craft stalls, performances, storytelling, workshops, and a cultural café - something for everyone! Addressing some of the limited opportunities for Black People/People of Colour, the pioneering Black Brighton Market venture creates a unique space for more communities have the chance to sell their art, goods and services, and to also showcase taster sessions, workshops and performances to the wider general public in the state-of-the-art Ironworks Studios. More details available on the Black Brighton Market website. All are welcome. THIS EVENT IS SPONSORED BY THE IRENE MENSAH BURSARY & FLO CREATIVE IN COLLABORATION WITH IRONWORKS STUDIOS AND THE ENTANGLED COORDINATES CO-CREATED THEATRE PRODUCTION ‘THIS IS NEW BRIGHTON’

_description: Words by the Water presents: Fringe and Friends After the success of the last Words by the Water and friends, we're back and we're doing it bigger and better. Ella Sadie Guthrie will be bringing her new sequence of poetry focusing on the dreamlike nature of Grief. The incomparable Kate Ireland will be launching her new book 'Adaptive Strategies for a Sensitive System' fresh off her tour supporting Joshua Idehen. Elle Dillon-Reams will be bringing snippets from her show MEAT as well as other soft and beautiful words to woo us with. Gray Taylor will be supporting with some wonderful spoken word All while the beloved illustrator Chris Riddell (Ottoline books, The Observer) will be making their words come to life. Drinks and Merch from all involved will be able to purchase at the Rooftop Bar. Doors at 6:30 for drinks, merch and mingling Show will start at 7:15pm and will include an interval Show will end at 9pm

title: My Neighbor Totoro Ghibli Taste Tales
summary: Step into a world of enchantment at Blossoms Cocktail Bar in the heart of the Lanes for an unforgettable evening of cinema and cuisine. _description: Step into a world of enchantment at Blossoms Cocktail Bar in the heart of the Lanes for an unforgettable evening of cinema and cuisine. Join us for an immersive experience as we bring the magic of Studio Ghibli’s beloved 'My Neighbour Totoro' to life like never before. As you settle in to watch this heart-warming classic, each scene will be paired with dishes inspired by the film's charming rural landscapes and whimsical characters. From fresh, vibrant meals to sweet treats, every bite will transport your taste buds straight into the world of Totoro and Mei, as we serve up food that’s as delightful as the story itself. Whether you're a die-hard Studio Ghibli fan or simply seeking a one-of-a-kind night out, this event promises to be a sensory journey that celebrates both the magic of cinema and the joy of food. Grab your spot for a truly magical evening where film and flavour meet. Seats are limited, so don’t miss your chance to be part of this exclusive experience at Blossoms.

_description: David Hoyle the original performance avalanche, returns to Brighton – an all singing, all raging bona-fide performance legend. In 2024 The Aviva Studios in Manchester held a three-week long retrospective of David’s career (so far) called Please Feel Free to Ignore My Work. ‘The greatest living performer’ – Justin Vivian Bond ‘He is raw, sometimes frightening, but also thrilling in his look-no-hands recklessness’ – The Guardian ‘There is nothing quite like it: bold and unique, electrifying and disarmingly humane’ – Time Out

Smart, silly and seductive: perfect for culture vultures, comedy lovers and history nerds. Fresh from sold-out gallery tours and 30M+ views online, Coco is finally bringing her chaos to Brighton. Come help her make history - misbehave.

_description: Step right up, ladies & gentlemen, boys & girls! Mr Bubbles is back in town with The Bubble Show®! Enter a world of wonder with this internationally acclaimed show, delighting families across theatres and festivals worldwide. Created and performed by Guinness World Record holder The Highland Joker, this theatrical experience blends storytelling, magic, science and bubble artistry into one unforgettable journey. From tiny to giant bubbles, smoke and fire bubbles, a sparkling cube and erupting bubble volcano - every moment bursts with colour, excitement and interactive fun. A joyful, family-friendly celebration that leaves audiences smiling, inspired and spellbound. A Brighton Fringe favourite since 2019, proudly made in Sussex. Other similarly titled shows now exist, but if you want the original award-winning production, be sure to book The Bubble Show® at Wagner Hall. Please also check out our new production Legends of Bubbleland: brightonfringe.org/events/legends-of-bubbleland

title: Jody Kamali This Is My Dad
venue_name: Laughing Horse @ a&o Hostel bar (Front Room)
summary: After his father’s sudden death, Jody digs through his belongings, uncovering the man he thought he knew. Suddenly, he’s back for one hour to tell us who he was. Playing both himself and his father, Jody unpicks his Dad’s bizarre life in a thoughtful, funny theatrical solo show about grief, secrets and the contradictions within us all. A preview for the Edinburgh Fringe from award-winning, Brighton Fringe veteran Jody Kamali.

AI Mozart is a ground-breaking live concert of original…
_description: Broadcast on Al Jazeera News and commissioned by Dame Wendy Hall’s Web Science Institute, SELLOUT EdFringe25 pianist Shiel performs music composed by AI. In 2025, Matthew Shiel and Fringe Classics made history by performing the world’s first piano recital of music composed by artificial intelligence at the Edinburgh Festival and this historic event was broadcast on Al Jazeera. AI Mozart is a ground-breaking live concert of original piano pieces composed by artificial intelligences in an eclectic variety of styles from Beethoven and Brahms to boogie-woogie and Latin salsa to ridiculous hybrid genres including Viking progressive death polka! Step into the future of music and be the first to listen to an AI composer LIVE at the Brighton Fringe! Matthew Shiel is an award-winning pianist who has collaborated with BBC Scottish Symphony Orchestra (broadcast on BBC Radio 3), The Band of His Majesty’s Royal Marine Band Scotland and has been broadcast on Al Jazeera.

Relaxed, inclusive, unforgettable – the ultimate afternoon for primary-age kids and their whole family. The funnest, furriest family gig you’ll find!

Arresting and…
_description: The attic burns with secrets untold! In Polly Teale’s bold reimagining, Bertha Mason - the infamous “madwoman in the attic”- steps from the shadows and emerges as the living embodiment of Jane’s suppressed longings and rage. A daring interpretation that reveals the storm beneath Charlotte Brontë’s classic. Dive into Jane’s inner world and unearth the psychological battles between passion and restraint, duty and desire. Arresting and emotionally charged, this adaptation breathes raw, urgent life into one of literature’s most enduring heroines — a Jane Eyre like no other.

title: Take Shelter
company: Take Shelter - WW2 Air Raid Shelter
summary: Step back in time to explore a unique part of Brighton's wartime history with one of our air raid shelter tours. During the war, every school would have had its own air raid shelter, now one of the only surviving examples in the UK is under the playground at Downs Junior School. There is lots to do and see during your visit to Take Shelter: Tour the shelter with one of our knowledgeable guides (ticketed), play one of our vintage fair…
_description: Step back in time to explore a unique part of Brighton's wartime history with one of our air raid shelter tours. During the war, every school would have had its own air raid shelter, now one of the only surviving examples in the UK is under the playground at Downs Junior School. There is lots to do and see during your visit to Take Shelter: Tour the shelter with one of our knowledgeable guides (ticketed), play one of our vintage fair games, browse our World War II museum, chat with our veterans about their wartime experiences, soak up the vintage sounds and learn wartime dances on our dancefloor, or enjoy a cup of tea and bite to eat from Mrs Button's Tea Room and take home a souvenir from the Take Shelter shop.

title: Chris Ford Jehovahs Witness For The Prosecution
summary: ​Chris Ford, known for his sharp wit and infectious charm, brings his Work in Progress show Jehovah’s Witness for the Prosecution to Brighton Fringe 2026. In this forty-minute set, with an opener from the London queer comedy circuit, Chris blends stories of growing up as a strict Jehovah’s Witness with life as an A&E nurse. The result is an honest, heartfelt, and hilarious exploration of identity, belief, and resilience. Through sharp…
_description: ​Chris Ford, known for his sharp wit and infectious charm, brings his Work in Progress show Jehovah’s Witness for the Prosecution to Brighton Fringe 2026. In this forty-minute set, with an opener from the London queer comedy circuit, Chris blends stories of growing up as a strict Jehovah’s Witness with life as an A&E nurse. The result is an honest, heartfelt, and hilarious exploration of identity, belief, and resilience. Through sharp storytelling and self-deprecating humour, Chris reflects on his teenage years navigating faith, shame, and desire within a world where conformity was demanded and questioning was forbidden. From secret moments of queer awakening to the absurdities of Kingdom Hall life, he delivers comedy that is as emotionally raw as it is laugh-out-loud funny. Jehovah’s Witness for the Prosecution marks a bold and deeply personal step from a promising queer comedian with a story that deserves to be heard, and laughed with.
