title: I Want To Speak To Your Manager How I Was Radicalised And Became Karen
summary: You’ve heard this woman in a café, complaining her extra-hot-skinny latté isn’t hot enough. You’ve seen her in a restaurant, demanding dishes that aren’t on the menu. You’ve met her in the ALDI car park, where her SUV takes up multiple spaces: the dreaded Karen. But could you – down-to-earth, humble, people-pleasing – become her? Holly did. And she’s ready to speak her truth. This debut one-woman-show from internationally-acclaimed…
_description: You’ve heard this woman in a café, complaining her extra-hot-skinny latté isn’t hot enough. You’ve seen her in a restaurant, demanding dishes that aren’t on the menu. You’ve met her in the ALDI car park, where her SUV takes up multiple spaces: the dreaded Karen. But could you – down-to-earth, humble, people-pleasing – become her? Holly did. And she’s ready to speak her truth. This debut one-woman-show from internationally-acclaimed slam poet and accidental keyboard warrior Holly Hughes is a bold, hilarious and brutally honest interrogation of who gets to be angry in today’s society. After all, what is so wrong with holding people accountable? When broligarch losers are destroying the world, is being a Karen REALLY that bad? Join the most hated woman on Google reviews to celebrate society's unsung heroes (serial complainers). After a sell-out run at Dublin Fringe Festival, Karen is ready for world domination. This is more than comedy. This is a movement. One complaint at a time.

It's a unique show brough by two people who look the same but couldn't be more different

title: Up Your Vision
summary: This year, Eurovision carries on as usual - and while the UK stays in, Brighton knows better! Despite its ongoing ethnic cleansing of Palestinians, Israel remains in the competition whilst Russia has been excluded since 2022 for its invasion of Ukraine. The double standard is clear. And we’re not the only ones calling it out: Iceland, Ireland, Norway, Spain, Slovenia, and the Netherlands have withdrawn from Eurovision this year in…
_description: This year, Eurovision carries on as usual - and while the UK stays in, Brighton knows better! Despite its ongoing ethnic cleansing of Palestinians, Israel remains in the competition whilst Russia has been excluded since 2022 for its invasion of Ukraine. The double standard is clear. And we’re not the only ones calling it out: Iceland, Ireland, Norway, Spain, Slovenia, and the Netherlands have withdrawn from Eurovision this year in protest. So we’re doing something different: Up Your Vision! is our joyful, defiant alternative. Expect a raucous evening of live music and comedy chaos, with a fancy dress glam station, prizes, and a secret after party from the awesome Queer House Party DJ’s. Hosted by Brighton’s own brilliant Jen Brister and featuring live music from Palestinian pop royalty Bashar Murad, Oceana, The Liberation Choir, waymore, The Unkempt Skiffle Band, Go For It Tribe, Merlin Shepherd, Nil Points Doors open 5:30 pm. Show will start promptly at 6 pm.

title: I Could Run This Country
summary: New for Brighton Fringe 2026, join our host Charlotte Madden and a panel of hilarious comedians for a chaotic hour of political decision making. Ever thought you could run the country better than our government? Well join us to find out. This is a high energy, chaotic hour where the audience gets involved in solving political situations so unbelievable they might be real. Come join the (political) party.

title: The Dance Of Time A Black Comedy About Life And Death
summary: In this black comedy there are shouts of joy, cries of despair and a roller coaster of emotions as Tom and Mary struggle to come to terms with an uncertain future, with their complicated relationship, and with the meaning of life. Whatever that is of course. In hilarious exchanges, they launch into a thought-provoking dialogue about some of the broader questions of life and death while at the same time managing to keep us all amused.…
_description: In this black comedy there are shouts of joy, cries of despair and a roller coaster of emotions as Tom and Mary struggle to come to terms with an uncertain future, with their complicated relationship, and with the meaning of life. Whatever that is of course. In hilarious exchanges, they launch into a thought-provoking dialogue about some of the broader questions of life and death while at the same time managing to keep us all amused. Throw in a little music, some shenanigans, a love story, bravery in the face of impending tragedy, some wry comments on modern society, a little dancing, some rather surprising moments, and you have a play that not only has messages about life that you did not know you needed - but is also a play that is also guaranteed to make you laugh. Only a few performances at the Fringe before going on tour. With two top class actors, this play, written by Tim Coakley and staged by renowned director Petina Hapgood, is a play you simply have to see.

Fasted takes us on a journey from a ballet school in Northern England to an austere fasting centre in Soviet Russia, overseen by the charismatic yet flawed Ivanov. Our protagonist, the ballerina Nina Glover, goes in search of a solution to her dysfunctional self-perception. Her search is hindered by the unwanted attention of an…
_description: What do you see when you look in the mirror? A true likeness or a body fat with shame and self-loathing? Fasted takes us on a journey from a ballet school in Northern England to an austere fasting centre in Soviet Russia, overseen by the charismatic yet flawed Ivanov. Our protagonist, the ballerina Nina Glover, goes in search of a solution to her dysfunctional self-perception. Her search is hindered by the unwanted attention of an admirer, Conrad Salt, a fellow ballet student, who follows her, believing his romantic intensity will win her heart.

_description: In 1993, Spanish pop duo Los del Rio released their most famous song to moderate success. In 1996, a remix of this song released by the the Bayside Boys took the whole world by storm - all thanks to a set of simple, easy to follow, dance moves. In 2014, one man decided to do that dance longer than anyone ever had before. Today, he still has not stopped doing that dance. The man is Connor Felix, the dance is the macarena and this is their story. Starring comedian and writer, Alex Richmond. This show is a work in progress.

title: Blip Barp An Alien Love Story
summary: Meet Blip Blarp — an ambitious alien with a mission: conquer Earth and produce 1,000 offspring. There’s just one problem. Dating humans is... complicated. As Blip Blarp navigates Earth’s baffling mating rituals — from awkward flirting to modern dating — she discovers that seduction is harder than planetary takeover. Blending comedy, burlesque, and extra-terrestrial absurdity, the show skewers swipe culture, romantic expectations, and…
_description: Meet Blip Blarp — an ambitious alien with a mission: conquer Earth and produce 1,000 offspring. There’s just one problem. Dating humans is... complicated. As Blip Blarp navigates Earth’s baffling mating rituals — from awkward flirting to modern dating — she discovers that seduction is harder than planetary takeover. Blending comedy, burlesque, and extra-terrestrial absurdity, the show skewers swipe culture, romantic expectations, and the universal longing to be desired. Audiences can expect an hour of playful provocation and cosmic camp wrapped in intergalactic glamour. Equal parts sexy, smart, and silly, this is a romantic comedy from another planet — one that proves love, lust, and loneliness are truly universal languages.
